nodes.title: Mutation and property-test quality gates
nodes.spec:

Deliverables:

- proptest-based Rust property tests in crates/kaifuu-core for patch compatibility and protected-span preservation
- Documented threshold constants in packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/mutation-thresholds.ts and crates/kaifuu-core/src/contracts.rs
- Local quality recipes `pnpm exec vitest run packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/schema.test.ts` and `cargo test -p kaifuu-core property`
nodes.acceptance:
- Selected schema, delta, and protected-span logic has mutation or property coverage
- The Vitest mutation-survivor guard rejects at least one committed invalid fixture per selected invariant
- Rust property tests run with fixed public seeds and bounded case counts documented in the test module
- Mutation/property failures produce actionable diagnostics
audit_focus: Slow CI
Meaningless mutation thresholds
Untested critical invariants

nodes.title: Harden exact upgrade recipe verification
nodes.spec:

Deliverables:

- Verifier diagnostics that name ignored-error prefixes when exact upgrade commands are checked
nodes.acceptance:
- The verifier rejects or explicitly flags exact upgrade commands that use just ignored-error prefixes
- A fixture containing -corepack use pnpm@latest fails verification instead of passing after prefix stripping
- The current canonical just upgrade recipe remains accepted without ignored-error prefixes
audit_focus: Ignored command failures
False-positive upgrade verification
Just recipe prefix handling

nodes.title: Self-contained DB test and scale smoke recipe
nodes.spec:
Add alpha-required local and CI-friendly database test recipes using the existing Itotori DB package and scale harness, with deterministic skip artifacts when a database URL is unavailable.

Deliverables:
- `just db-up`, `just db-wait`, `just db-migrate`, `just db-reset`, `just ci-itotori`, and `just itotori-scale-smoke` recipe contract
- `DATABASE_URL` and `ITOTORI_SCALE_SCHEMA` environment-variable documentation for local and CI runs
- Generated smoke summary at `.tmp/itotori-scale-harness/smoke/summary.json` from `scripts/itotori-scale-harness.mjs --profile smoke`
- No-database skip report at `.tmp/itotori-db/no-database-skipped.json` with status, reason, command, checkedEnv, and timestamp fields
- Contributor docs naming the disposable Postgres path and the exact commands that feed alpha readiness
nodes.acceptance:
- `just db-up && just db-wait && just db-migrate && just db-reset` provisions or resets an isolated Postgres database using `DATABASE_URL` without depending on a developer's existing schema
- When `DATABASE_URL` is unset for the DB-only verification path, the workflow exits successfully, prints `itotori db tests skipped: DATABASE_URL unset`, and writes `.tmp/itotori-db/no-database-skipped.json`
- `pnpm --filter @itotori/db test` and `pnpm --filter @itotori/app test` run under `just ci-itotori` against the documented disposable database
- `just itotori-scale-smoke` runs `node scripts/itotori-scale-harness.mjs --profile smoke` and produces `.tmp/itotori-scale-harness/smoke/summary.json` with profile, outputPath, budgetPassed, unitCount, batchCount, scheduledJobCount, and schemaKept
- Docs name `DATABASE_URL`, `ITOTORI_SCALE_SCHEMA`, `COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME`, and the no-secret public CI defaults
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: moved from alpha to real-game-testing-ready — the old "alpha" framing was renamed; substance unchanged. See docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: DB tests that depend on hidden local state
Unset DATABASE_URL producing noisy failures
Required scale smoke docs without a runnable CI-safe target
Shared tooling that leaks implementation-specific setup

nodes.title: Roadmap verification semantics and placeholder validator
nodes.spec:
Validator detects runnable verification, concrete deliverables, non-placeholder acceptance, and integration nodes naming exact surfaces.

Deliverables:
- Verification semantics rule
- Generic placeholder detector
- Roadmap meta-node and planning-only detector
- Integration-node validator
- Regression tests
- Validator docs update
nodes.acceptance:
- Every non-docs-only planned implementation node has at least one runnable command verification entry
- Manual review, manual smoke, and fixture review entries are rejected when they are the only evidence for tests or smoke behavior
- Generic deliverables such as implementation, fixtures, tests, regression coverage, and end-to-end fixture are rejected unless they name concrete surfaces or artifacts
- Placeholder acceptance such as has concrete executable behavior or schema validation is rejected with the offending field name
- Meta follow-up packs, decision nodes, feasibility nodes, and normalize-later nodes are rejected unless their status is cancelled
- Integration nodes must name the exact composed surfaces, artifacts, commands, or adapters they wire together
- Validator errors identify the node id, field, and offending value that needs repair
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: moved from alpha to real-game-testing-ready — the old "alpha" framing was renamed; substance unchanged. See docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.

Meta follow-up packs left planned

Split CatSystem2 parser/readiness proof into its own fixture-backed unit with concrete script, string-table, encoding, and patch-back constraints.

Deliverables:
- CatSystem2 synthetic or public fixture
- String-table parser proof
- Encoding and patch-back constraint tests
- Unsupported variant diagnostics
nodes.acceptance:
- CatSystem2 fixture provenance is documented and contains no private retail bytes
- String-table, encoding, and patch-back constraints are captured as executable tests or fixture metadata
- Unsupported or unknown variants fail semantically before patch writes
- Later CatSystem2 adapter work can depend on this proof without redoing format discovery
audit_focus: CatSystem2 proof ending as notes only
Unsafe fixture provenance
Patch-back constraints omitted

nodes.title: BGI parser-to-patcher proof slice
nodes.spec:
Add the concrete BGI bytecode/string-reference extraction-to-patching proof path that sits between detector readiness and a broader production adapter.

Deliverables:

- False-positive string references and Shift-JIS edge cases have regression fixtures
- Encrypted/compressed or unknown variants fail semantically before patch writes
- Adapter support claims depend on this proof rather than detector readiness alone

nodes.title: KiriKiri XP3 profile proof command
nodes.spec:
Implement an XP3 profile proof command scoped to plain XP3 as the claimed-support concern: classifies plain XP3 (claimed: detect, extract, patch-back), and additionally distinguishes encrypted XP3, helper-required XP3, and unsupported protected-executable cases for routing diagnostics only with no encrypted patch-back claim.

Deliverables:
- `kaifuu xp3 profile-proof --fixture fixtures/kaifuu/kirikiri/xp3-profile.json` command contract
- XP3 profile schema examples for plain, encrypted, helper-required, and unsupported protected executable profiles
- Capability fixture report with archive id, crypt profile id, keyRef requirement, and patch capability level
- Negative fixtures for missing crypt profile, unknown encryption plugin, and leaked archive paths
nodes.acceptance:
- Plain XP3, encrypted XP3, helper-required XP3, and protected executable cases produce distinct capability outcomes.
- The proof report includes fixture id, profile id, archive hash, crypt profile status, helper requirement, and semantic remediation.
- Private archive paths, raw keys, and decrypted text cannot appear in the report.
- Unsupported cases fail before extract or patch claims are made.

nodes.title: MV/MZ encrypted media readiness command
nodes.spec:
Implement a fixture command that detects RPG Maker MV/MZ encrypted media, validates key profile requirements, and reports readiness without implying script encryption support.

Deliverables:
- `kaifuu rpgmaker encrypted-media-proof --fixture fixtures/kaifuu/rpgmaker/encrypted-media.json` command contract
- Fixture matrix for `.rpgmvp`, `.rpgmvm`, `.rpgmvo`, missing key, wrong key, and plaintext assets
- Readiness report with media asset kind, keyRef status, decryptability, and patch capability
- Negative tests for raw key leakage and media/script capability conflation
nodes.acceptance:
- Encrypted image, audio, and video media variants are detected with exact asset-kind capability levels.
- Missing or wrong keys return semantic diagnostics before decrypted bytes are persisted.
- Readiness output never claims dialogue extraction or script patch support based only on media-key detection.
- Public fixtures use synthetic media and public test keys only.

nodes.title: Local key resolver and secret store
nodes.spec:
Implement the local-only key material resolver that turns validated key-profile secret refs into adapter-consumable bytes without persisting or logging raw secrets.

Deliverables:
- SecretRef parser and enum-backed scheme model
- Ignored local-secret store adapter
- Prompt and external-secret resolver interfaces
- Resolved-key validation proof records
- Negative tests for raw-key, path, account, and high-entropy leakage
nodes.acceptance:
- Profiles may reference local-secret, os-keychain, secret-manager, or prompt refs, but committed profiles and reports never contain raw key bytes
- The resolver returns typed key material only inside the extraction/helper process boundary and zeroizes or drops it after use where practical
- Validation proofs record method, proof hash, byte length, helper/tool version, and readiness status without decrypted private strings
- Missing, malformed, unavailable, cancelled, or validation-failed secret refs produce stable semantic errors
- Public tests use fixture-only keys and prove logs, reports, CLI errors, and debug output stay redacted
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: moved from alpha to real-game-testing-ready — the old "alpha" framing was renamed; substance unchanged. See docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Raw secret leakage
Machine-specific secret refs in committed artifacts
Adapters bypassing the resolver

nodes.title: Synthetic encrypted fixture suite

nodes.acceptance:
- Every generated fixture is redistributable, deterministic, small, and visibly marked as fixture-only
- Fixtures cover KiriKiri/XP3-like, Siglus-like, RPG Maker MV/MZ asset-like, Wolf-like, BGI/container-like, and unknown archive signals without copying retail bytes
- Public CI proves missing-key, bad-key, helper-unavailable, protected-executable, redaction, and detector aggregate-output behavior
- Fixture keys are safe to commit because the encrypted payloads and generation scripts are also public-safe
- Private-local corpus validation remains an additional local workflow, not a hidden CI dependency
- Helper-result fixtures conform to the KAIFUU-085 helper result schema.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: moved from alpha to real-game-testing-ready — the old "alpha" framing was renamed; substance unchanged. See docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.

nodes.title: Layered text access pipeline contract
nodes.spec:
Recast engine access as reversible per-surface transforms: locate surface, unpack container, decrypt, decode/decompile, normalize text, patch back, with plaintext represented as identity layers.

Deliverables:
- Layered access profile model
- Container, crypto, codec, and surface transform enums
- Capability-level contract for identify, inventory, extract, and patch
- Identity/null-key fixtures for plaintext engines
nodes.acceptance:
- GameProfile and adapter capability output can represent container/archive, encryption/obfuscation, codec/decompile, and patch-back stages per text-bearing surface
- Plaintext engines are modeled as identity container, null-key crypto, and identity codec rather than a separate architecture
- Per-surface differences are representable, such as MV/MZ JSON text with encrypted media assets
- Adapters fail before writes when any required transform stage is unsupported, missing key material, or helper-gated
- Existing fixture adapter remains an identity-layer configuration with no hello-world regression
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: moved from alpha to real-game-testing-ready — the old "alpha" framing was renamed; substance unchanged. See docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Plaintext and encrypted engines diverging into separate architectures
Per-surface transform loss
False patch capability claims

nodes.title: KiriKiri XP3 capability profile generator and validator
nodes.spec:
Generate and validate KiriKiri XP3 capability profiles from detector, key-helper, crypt-profile, and archive fixture evidence instead of hand-authored readiness prose. Capability profiles may describe encrypted XP3 variants for routing diagnostics, but only plain XP3 can enter claimed patch-back support; encrypted-variant entries are advertised as research-tier only.

Deliverables:
- `kaifuu xp3 capability-profile --fixture fixtures/kaifuu/kirikiri/xp3-profile.json` command contract
- Capability profile schema examples for plaintext .ks, plain XP3, encrypted XP3, helper-required XP3, protected executable, and universal dump workflows
- Validator tests for detector evidence, helper requirement, keyRef state, archive hash, and patch capability tuple
- Redaction tests for keys, helper dumps, decrypted text, private archive names, and local paths
nodes.acceptance:
- Every XP3 capability profile records source node id, fixture id, engine variant, archive profile, key/helper requirement, capability tuple, validation command, and redaction status.
- Profiles cannot claim extract or patch support without matching detector, archive, key, and patch evidence.
- Plaintext .ks is marked as the null-container special case, not the commercial KiriKiri baseline.
- Private-local XP3 triage can produce aggregate readiness evidence without publishing keys, assets, helper dumps, paths, or source text.

nodes.title: Asset OCR and text-region extraction prototype

nodes.acceptance:
- The public fixture command produces schema-valid text regions with provenance and stable content hashes
- Uncertain OCR is represented as findings with source asset references

Unsupported image formats

nodes.title: Target span mapping edge cases

nodes.title: First real-engine end-to-end vertical slice
nodes.spec:
Run the full Itotori/Kaifuu/Utsushi loop on a public or private-local RPG Maker MV/MZ full-surface fixture after inventory/readiness metadata is available, without relying on the synthetic fixture engine.

Deliverables:
- Real-engine fixture profile
- Full run command
- Dashboard evidence
- Patch package
nodes.acceptance:
- Inventory, readiness, extract, draft, QA, patch, delta apply, runtime evidence, feedback, and rerun all work for the MV/MZ fixture profile
- No raw copyrighted assets are committed
- All failures are structured findings
- The run reports catalog/local-corpus/readiness identity when available instead of treating bridge import as the first project fact

nodes.title: Playable draft feedback loop
nodes.spec:

Deliverables:
- Feedback UX
- Triage wiring
- Repair rerun
- Before/after dashboard evidence
nodes.acceptance:
- A typo correction can be submitted with context and batched
- Style disputes can become decision queue items
- Repair jobs update only affected work where possible

nodes.title: Alpha readiness cost and quality benchmark run
nodes.spec:
Run the alpha readiness benchmark comparing raw MTL, Itotori drafts, deterministic QA, LLM QA, cost, provider route evidence, and runtime evidence on public fixtures with optional private-local aggregate inputs.

Deliverables:
- Benchmark run artifacts
- Cost report artifact
- Quality report artifact
- README-safe summary renderer
nodes.acceptance:
- Reports avoid unverifiable public claims
- MTL baseline is included
- Cost accounting includes model/provider metadata and ledger-derived token/cost estimates
- Public-fixture benchmark decides alpha pass/fail; private-local aggregates are supplementary evidence and never gate the alpha claim or substitute for a missing public result.
- The benchmark command can run without private-local corpora by using public fixtures
- Readiness output links to benchmark seed selection, provider proof, and quality report artifacts

nodes.title: Alpha engine capability matrix generator
nodes.spec:
Implement a generated engine capability matrix that derives breadth/readiness evidence from adapter registries, readiness profiles, and fixture runs instead of hand-written claims.

Deliverables:
- Engine capability matrix schema
- Matrix generator command or script
- MV/MZ full-surface run artifact inputs
- TyranoScript null-key artifact inputs
- Packed-engine readiness profile inputs
- BGI detector/profile readiness artifact inputs
- Known-limitation renderer
nodes.acceptance:
- The matrix is generated from adapter registry, fixture output, readiness profile, claimed-support tuples, and validation artifact inputs
- Positive extraction/patch adapters and readiness-only packed/encrypted profiles are mechanically distinguished
- RenPy is not presented as an alpha Japanese opportunity driver
- KiriKiri breadth is represented by XP3/readiness evidence rather than plaintext-only support
- BGI is represented as detector/profile readiness evidence without parser or patch support claims
- Capability levels distinguish identify, inventory, extract, patch, helper, and runtime support for each engine family
- MV/MZ encrypted media, Siglus known-key Scene/Gameexe, KiriKiri XP3 crypt smoke, and Wolf encrypted archive smoke appear as separate capability rows
- Stale or manually edited capability entries fail validation

nodes.title: Alpha localization project readiness hardening
nodes.spec:
Prepare the suite to start a first real localization project with executable install, docs, security, limitation, example, and readiness checks.

Deliverables:
- Alpha readiness README
- Install docs
- Security and limitations docs
- Alpha readiness checklist command or checked artifact
- Fresh-clone public fixture demo command coverage
nodes.acceptance:
- A new user can run public fixture demos from a fresh clone
- Known legal/safety boundaries are practical and not overstated
- CI, alpha proof workflow, benchmark smoke, and recorded or opted-in real-provider proof are required and green
- Docs frame the product as catalog, inventory, readiness, extraction, localization, patching, and validation rather than only translation
- The milestone is framed as readiness to start a real localization project, not as a terminal product release
- Readiness docs are checked against generated capability and benchmark artifacts rather than hand-maintained claims
- The final alpha readiness checklist validates references to KAIFUU-042, ALPHA-006, ALPHA-007, ALPHA-008, ITOTORI-116, ITOTORI-117, UTSUSHI-119, SHARED-025, UNIV-013, SHARED-013, SHARED-014, and UNIV-021, and confirms UTSUSHI-119 consumed PatchResult plus SHARED-025 manifest ids for patched-output runtime proof.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: kept alpha under the new 4-tier framework (single-game Sweetie HD e2e set per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md). | Re-tier 2026-06-24: rewired dependsOn to drop demoted-to-beta engine deps and add the new alpha set per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md §6.
audit_focus: Papering over known limitations
Install instructions untested
Milestone treated as a product endpoint

nodes.title: First real-engine end-to-end alpha vertical (Sukara/Oshioki Sweetie HD)
nodes.spec:
Prove Kaifuu+Utsushi can run the full localization chain end-to-end on Sukara's *Oshioki Sweetie HD Remaster + Sweets fandisc* (RealLive engine), sourcing game bytes from the vault-curation catalog at /archive/vault/, through detect -> extract -> key resolution if required by the engine -> decompile -> patch -> verify -> delta-apply -> Utsushi render-validate. No shell-outs to existing tools (GARbro/SiglusExtract/UberWolf/etc.). If dynamic key discovery proves necessary for this title, the bounded helper boundary in KAIFUU-064 is used; if static parsing suffices, no helper infrastructure is invoked.

Deliverables:
- Full real-engine vertical command for the Sukara/Oshioki Sweetie HD + Sweets fandisc title
- Vault-sourced run evidence bundle (read-only access via the itotori vault-source adapter)
- Render-validate redacted public screenshots usable as feedback evidence
- End-to-end run report with detect, extract, key-resolution (if any), decompile, patch, verify, delta-apply, and Utsushi render-validate stage artifacts
- Out-of-profile semantic diagnostics surface
nodes.acceptance:
- The full chain succeeds end-to-end on the Sukara/Oshioki Sweetie HD Remaster + Sweets fandisc title using only Kaifuu+Utsushi (no shell-outs to GARbro/SiglusExtract/UberWolf/rlvm/siglus_static_key_tool or any other existing tool)
- Game bytes are sourced exclusively through the itotori vault-source adapter (KAIFUU-176) against /archive/vault/; the vault is read-only for the duration of the run
- If dynamic key discovery proves necessary for this title, the bounded helper boundary in KAIFUU-064 is used; if static parsing suffices, no helper infrastructure is invoked
- Utsushi render-validate produces redacted public screenshots usable as feedback evidence (no private paths, no key material, no untranslated decrypted text dumps)
- Out-of-profile failures emit semantic diagnostics rather than silent skips, and the run report distinguishes in-profile pass, in-profile bug, and out-of-profile diagnostic outcomes
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: kept alpha under the new 4-tier framework (single-game Sweetie HD e2e set per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md). | Re-tier 2026-06-24: pruned 7 dependsOn edge(s) that crossed the new tier boundary [KAIFUU-042(beta), KAIFUU-060(beta), KAIFUU-067(beta), KAIFUU-071(beta), KAIFUU-105(beta), KAIFUU-106(beta), KAIFUU-107(beta)]; per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md §6.

verification.value: Sukara/Oshioki Sweetie HD vault-sourced end-to-end vertical run review

audit_focus: Shell-out to existing tools (GARbro/SiglusExtract/UberWolf/rlvm/siglus_static_key_tool) hidden as a fallback path
Vault mutation or write access during a vertical run
Render-validate screenshots leaking private paths, keys, or untranslated decrypted text
Out-of-profile failures masquerading as silent skips or as in-profile bugs
Helper boundary invoked when static parsing would have sufficed for this title
Vertical claiming generality beyond the Sukara/Oshioki Sweetie HD + Sweets fandisc title

nodes.title: Bridge import source-unit retention
nodes.spec:
Replace hard-delete behavior for source units and assets removed during bridge reimport with tombstone, archive, or validity-window semantics so locale branch and runtime evidence history is preserved.

Deliverables:
- Source-unit retention model
- Asset retention model
- Repository reimport update
- Dashboard/runtime query filtering updates
- Reimport history regression tests
nodes.acceptance:
- Reimporting a bundle that omits prior source units or assets no longer cascades away locale branch unit rows, runtime evidence refs, or historical findings
- Current dashboard and export queries ignore inactive units/assets unless explicitly requesting history
- Retention semantics preserve enough provenance to explain which source revision removed or superseded a unit
audit_focus: Historical runtime evidence lost on reimport
Inactive units leaking into current exports
Retention model breaking source revision diffs

nodes.title: Retire literal Hello World CI into alpha proof workflow
nodes.spec:
Replace the permanent literal Hello World workflow with a durable alpha proof workflow that preserves end-to-end integration evidence while removing toy branding, stale scaffold assumptions, and placeholder success-string assertions.

Deliverables:
- .github/workflows/alpha-proof.yml and root `just alpha-proof` recipe for the public fixture alpha proof vertical
- Removal or documented alias policy for the old hello recipe and GitHub Hello World workflow name after the alpha proof workflow is green
- Documentation update explaining that integration proof is preserved through alpha proof artifacts rather than literal hello-world tests
- Regression tests or fixture assertions proving the alpha proof checks schema-valid artifact linkage instead of only a success string
- CI status documentation naming alpha proof as the required integration guardrail and forbidding a separate stale Hello World source of truth
nodes.acceptance:
- The primary CI integration workflow is named and documented as a suite alpha proof or public fixture vertical, not Hello World
- `just alpha-proof` runs `pnpm exec vp run alpha:public-fixture` or a strict wrapper around it, and fails unless bridge, patch export, PatchResult, provider proof, benchmark report, runtime observation, dashboard/read-model ingestion, and SHARED-025 manifest records share the same public fixture id, source revision, locale branch, and content hashes.
- The replacement workflow runs the same or stronger e2e contract as ALPHA-007, including Itotori, Kaifuu, Utsushi, SHARED-025 artifacts, and semantic diagnostics
- CI no longer passes solely because `status=hello_world_passed` prints; it validates bridge, patch, provider proof, benchmark, runtime observation, dashboard/read-model ingestion, and SHARED-025 manifest linkage for the same public fixture id and source revision.
- The old Hello World workflow is removed or renamed into the alpha proof workflow; if `just hello` remains, it is only a compatibility alias that invokes `just alpha-proof` and cannot diverge.
- .github/workflows/hello.yml is removed or changed to a compatibility alias that invokes `just alpha-proof`; there is no independent `status=hello_world_passed` CI gate.
- The alpha proof workflow remains public-fixture-only, deterministic, and free of private corpora, raw provider payloads, live credentials, or private local paths.
- README, docs/hello-world.md or its successor, docs/ci-cache-and-affected.md, and roadmap baseline language no longer imply literal hello-world tests are the long-term proof
- GitHub Actions remain green after the workflow rename or replacement

Deliverables:
- Jump-target smoke route
- Known target fixture
- Runtime evidence link assertion
- Browser test
nodes.acceptance:
- A public fixture can jump to one declared target from a playback session
- The smoke records target id, manifest id, trace id, and evidence artifact link
- Missing or unsupported targets fail with semantic diagnostics
- The smoke does not implement annotations or redaction fixtures
audit_focus: Scope creep from split runtime/browser work
Fixture-only validation drift
Unsupported capability hidden

nodes.title: Browser annotation artifact roundtrip
nodes.spec:
Roundtrip browser review annotations through managed artifact storage and feedback import boundaries.

Deliverables:
- Annotation artifact schema
- Browser annotation save/load flow
- Feedback import handoff fixture
- Roundtrip tests
nodes.acceptance:
- Annotations serialize under the managed artifact root with manifest id, source unit id, trace id, reviewer note, and redaction status
- Reloading the browser fixture restores annotations without losing artifact links
- Feedback import handoff uses UTSUSHI-066 boundaries instead of direct database writes
- Private paths and screenshots are not embedded in annotation JSON
audit_focus: Scope creep from split runtime/browser work
Fixture-only validation drift
Unsupported capability hidden

nodes.title: Browser playback redaction and security fixture
nodes.spec:
Validate browser playback artifact redaction, path confinement, and unsupported asset handling.

Deliverables:
- Browser redaction fixture
- Artifact path confinement tests
- Private field rejection tests
- Unsupported asset diagnostics
nodes.acceptance:
- Browser artifacts reject private absolute paths, raw story text outside allowed fields, screenshots marked private, and unmanaged output paths
- Unsupported local asset access produces deterministic diagnostics
- The fixture proves artifact root confinement for playback and manifest assets
- Security tests run without private local corpora
audit_focus: Scope creep from split runtime/browser work
Fixture-only validation drift
Unsupported capability hidden

nodes.title: MV/MZ switch and variable command pack
nodes.spec:
Add MV/MZ switch and variable event-command replay fixtures with snapshot expectations.

Deliverables:
- Switch command fixture
- Variable command fixture
- Snapshot expectation updates
- Unsupported operation diagnostics
nodes.acceptance:
- Switch and variable commands update declared snapshot fields in golden fixtures
- Unsupported operators or variable scopes fail conformance predictably
- Message and choice behavior from UTSUSHI-033 remains unchanged
- The pack declares exact command ids and state fields covered
audit_focus: Scope creep from split runtime/browser work
Fixture-only validation drift
Unsupported capability hidden

nodes.title: MV/MZ transfer and map command pack
nodes.spec:
Add MV/MZ transfer and map event-command replay fixtures with trace expectations.

Deliverables:
- Transfer command fixture
- Map transition fixture
- Trace expectation updates
- Unsupported map diagnostic tests
nodes.acceptance:
- Transfer commands emit declared map/coordinate trace events for public fixtures
- Unsupported transfer modes and missing maps fail conformance predictably
- The pack does not implement switch, variable, or browser jump behavior
- Trace output links to source event ids and fixture map ids
audit_focus: Scope creep from split runtime/browser work
Fixture-only validation drift
Unsupported capability hidden

nodes.title: Wolf database-state replay profile
nodes.spec:
Add Wolf database-state replay fixtures and snapshot conformance as a separate profile from common events.

Deliverables:
- Wolf database-state fixture
- Database snapshot expectations
- Unsupported DB structure diagnostics
- Profile conformance tests
nodes.acceptance:

- Unsupported database structures fail conformance predictably
