nodes.title: Scene header parser (0x1d0-byte typed decoder)
nodes.spec:
Decode the 0x1d0-byte scene header documented by RLDEV / rlvm scenario.cc into a typed `SceneHeader { compiler_version, kidoku_offset, kidoku_count, dramatis_offset, dramatis_count, bytecode_offset, bytecode_uncompressed_size, bytecode_compressed_size, entrypoint_table, savepoint_message, savepoint_selcom, savepoint_seentop, z_minus_one, z_minus_two }`. Verifies against Sweetie HD scene-blob bytes 0x13880..0x13a50.

Deliverables:
- Typed `SceneHeader` struct with all documented fields decoded u32 LE
- Sweetie HD scene #0001 round-trip pinning header field values
- Out-of-profile compiler-version Warning (`utsushi.reallive.unknown_compiler_version`) without halting
- Field-by-field provenance citation against rlvm `scenario.cc` constructor (P) or Sweetie HD bytes (V)
nodes.acceptance:
- For Sweetie HD scene #0001 (file offset 0x13880, scene-blob offset 0): `compiler_version=110002`, `kidoku_offset=464`, `kidoku_count=1`, `bytecode_offset=468`, `bytecode_uncompressed_size=1660`, `bytecode_compressed_size=1062`, entrypoint_table starts at 0x34 with the `0x06` lattice. (Documented in `docs/research/reallive-engine.md` § D.)
- Header fields are all u32 LE.
- Out-of-profile compiler-version values (anything not in {10002, 110002, 1110002}) emit `utsushi.reallive.unknown_compiler_version` Warning and the header still parses.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit 6142a4d). SceneHeader::parse + encode in utsushi-reallive; Sweetie HD scene 1 verified.
audit_focus: Any field whose offset can't be cited from rlvm's `scenario.cc` header constructor (P) or the Sweetie HD bytes (V) — speculative fields are out

nodes.title: AVG32 LZ + XOR scene decompressor
nodes.spec:
Implement the AVG32 byte-by-byte XOR (256-byte mask) plus the LZSS sliding-window decompressor that turns a scene's `bytecode_compressed_size` bytes into `bytecode_uncompressed_size` bytes. Also implement the second-level XOR pass for compiler-version `110002`. Verifies against Sweetie HD scene-1 compressed payload at byte 0x13a54..0x13e7a.

Deliverables:
- AVG32 256-byte XOR mask (re-derived from RLDEV public docs, attributed in source)
- LZSS sliding-window decompressor (4096-byte window, max-length runs)
- Second-level XOR pass plumbing for compiler-version 110002 (`xor_2_key = None` shipped if unknown)
- Synthetic round-trip suite covering 8 stream shapes (literals, back-references, max-distance, max-length, mixed)
- `utsushi.reallive.xor2_key_unknown` Warning when key absent — never silent
nodes.acceptance:
- Decompressing Sweetie HD scene #0001's 1062 compressed bytes (file offset 0x13a54..0x13e7a) produces exactly 1660 uncompressed bytes.
- The first byte of the uncompressed stream is in the documented BytecodeElement opener set `{0x00, 0x0a, 0x21, 0x23, 0x24, 0x2c, 0x40}` or a printable Shift-JIS lead byte (`0x81`-`0x9F` / `0xE0`-`0xFC`). If it is not, the test fails (an immediate 'XOR-2 key is wrong' canary).
- A round-trip suite recompresses + decompresses 8 synthetic streams covering pure literals, pure back-references, 1-byte distance, max-distance (4096-byte window), max-length runs (17 bytes), mixed.
- Sukara-title XOR-2 key handling: if the key is unknown for Sweetie HD, the node ships with `xor_2_key = None` and emits `utsushi.reallive.xor2_key_unknown` Warning — the node does not silently skip the second pass and pretend success. Concrete identification of the Sweetie HD encryption mechanism is captured in docs/research/reallive-sweetie-hd-encryption-mechanism.md; any recovered mask lands as a separate concrete node, not as a research deferral.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit 21291af). AvgDecompressor + AVG32_XOR_MASK; Sweetie HD scene-1 verified (1660 bytes, opener set, preamble self-consistency).
audit_focus: The 256-byte mask must be re-derived (it's a public byte string; re-typing from RLDEV docs is fine, but copying from `rlvm/src/libreallive/compression.cc` is not — the constants must be attributed)
LZSS window pointer arithmetic copied verbatim from C without re-derivation
Wrong distance-encoding silently producing garbage

nodes.title: Bytecode element stream decoder
nodes.spec:
Implement the lead-byte switch (`0x00`/`0x2C` comma, `0x0A`/`0x21`/`0x40` meta, `0x24` expression, `0x23` command, default textout) on the decompressed scene bytes. Produce a `Vec<BytecodeElement>` with each element carrying its scene-blob byte range. Verifies against Sweetie HD scene #0001 decompressed bytes 0..1660.

Deliverables:
- Lead-byte dispatch over decompressed scene bytes producing `Vec<BytecodeElement>`
- Per-element scene-blob byte range partition (matching KAIFUU-173 parser-boundary contract)
- `CommandElement` header decode exposing `module_type`, `module_id`, `opcode (u16 LE)`, `arg_count`, `overload`
- Selection-element option marker recognition (`0x30`-`0x34`) distinct from default textout
- Sweetie HD scene #0001 partition test (≤200, ≥50 elements; first is `0x21` or `0x40` meta)
nodes.acceptance:
- Decoding Sweetie HD scene #0001's 1660 uncompressed bytes produces a bounded element stream (target: ≤ 200 elements, ≥ 50 elements based on the 1660-byte size and typical RealLive density). The first element is either an entrypoint MetaElement (`0x21`) or a kidoku MetaElement (`0x40`).
- The element-stream byte ranges partition the 1660 uncompressed bytes completely (same partition guarantee as the existing parser-boundary contract in KAIFUU-173).
- The first `CommandElement` decoded must have `command[0]=0x23` and expose `module_type` (byte 1), `module_id` (byte 2), `opcode` (u16 LE at bytes 3-4), `arg_count` (byte 5), `overload` (byte 6).
- Selection-element option markers (`0x30`-`0x34`) are recognised and distinguished from default textout.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged. BytecodeElement + decode_bytecode_stream in utsushi-reallive; 235 elements on Sweetie HD scene-1, partition invariant holds.
audit_focus: 'Default branch' textout swallowing meta-marker bytes
Forgetting that `0x00` and `0x2C` are both comma
Treating `0x40` as arithmetic instead of meta

nodes.title: Expression evaluator (RealLive expression byte-stream)
nodes.spec:
Implement the RealLive expression byte-stream reader: arithmetic 0x02-0x09, comparison 0x28-0x2D, logical 0x3C/0x3D, compound assignment 0x14-0x24, `0xFF` int-literal, `0xC8` store-register, `$<bank>[<idx_expr>]` memory reference, `(`/`)` grouping, `,` separator. Synthetic fixtures cover the table; real Sweetie HD bytes exercised once the VM lands.

Deliverables:
- Expression byte-stream reader covering arithmetic, comparison, logical, compound-assignment, literal, store-register, memory-ref, grouping, separator
- 50-case synthetic round-trip suite spanning every operator
- Evaluator with variable-bank read/write through the substrate facade
- `utsushi.reallive.unknown_expression_operator` Warning emission for out-of-spec bytes
- Gated real-bytes test (`expression_real_sweetie_hd_first_command_args`) wired for UTSUSHI-208 follow-up
nodes.acceptance:
- Round-trip 50 synthetic expressions covering each operator at least once; serialised bytes round-trip through the parser.
- Evaluate `$\x0B[0]+5` (intB[0] + 5) against a variable bank where intB[0]=10 → 15. Specific operator/bank cases: `\xFF\x01\x00\x00\x00 \x06 \xFF\x02\x00\x00\x00` (1 + 2) → 3; `\xFF\x05\x00\x00\x00 \x29 \xFF\x05\x00\x00\x00` (5 < 5) → 0; `$\x0B[\xFF\x00\x00\x00\x00] \x14 \xFF\x07\x00\x00\x00` (intB[0] = 7) updates intB[0] to 7.
- Operators outside the documented byte set emit `utsushi.reallive.unknown_expression_operator` Warning and the expression returns its partial result.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit a42e31b). Expression evaluator + VarBanks; 20/20 Sweetie HD scene-1 expressions parse to Assignment ASTs.
audit_focus: Sign extension of `i32 LE` constants
Adding C-style precedence to flat / fully-parenthesised RealLive expressions
Store-register read vs write distinction silently confused

nodes.title: Variable banks + store register (typed `intA`..`intZ` / `strS`/`strM`/`strK`)
nodes.spec:
Define typed banks `intA`-`intZ` (13 letters per RLDEV; rlvm caps each at 2,000), `strS`/`strM`/`strK`, and a u32 store register. Expose `get(bank, idx) -> Value`, `set(bank, idx, value)`, and a `Snapshot` / `Restore` impl wired to the substrate `Inspectable`/`Restorable` traits so VM state snapshots flow through UTSUSHI-023 unchanged.

Deliverables:
- Typed bank model (`intA`..`intZ`, `strS`, `strM`, `strK`) capped at 2,000 indices each
- u32 store register
- `Snapshot` / `Restore` impl wired through substrate `Inspectable`/`Restorable` traits
- Sparse snapshot JSON (<1KB for empty machine)
- Shift-JIS string-bank round-trip preserving bytes verbatim
- `utsushi.reallive.bank_index_out_of_range` Warning with clamp on overflow writes
nodes.acceptance:
- `intA[0] = 42; snapshot; intA[0] = 99; restore; assert intA[0] == 42` round-trips through `SnapshotStore`.
- `Snapshot` JSON for an empty machine is < 1 KB (no per-bank zero-fill); only set indices appear.
- Out-of-range writes (e.g. `intA[2000]`) emit `utsushi.reallive.bank_index_out_of_range` Warning and clamp.
- String banks store as Shift-JIS bytes verbatim — not lossy UTF-8 round trip.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit a351a63). Sparse VarBanks + Inspectable/Restorable; empty snapshot 361 bytes; 185 tests pass.
audit_focus: Treating `intC` as identical to `intA` (the banks are semantically distinct in scripts)
Snapshot key format leaking banks-by-position rather than banks-by-name

nodes.title: Structured Gameexe.ini parser (Shift-JIS, dotted-path)
nodes.spec:
Replace the line-classifier in `kaifuu-reallive::gameexe` (parser-boundary inventory only) with a structured Shift-JIS parser producing a `Gameexe` tree that supports `get_str`, `get_int_array`, `get_tuple3`, and dotted-path lookup (`get("SYSCOM.005.000")`). Verifies against Sweetie HD `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/Gameexe.ini`.

Deliverables:
- Structured `Gameexe` tree with `get_str`, `get_int`, `get_int_array`, `get_int_pair`, `get_tuple3`, and `list_namespace` accessors
- Dotted-path lookup (`SYSCOM.005.000`)
- Shift-JIS preservation on read and round-trip on output
- Mixed `=`/`:` separator handling (FOLDNAME triples) and parenthesised value lists (NAMAE)
- Sweetie HD pinned values: SEEN_START, CAPTION, FOLDNAME.G00, SCREENSIZE_MOD, CANCELCALL, MOUSEACTIONCALL.000.AREA, WINDOW_ATTR, SYSCOM.* count, NAMAE.* count
nodes.acceptance:
- Against `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/Gameexe.ini` (1,345 lines): `gameexe.get_int("SEEN_START") == 1` (verified: `#SEEN_START=0001`); `gameexe.get_str("CAPTION") == "オシオキSweetie＋Sweets!! HD Edition　"`; `gameexe.get_tuple3("FOLDNAME.G00") == ("G00", 0, "G00.PAK")`; `gameexe.get_int_array("SCREENSIZE_MOD") == [999, 1280, 720]`; `gameexe.get_int_pair("CANCELCALL") == (9999, 10)`; `gameexe.get_int_array("MOUSEACTIONCALL.000.AREA") == [1232, 0, 1279, 719]`; `gameexe.get_int_array("WINDOW_ATTR") == [100, 100, 160, 200, 0]`; `gameexe.list_namespace("SYSCOM").len() >= 32`; `gameexe.list_namespace("NAMAE").len() == 11`.
- The parser handles the `=` / `:` mixed separator (FOLDNAME line) and parenthesised value lists (`(1,016, -1)` in NAMAE lines).
- Shift-JIS encoding is preserved on output; round-trip is exact for keys the parser recognises.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit fefe3b6). Gameexe Shift-JIS parser in utsushi-reallive (no kaifuu dep); 1344 dotted-path entries on real Sweetie HD; 22 unit tests + 4 real-bytes test entries.
audit_focus: Lossy UTF-8 conversion silently dropping high-byte characters
Reusing `kaifuu-reallive`'s inventory classifier under the hood instead of parsing structure
Failing to model the `KEY = "..." = N : "..."` triple shape

nodes.title: Bytecode VM (fetch / decode / dispatch / advance)
nodes.spec:
Implement `Vm { scene, pc, stack, banks, store_reg, longop_queue }` with fetch/decode/dispatch/advance loop. Dispatch hooks call into per-module RLOperation tables (separate nodes below). Snapshot/restore round-trips paused longops through the substrate facade. [substrate-gap: requires substrate extension — see docs/research/reallive-engine-dag-proposal.md substrate-gap callouts]

Deliverables:
- `Vm { scene, pc, stack, banks, store_reg, longop_queue }` runtime with fetch/decode/dispatch/advance loop
- `max_steps` deterministic out-of-budget terminator (no panic on infinite goto loop)
- `gosub`/`ret` and cross-scene `farcall`/`rtl` stack-frame handling
- Longop yield/resume + snapshot-at-suspend round trip through `SnapshotStore`
- Sweetie HD scene #0001 step-until-Unimplemented harness
nodes.acceptance:
- Stepping the VM on a synthetic scene `goto +0` infinite loop with a `max_steps=100` terminator produces a deterministic `out_of_budget` outcome (no panic).
- A `gosub` followed by `ret` returns the pc to the post-`gosub` byte.
- A `farcall` (cross-scene) followed by `rtl` returns to the calling scene at the post-`farcall` byte.
- Longop yields (synthetic `pause` longop) suspend the VM; the next `step` call resumes from the paused state, and a snapshot taken at the suspend point restores into the same longop with the same private state.
- End-to-end: stepping Sweetie HD scene #0001 emits at least one `CommandElement` dispatch (the first command of the prologue) before hitting an `Unimplemented` opcode boundary — proves the VM can drive a real scene up to the opcode coverage frontier.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit 4c69a40). Vm + RLOperation + DispatchOutcome + LongOp scheduler + Inspectable/Restorable; 213 tests pass.
audit_focus: Longop scheduler being a placeholder enum that never fires
`step` advancing the pc by a constant instead of by the element's byte length
Gosub/ret stack mishandling on cross-scene jumps

nodes.title: Text / messaging RLOperation family (module_msg subset)
nodes.spec:
Implement the text/messaging opcodes (module_msg equivalent): `text` (textout element), `pause`, `par`, `br`, `page`, `msgHide`, `msgHideAll`, `msgClear`, `FontColor`, `FontSize`, `TextWindow`, `FastText`, `NormalText`, `FaceOpen`, `FaceClose`. Target: ~15 opcodes of the ~35 in rlvm's module_msg. Speaker resolution through the `NAMAE` Gameexe table.

Deliverables:
- Implementation of ~15 module_msg opcodes through the substrate `TextSurfaceSink`
- `TextLine` emission with speaker (from `intA`/`intB` per RealLive convention) and Shift-JIS-decoded body
- Synthetic `[textout 'こんにちは'] [pause]` -> one `TextLine` + idle smoke
- Sweetie HD scene #0001 first-textout match (gated on UTSUSHI-203/204/208)
- `utsushi.reallive.unimplemented_opcode` Warning for the ~20 unimplemented opcodes (carries module_type, module_id, opcode)
nodes.acceptance:
- Each implemented opcode emits exactly one `TextLine` / `TextSurfaceEvent` through the substrate `TextSurfaceSink`, with the speaker name (from `intA` / `intB` per RealLive convention) and Shift-JIS-decoded body.
- A synthetic scene `[textout "こんにちは"] [pause]` produces one `TextLine { speaker: "", body: "こんにちは" }` followed by an idle state until the next input event.
- The decoded text matches the Shift-JIS round-trip exactly (no UTF-8 drift).
- Unimplemented opcodes in this module emit `utsushi.reallive.unimplemented_opcode` Warning carrying `module_type`, `module_id`, `opcode` — and the VM advances past them without aborting.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit 4c87424). 13 module_msg RLOps + PauseLongOp + SelectLongOp; Sweetie HD scene 1 emits 3 TextLines including ＳｅｅｎＥｎｄ.
audit_focus: TextLine emission shape (must use existing facade type, not a new one)
Decoded body being lossy
Speaker name not resolved through `NAMAE` table

nodes.title: Control-flow RLOperation family (`goto`/`gosub`/`farcall`/`ret`/`rtl` subset)
nodes.spec:
Control-flow opcodes: `goto`, `goto_if`, `goto_unless`, `goto_on`, `goto_case`, `gosub`, `gosub_if`, `gosub_unless`, `gosub_on`, `gosub_with`, `ret`, `ret_with`, `rtl`, `rtl_with`, `jump`, `farcall`, `farcall_with`. Target: 17 of rlvm's 22. Exposes `farcall` for the system-call dispatch node to consume.

Deliverables:
- Implementation of 17 control-flow opcodes covering conditional + indexed + case + gosub_with + farcall variants
- Parameter stack-frame push/pop for `gosub_with` and `ret_with`
- Cross-scene `farcall` validated against `RealSceneIndex` scene entries
- Default-sink semantics for `goto_case` (fallthrough, not Fatal)
- `farcall(9999, 10)` smoke against Sweetie HD scene 9999 entrypoint layout
nodes.acceptance:
- `goto_if($intA[0] == 1, label)`: with `intA[0]=1`, pc advances to label; with `intA[0]=0`, pc advances to next element.
- `goto_on($intA[0], [l0, l1, l2, l3])`: with `intA[0]=2`, pc advances to `l2`.
- `goto_case($intA[0], [(1, l1), (5, l5)])`: with `intA[0]=5`, pc advances to `l5`; with `intA[0]=99`, pc advances past the `goto_case` (default sink).
- `gosub_with(label, $intA[0])`: pushes a stack frame whose parameter slot 0 = current intA[0]; the called scene's expressions can read that parameter; `ret_with(...)` propagates the return value back into the caller's store register.
- `farcall(scene_id, entrypoint)`: cross-scene jump with proper stack-frame push for `rtl` return; targets must be valid scene entries in the `RealSceneIndex`.
- System-call entry into Sweetie HD scene 9999 via the `CANCELCALL=9999,10` route works once UTSUSHI-212 (system-call dispatch) is in place; this node just exposes `farcall` for UTSUSHI-212 to call.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit cbbd7a6). 12 control-flow opcodes + SelectionLongOp; 277 tests pass.
audit_focus: Off-by-one on entrypoint-table indexing
Missing parameter-stack cleanup on `ret_with`
Treating `goto_case` default as a fatal instead of a fallthrough

nodes.title: Choice family (`select` / `select_s` / `select_w` / `select_objbtn`)
nodes.spec:
Implement `select`, `select_s`, `select_w`, `select_objbtn`. The choice mechanism is a longop: it suspends the VM, emits one `TextLine` per option (annotated as a choice), waits for an `InputEvent::ChoiceMade(ChoiceIndex)`, then resumes by writing the index into the store register. Honours `SELBTN.NNN.*` styling from Gameexe. [substrate-gap: requires substrate extension — see docs/research/reallive-engine-dag-proposal.md substrate-gap callouts]

Deliverables:
- `select` / `select_s` / `select_w` / `select_objbtn` longop implementations using the substrate scheduler
- Choice-kind `TextLine` emission per option through `TextSurfaceSink`
- `InputEvent::ChoiceMade(ChoiceIndex)` resume path writing the index to store_reg
- `SELBTN.NNN.*` Gameexe styling honoured on rendered choice text
- First-choice-scene smoke against Sweetie HD Seen.txt (discoverable post-UTSUSHI-203/204)
nodes.acceptance:
- A synthetic scene with `select_s ["a", "b", "c"]` emits 3 `TextLine` events of `kind = Choice` (existing substrate type), then suspends.
- Feeding `ChoiceIndex(1)` resumes; store register reads as 1; pc advances past the choice element.
- Sweetie HD's first `select`/`select_s` in scene #0001 (location TBD — will be the first 0x23-opener element with module_id matching sel-module dispatch in the real bytecode) decodes its choice strings correctly. (If scene #0001 doesn't have a choice, this node's real-bytes test targets the first scene that does — discoverable once UTSUSHI-203/204 land.)
- Choice strings honour `SELBTN.NNN.*` styling values from Gameexe.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md. | Re-tier 2026-06-24: pruned 1 dependsOn edge(s) that crossed the new tier boundary [UTSUSHI-225(continuous)]; per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md §6.
audit_focus: Longop coupling — the longop must use the substrate scheduler, not a private wait loop

nodes.title: String / memory / system-arithmetic RLOperation families
nodes.spec:
Implement string ops (`strcpy`, `strcat`, `strlen`, `Uppercase`, `Lowercase`, `itoa`, `atoi`, `strout`, `intout`, `strpos`, `strlpos`, `hantozen`, `zentohan`), memory ops (`setarray`, `setrng`, `cpyrng`, `setarray_stepped`, `setrng_stepped`, `cpyvars`, `sum`, `sums`), system arithmetic (`rnd`, `pcnt`, `abs`, `power`, `sin`, `cos`, `min`, `max`, `constrain`). Target: ~24 of the ~70 across rlvm's module_str + module_mem + module_sys arithmetic subset.

Deliverables:
- ~24 opcodes implemented across string, memory, and system-arithmetic modules
- Per-op input/output table (≥3 cases incl. boundary) wired as unit tests
- Deterministic `rnd` seeded from substrate `LogicalClock` with `SnapshotStore` round-trip of rng state
- Shift-JIS half/full-width conversions (`hantozen`, `zentohan`) verified
- Sparse memory-range setters (`setrng_stepped`, `setarray_stepped`) covered with step + bound cases
nodes.acceptance:
- Each op: input/output table with at least 3 cases including a boundary (empty string, zero-size range, max u16 index).
- `rnd` is deterministic when the substrate `LogicalClock` is fixed; snapshot/restore of the rng state round-trips through `SnapshotStore`.
- Shift-JIS handling: `Uppercase("ＡＢＣ")` returns `"ＡＢＣ"` (already upper); `hantozen("abc")` returns `"ａｂｃ"` (full-width); these are the documented half/full conversions per RLDEV.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: `rnd` reading from the OS rng instead of substrate clock-seeded rng
Encoding conversions silently dropping half-width katakana

nodes.title: System-call dispatch wired to Gameexe routes
nodes.spec:
Wire the eight Gameexe-declared system-call routes (`CANCELCALL`, `SYSTEMCALL_SAVE`/`LOAD`/`SYSTEM`, `MOUSEACTIONCALL.000`, `LOADCALL`, `EXAFTERCALL`, `WBCALL.000`-`007`) into the VM event loop. Each route is a `farcall(scene_id, entrypoint)` from the control-flow node triggered by the matching substrate `InputEvent` kind.

Deliverables:
- Eight system-call routes wired into the VM event loop as `farcall(scene_id, entrypoint)` invocations
- Pointer hot-region dispatch (`MOUSEACTIONCALL.000.AREA`) via `InputEvent` match
- `_MOD` flag handling (e.g. `CANCELCALL_MOD=0` disables the cancel route)
- Sweetie HD route smoke against `Gameexe.ini` lines 14-28
- No private dispatch path — all routes call into UTSUSHI-211's `farcall`
nodes.acceptance:
- Boot with Sweetie HD's `Gameexe.ini` loaded; the dispatcher reports 8 known routes with the documented (scene_id, entrypoint) pairs from `docs/research/reallive-engine.md` § H.
- `MOUSEACTIONCALL.000.AREA=1232,0,1279,719`: a pointer-move event with `(x=1250, y=300)` triggers the route; a pointer-move with `(x=100, y=100)` does not.
- `CANCELCALL_MOD=0` disables the cancel route entirely (mods interpreted per RLDEV).
- Routes call into UTSUSHI-211's `farcall` — no private dispatch path.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.

nodes.title: Graphics object stack (headless render pipeline)
nodes.spec:
Implement the rlvm `GraphicsSystem` equivalent: a stack of ~256 graphics objects (foreground + background planes), each with `(position, scale, alpha, colour_tone, image_ref, layer_order)` state, plus a render-pass that walks the stack and rasterises a per-frame `FrameArtifact` into the substrate artifact store. Deterministic PNG output. [substrate-gap: requires substrate extension — see docs/research/reallive-engine-dag-proposal.md substrate-gap callouts]

Deliverables:
- ~256-slot graphics object stack (foreground + background planes) with full per-object state
- Render-pass walking the stack into a `FrameArtifact` through the substrate `FrameArtifactSink`
- Deterministic-PNG output (no timestamp metadata; byte-identical across runs)
- `SCREENSIZE_MOD=999,1280,720` Gameexe-observed framebuffer dimensions
- Wipe object (full-screen colour) smoke producing a solid-colour PNG
- `FrameArtifact` carries `frame_index`, `evidence_tier=E1`, and a real PNG `artifact_id`
nodes.acceptance:
- Allocating 256 objects, setting positions, calling render → deterministic PNG bytes (same input → same output bytes including PNG metadata).
- Two render passes with the same state produce byte-identical PNGs.
- The render pass observes the `SCREENSIZE_MOD=999,1280,720` Gameexe value and emits a 1280x720 buffer.
- A 'wipe' object (full-screen colour) renders to a solid-colour PNG matching the documented colour byte order.
- The frame artifact carries `frame_index`, `evidence_tier=E1`, and an `artifact_id` resolving to a PNG blob.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md. | Re-tier 2026-06-24: pruned 1 dependsOn edge(s) that crossed the new tier boundary [UTSUSHI-226(continuous)]; per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md §6.

nodes.title: Graphics RLOperation family (module_grp + module_obj subset)
nodes.spec:
Implement the rlvm module_grp + module_obj_management + module_obj_fg_bg subset: `allocDC`, `wipe`, `shake`, `load`/`open`/`openBg`, `copy`/`fill`/`invert`/`mono`/`colour`/`light`, `fade`, `stretchBlit`/`zoom`, `objAlloc`/`objFree`/`objInit`/`objCopy`, per-object setters `objSetPos`, `objSetAlpha`, `objSetScale`, `objSetLayer`, `objShow`/`objHide`. Target: ~25 opcodes of the ~150 across rlvm's module_grp + module_obj_*.

Deliverables:
- ~25 graphics opcodes wired to the graphics object stack with observable mutations through `state_snapshot`
- `openBg("BG01A1")` flow: VFS read + g00 decode (UTSUSHI-217) + bg plane registration
- `fade(target_alpha, ms)` longop ticking the bg plane alpha across substrate clock ticks
- Gated real-bytes test against Sweetie HD `BG01A1.g00` (depends on UTSUSHI-217 landing)
- Layer-ordering honoured (`objSetLayer` actually re-orders render-pass output)
nodes.acceptance:
- Each opcode produces an observable mutation of UTSUSHI-213's graphics object stack visible via a `state_snapshot` API.
- `openBg("BG01A1")` reads `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/g00/BG01A1.g00` via the substrate VFS and registers it as the bg plane background; the next render emits a 1280x720 PNG whose top-left pixel matches the documented bg colour (after g00 type-0 decode lands in UTSUSHI-217).
- `fade(target_alpha, ms)` schedules a longop that mutates the bg plane's alpha over `ms / clock_tick_period` ticks.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Opcodes that mutate state but never produce a visible effect (stack updates that don't render)
Layer-ordering that ignores `objSetLayer`

nodes.title: g00 image decoder (types 0 / 1 / 2)
nodes.spec:
Decode the three g00 sub-formats: type 0 (raw 24-bpp BGR), type 1 (8-bpp paletted + LZSS), type 2 (24-bpp + region list + LZSS). Output is a `(width, height, pixels_rgba: Vec<u8>, regions: Vec<G00Region>)`. Verifies against Sweetie HD `BACK.g00`, `BG01A1.g00`, and a 2,450-file corpus histogram.

Deliverables:
- Decoders for g00 types 0, 1, and 2 with shared LZSS distance-encoding
- BGR -> RGBA pixel reorder
- Type 2 region list exposed as `Vec<G00Region { rect, name? }>` for `objLoadRegion`
- Sweetie HD `BACK.g00` type-0 decode pinned (width/height + first-pixel BGR order)
- 2,450-file corpus histogram (type 0/1/2/unknown distribution) + `utsushi.reallive.g00_no_type_N_in_corpus` Warning
nodes.acceptance:
- For Sweetie HD's `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/g00/BACK.g00` (type 0): decoded width is non-zero, decoded `pixels_rgba.len()` matches `width * height * 4`, and the first pixel matches the documented BGR byte order from the file header.
- A directory-wide histogram pass reports the lead-byte distribution across all 2,450 `.g00` files (counts of type 0 / 1 / 2 / unknown). The acceptance criterion requires types 0, 1, and 2 are each decoded for at least one Sweetie HD file (if the corpus contains that type) — emit `utsushi.reallive.g00_no_type_N_in_corpus` for types not present.
- Type 2 decoded files expose a `regions: Vec<G00Region { rect, name? }>` list usable by `objLoadRegion` in UTSUSHI-214.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Treating 'BGR' as 'RGB' silently
LZSS distance encoding regression that decodes a few bytes and then garbage
Region table off-by-one against type 2 sub-bitmap counts

nodes.title: Audio system: NWA + OVK decoders + AudioEvent emitter
nodes.spec:
Implement NWA decoder (raw PCM + run-length variants), OVK decoder (16-byte header entries + Ogg Vorbis sample passthrough), and RLOperations `bgmPlay`, `bgmStop`, `bgmFadeOut`, `koePlay`, `koeStop`, `wavPlay`, `wavStop`, `playSe`. Target: ~15 of rlvm's ~60 across module_bgm + module_koe + module_pcm + module_se. [substrate-gap: requires substrate extension — see docs/research/reallive-engine-dag-proposal.md substrate-gap callouts]

Deliverables:
- NWA decoder (raw PCM + RLE variants) verified against Sweetie HD `ASA.nwa`
- OVK decoder (16-byte header entries, Ogg Vorbis passthrough) verified against `z0001.ovk`
- ~15 audio RLOperations (bgm/koe/wav/se) emitting `AudioEvent` through the substrate sink
- `koePlay` resolution through the `NAMAE` speaker table to `(archive_id, sample_id)` metadata
- `bgmPlay` resolution through `FOLDNAME.BGM` to the on-disk NWA path
nodes.acceptance:
- NWA: against `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/bgm/ASA.nwa` (18,317,046 bytes, raw 16-bit PCM), decoder returns 33,818,820 sample frames at 44,100 Hz, 16-bit, 2-channel.
- OVK: against `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/koe/z0001.ovk`, decoder returns 2 entries with `(sample_num=46, length=36)` and `(sample_num=52, length=183,476)`. The first sample's raw bytes start with `OggS` magic.
- `koePlay($intA[0]=46)` resolves through the speaker table to `z0001.ovk sample 46` and emits `AudioEvent { kind: VoicePlay, archive_id: "z0001", sample_id: 46, evidence_tier: E1 }`.
- `bgmPlay("ASA")` resolves through `Gameexe FOLDNAME.BGM` to `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/bgm/ASA.nwa` and emits `AudioEvent { kind: BgmStart, asset_id: "bgm/ASA", evidence_tier: E1 }`.
- No actual sample mixing required; the decoder just verifies header decode and emits metadata.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Treating NWA as raw bytes (i.e. skipping the offset table)
OVK entry size as anything other than 16 bytes
AudioEvent payload missing voice-archive metadata

nodes.title: Save / load (AVG-derived format: REALLIVE.sav / save999.sav / read.sav)
nodes.spec:
Implement read + write of `REALLIVE.sav` (per-slot system save), `save999.sav` (global save), `read.sav` (per-line read flags). Format follows the AVG32-derived `SAVE_FORMAT=3` Gameexe declaration. Substrate `SnapshotStore` is the in-memory backing; on-disk serialiser is separate.

Deliverables:
- `SystemSave`, `GlobalSave`, `ReadFlags` typed readers/writers
- AVG-derived `SAVE_FORMAT=3` serialiser keyed against magic strings (`AVG_SYSTEM_SAVE`, `AVG_GLOBAL_SAVE`)
- Shift-JIS title decode (`オシオキSweetie＋Sweets!! HD Edition\u{3000}`) round-trip
- Synthetic round-trip producing byte-identical output
- Read-only mount enforcement (writes to the research mount banned at the test layer)
nodes.acceptance:
- Reading `$GAME/SAVEDATA/REALLIVE.sav` produces a `SystemSave { magic: "AVG_SYSTEM_SAVE", slots: [...] }` with the declared file-size (24,876) cross-checked against the `2C 61 00 00` leading u32.
- Reading `$GAME/SAVEDATA/save999.sav` produces a `GlobalSave { magic: "AVG_GLOBAL_SAVE", ... }`.
- Reading `$GAME/SAVEDATA/read.sav` produces a `ReadFlags { title: "オシオキSweetie＋Sweets!! HD Edition\u{3000}", ... }` (the Shift-JIS title decodes round-trip).
- Writing a freshly-snapshotted save produces byte-identical output to a known synthetic fixture (round-trip).
- The substrate `SnapshotStore` is used as the in-memory backing for save state; on-disk write is a separate serialiser.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: promoted continuous -> alpha — required for the redefined alpha (utsushi-reallive Linux runtime / real-bytes extraction) per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Writing to the read-only research mount (must be banned at the test layer)
Endianness flips between read and write
Silently truncating slots

nodes.title: Sukara title XOR-2 key resolution (research-only)
nodes.spec:
Cancelled 2026-06-24 as an instance of the "Investigation as a DAG node" anti-pattern (see docs/orchestration-operating-model.md). The XOR-2 question is being resolved interactively via the encryption-mechanism probe under docs/research/; concrete follow-up (a `xor_2_key = None` ship, a recovered key constant + test, or a specific recovery node) gets written from that probe's findings instead of from this planning ticket. Original framing follows: Determine whether Sweetie HD's compiler-version-110002 bytecode uses the AVG32 second-level XOR pass, and if so, recover the key. Research-only node — no code changes if the key is off; one constant + documentation if it's recoverable. Either outcome acceptable; what is not is shipping UTSUSHI-203 with a hardcoded 'Key 09' guess and pretending it's Sukara's.

Deliverables:
- Bench tool under `crates/utsushi-reallive/benches/` decompressing scene #0001 with no XOR-2 and reporting byte statistics
- Entropy + lead-byte distribution analysis against the documented opener set
- Known-bytes attack scaffold (if entropy random) seeded by MetaElement opener at offset 0
- `docs/research/reallive-engine.md` update recording the resolved finding (key off OR key + value OR follow-up path)
- Shipped `xor_2_key = None` constant when the title family is confirmed key-off
nodes.acceptance:
- A bench tool (under `crates/utsushi-reallive/benches/`) decompresses Sweetie HD scene #0001 with no XOR-2 pass and reports byte statistics of the first 64 bytes (entropy, lead-byte distribution against the documented `{0x00, 0x0a, 0x21, 0x23, 0x24, 0x2c, 0x40}` + Shift-JIS leads).
- If the entropy is structured (key off), the node ships with `xor_2_key = None` for the Sukara title family and `docs/research/reallive-engine.md` is updated to record the finding.
- If the entropy is random (key on), the node ships with a known-bytes attack (RealLive scenes always start with a MetaElement opener byte `0x21` or `0x40` at offset 0 of the bytecode) and either recovers the key or documents the recovery path for a follow-up node.
- Either outcome is acceptable; what is not acceptable is shipping UTSUSHI-203 with a hardcoded 'Key 09' guess and pretending it's Sukara's.
nodes.status_reason: Investigation deferred into the DAG (2026-06-24 rule). Research happens interactively; concrete nodes get written from findings. See docs/orchestration-operating-model.md "Investigation as a DAG node" anti-pattern.
audit_focus: Silent acceptance of garbage decompressed bytes
Using a Visual-Arts title key on a Sukara title

nodes.title: End-to-end Sweetie HD scene-1 text-replay smoke
nodes.spec:
Drive Sweetie HD scene #0001 through the VM until either (a) the first `pause` opcode fires producing a `TextLine` capture through the substrate `TextSurfaceSink`, or (b) an unimplemented opcode trips a documented diagnostic. Byte-deterministic `ReplayLog` JSON across two runs; snapshot/restore identity at any tick boundary.

Deliverables:
- `utsushi-reallive::replay_scene(seen_path, 1)` driver function
- `ReplayLog` JSON capturing TextLine events + diagnostics, byte-deterministic across runs
- Snapshot-at-tick-boundary + restore-into-identical-state round trip
- Warning-not-Fatal posture for unimplemented opcodes so the smoke reaches first textout
- Sweetie HD scene #0001 end-to-end smoke producing at least one non-empty Shift-JIS `TextLine`
nodes.acceptance:
- Running `utsushi-reallive::replay_scene(seen_path, 1)` against `$GAME/REALLIVEDATA/Seen.txt` produces a `ReplayLog` with at least one `TextLine` event whose body is non-empty Shift-JIS text.
- The replay is byte-deterministic: two runs produce identical `ReplayLog` JSON.
- The same replay can be snapshotted at any tick boundary and restored to identical state.
- Unimplemented opcodes emit Warnings (not Fatals) so the run reaches 'first textual output' before any unknown stops it.
nodes.status_reason: Implementation merged (commit 2f2901b). Alpha-defining e2e: real Sweetie HD scene 1 -> first TextLine 州 via TextSurfaceSink. Byte-deterministic JSON (SHA256 ea7ceaa9…dacc0). Snapshot/restore round-trips. 31 unknown opcodes as warnings; final_outcome EndOfScene events=271 (never Fatal).
audit_focus: The test passing because the VM happens to halt on a Warning before producing any output
'Deterministic' actually being flaky and hidden by retry
Snapshot-restore being a no-op

nodes.title: Cross-engine substrate conformance + Siglus lineage notes
nodes.spec:
Tie the RealLive port into UTSUSHI-147's cross-engine conformance fixture. Document which sub-nodes of the decomposition will be reusable when the Siglus port lands (the AVG32 -> RealLive -> Siglus lineage Visual Arts documents). Boundary-aware: 'reusable' claims must be proven against a Siglus prototype rather than asserted.

Deliverables:
- Cross-engine facade-only-imports conformance test reusing UTSUSHI-147's fixture
- Appendix in `docs/research/reallive-engine.md` documenting reusable vs RealLive-only surfaces (expression encoding, bank model, AVG32 LZ+XOR, Gameexe-style config vs rlvm-specific opcode catalogue, OVK voice archives, module identifiers)
- Engine-specific boundary notes wherever an acceptance criterion would break under a Siglus reuse claim
- Identical-import audit between RealLive scaffold and Siglus minimal-port scaffold
nodes.acceptance:
- UTSUSHI-200..UTSUSHI-220's facade usage is confirmed identical to a Siglus minimal-port scaffold (only `utsushi_core::substrate::*` imports; no engine-specific facade exceptions).
- Lineage notes in `docs/research/reallive-engine.md` (new appendix) document: reusable across engines (expression encoding, variable banks — Siglus uses 26 letters not 13 but the trait carries, AVG32 LZ + XOR, Gameexe-style config — Siglus uses Resource.txt but dotted-path tree generalises, headless sink pipeline, snapshot/restore contract); RealLive-only (rlvm-specific opcode catalogue, OVK voice archives — Siglus uses different containers, specific module identifiers).
- Any UTSUSHI-200..UTSUSHI-220 node whose acceptance criterion would break under a Siglus reuse claim emits a documented 'engine-specific boundary' note instead of pretending portability.

nodes.title: Substrate M.1 — composite asset package + try-dir-then-archive resolver
nodes.spec:
Replace the single-path VFS resolver with `CompositeAssetPackage` whose `resolve` walks an ordered source list of `(plaintext_dir | archive_reader)` first-match-wins, with case-folded O(1) directory indices. Introduces the `AssetArchiveReader` trait so PAK and XP3 archive readers can plug in later without re-architecting the resolver. Validated against ≥2 engine families' real bytes (RealLive Sweetie HD's `#FOLDNAME.G00 = "G00" = 0 : "G00.PAK"` pattern + MV/MZ Lust Memory's `www/img/` + `www/data/`). The single-source resolver shipped with UTSUSHI-020 is removed in the same change — no shim, no #[deprecated]. From docs/audits/substrate-honesty.md §M.1.

Deliverables:
- `CompositeAssetPackage` impl in `utsushi-core::vfs` with ordered source list
- `AssetArchiveReader` trait (sealed) with empty default impl set
- Case-folded directory index built lazily on first resolve, cached per source
- Old single-path resolver fully removed from `utsushi-core::vfs` (no shim)
- Integration test loading the RealLive Sweetie HD asset layout via `KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH` env-gated test
- Integration test loading the MV/MZ Lust Memory `www/data/` layout via `KAIFUU_REAL_LUST_MEMORY_PATH` env-gated test
nodes.acceptance:
- `CompositeAssetPackage` resolves a `G00/BG01A1.g00` query by first checking the `G00/` plaintext dir and falling back to a registered `G00.PAK` archive reader without rebuilding the directory index between calls.
- Against `/scratch/itotori-research/sweetie-hd/extracted/.../REALLIVEDATA/`: resolver enumerates 13 asset folders with at least one plaintext-only folder, one archive-only folder, and one mixed folder; each enumerated entry's path is reachable via `composite.resolve(path)` in O(1) per query.
- Against `/scratch/itotori-research/rpg-maker-mv-mz/extracted/.../www/`: resolver enumerates `data/`, `img/`, `audio/` as plaintext sources; resolving `data/System.json` returns identical bytes to `fs::read(<path>)`.
- `rg -n 'fn resolve_asset' crates/utsushi-core/src/vfs/` returns zero hits for the legacy single-path resolver name; `git log -p` of the merge commit shows the old symbol deleted, not just unused.
- Acceptance test runs are gated on the two env vars; when unset, the tests `assert!(env::var(...).is_err())` and skip with an explicit `eprintln!` rather than silently passing.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: kept alpha under the new 4-tier framework (single-game Sweetie HD e2e set per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md).

Acceptance criteria that exercise only synthetic fixtures — multi-game requirement not satisfied; demote to `planned` until the second corpus is exercised.
Case-folded index that rebuilds per call (silent O(n) on every resolve) — must be lazily cached.
`AssetArchiveReader` impls leaking outside the seal so callers can bypass the resolver's first-match policy.

nodes.title: Substrate M.2 — snapshot envelope size class (Small / Medium / Large)
nodes.spec:
Replace the fixed `SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES = 16 KiB` constant with a three-tier `SnapshotEnvelope::{Small, Medium, Large}` enum (16 KiB / 256 KiB / 4 MiB) declared per port at manifest level. Sweetie HD's `REALLIVE.sav` is 24 876 bytes raw; the normalised snapshot of the int/str banks + graphics layer state is at least 64 KiB and likely 256 KiB. The current 16 KiB constant is below the floor and silently truncates. Bumps `SnapshotSchemaVersion`. The old fixed constant is deleted in the same change — no shim, no compat mode. Validated against ≥2 engine families' snapshot shapes. From docs/audits/substrate-honesty.md §M.2.

Deliverables:
- `SnapshotEnvelope` enum in `utsushi-core::snapshot` with Small/Medium/Large variants and per-variant byte limit
- `SnapshotManifest::envelope_class` field; runner enforces per-port at write time
- `SnapshotSchemaVersion` bumped with explicit upgrade-path-not-supported note in changelog
- Old `SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES` constant fully removed (no `pub use` alias, no shim)
- Test that a Medium-class snapshot of RealLive Sweetie HD scene 1 state round-trips through serialize → deserialize → byte-equal compare
- Test that a Small-class snapshot exceeding 16 KiB returns a typed `SnapshotEnvelopeOverflow` error (not silent truncation)
nodes.acceptance:
- `SnapshotEnvelope::Small.max_bytes() == 16 * 1024`, `Medium.max_bytes() == 256 * 1024`, `Large.max_bytes() == 4 * 1024 * 1024` — const-asserted.
- A Medium-class snapshot serialized from the simulated RealLive Sweetie HD scene 1 state (int bank: 1000 entries, str bank: 200 entries, graphics: 4 layers each with asset_id + transform) round-trips byte-equal through `serialize` → `deserialize` → `serialize` and is between 64 KiB and 256 KiB.
- A Small-class snapshot whose serialized form exceeds 16 KiB returns `Err(SnapshotEnvelopeOverflow { envelope_class: Small, observed_bytes: <n>, limit_bytes: 16384 })` and writes nothing — no partial output, no silent truncation.
- `rg -n 'SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES' crates/` returns zero hits.
- A second engine family's snapshot test (MV/MZ `www/save/file1.rpgsave` shape — JSON object with `$gameSystem`, `$gameMap`, `$gameVariables`) round-trips through the Medium envelope at a non-trivial byte count.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: kept alpha under the new 4-tier framework (single-game Sweetie HD e2e set per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md).
audit_focus: Any preserved alias for `SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES` — P0.
Schema-version bump skipped or marked as backwards-compatible (it is not).
Test that proves the envelope works at fixture scale only — multi-engine requirement not satisfied.
`SnapshotEnvelope::Large` variant used where `Medium` suffices (over-allocation hiding the regression check).

nodes.title: Substrate M.3 — EnginePort → substrate-sinks bridge (legacy hooks removed)
nodes.spec:
Refactor `EnginePort::observe` to push into the substrate sinks (`TextSurfaceSink`, `FrameArtifactSink`, `AudioEventSink`) instead of returning the legacy `ObservationHookEvent` enum. Adds `EnginePort::sink_set(&self) -> &SinkSet` (or equivalent) so the runner drains sinks per tick. The legacy `ObservationHookEvent` type and every variant of it are deleted in the same change — no compat enum, no #[deprecated], no shim trait. Migrates `utsushi-fixture` to the new path so the alpha gate "≥1 non-test consumer of each substrate subsystem" is satisfied on day one. This is the single most-fixture-shaped element of the cascade per the substrate-honesty audit. From docs/audits/substrate-honesty.md §M.3.

Deliverables:
- `EnginePort` trait redefined: `observe` returns `Result<()>` (not `ObservationHookEvent`); new `sink_set(&self) -> &SinkSet` accessor
- `SinkSet` struct in `utsushi-core::substrate` holding `Arc<dyn TextSurfaceSink>` + `Arc<dyn FrameArtifactSink>` + `Arc<dyn AudioEventSink>`
- `Runner::tick` drains all three sinks per tick via the new accessor; ordering documented
- `ObservationHookEvent` enum + every variant + every match arm referencing it deleted from the workspace
- `utsushi-fixture` migrated to implement `EnginePort` via the new sinks-bridge path; its tests pass against the new trait without referencing the old enum
- `KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH`-gated test demonstrating a thin RealLive-shaped port pushing TextSurfaceSink + FrameArtifactSink events during a 10-tick run
- Equivalent test exercising `utsushi-fixture` to prove the substrate has ≥2 distinct production consumers (per the alpha gate)
nodes.acceptance:
- `rg -n 'ObservationHookEvent' crates/` returns zero hits across the workspace (production and test code).
- `EnginePort::observe` signature is `fn observe(&self, ctx: &ObserveContext) -> Result<(), EnginePortError>`; no `-> ObservationHookEvent` or `-> Vec<HookEvent>` form remains.
- `utsushi-fixture` implements `EnginePort` and its 10-tick run pushes at least one `TextSurfaceSink::emit` call and one `FrameArtifactSink::emit` call per tick where applicable; collector test asserts the counts.
- A second port impl in `crates/utsushi-reallive-port-skeleton` (or similar minimal crate) pushes the same sinks against simulated RealLive Sweetie HD scene 1 state; the substrate is exercised by ≥2 distinct production consumers.
- Runner ordering doc: `tick()` calls `engine.observe()` first, then drains text, then frame, then audio, in that order; documented invariant matched by behaviour test.
- No `#[deprecated]` markers, no `pub use ... as ObservationHookEvent` re-exports, no `legacy_observe()` compat method exists anywhere.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: kept alpha under the new 4-tier framework (single-game Sweetie HD e2e set per docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md).

nodes.title: Substrate M.4 — pixel-bound mouse area input event
nodes.spec:
Add `InputEvent::AreaHit { area_id: String, screen_x: u16, screen_y: u16 }` so the substrate can represent RealLive's `MOUSEACTIONCALL = AREA=x0,y0,x1,y1` semantics without falling back to `Raw` opacity. Records the hit deterministically in the replay log and surfaces a `bridge_unit_id` linkage so the hit maps to the scene-jump target. The legacy `Raw`-only mouse path stays only for engines that genuinely have no area concept (none of the alpha-claimed three); for RealLive specifically the `Raw` mouse path is replaced. Requires ≥2 real-world RealLive games to validate the area dispatch isn't Sweetie-HD-specific; until a second RealLive title is sourced this node remains `planned` with a sourcing-required note. From docs/audits/substrate-honesty.md §M.4.

Deliverables:
- `InputEvent::AreaHit` variant added to `utsushi-core::input::InputEvent`
- Replay log writer + reader round-trip the variant deterministically (golden test)
- `bridge_unit_id` field on `AreaHit` populated by the engine port; conformance check can compare across two ports' replay logs
- RealLive-specific code paths invoking `InputEvent::Raw` for mouse clicks deleted; only non-RealLive engines retain `Raw` opacity
- Test against Sweetie HD's `MOUSEACTIONCALL = AREA=1232,0,1279,719` declaration: a synthetic click at (1255, 359) emits `AreaHit { area_id: "mouseaction_call_0", screen_x: 1255, screen_y: 359 }` and the replay log round-trips byte-equal
- Test against a second real RealLive title's MOUSEACTIONCALL declaration (sourcing required — node stays planned until available)
nodes.acceptance:
- `InputEvent::AreaHit` exists with the named fields and is serialised by the replay log without falling through to `Raw`.
- Conformance check compares two ports' replay logs for the same scripted click and reports byte-equal `AreaHit` payloads or surfaces a typed `AreaIdMismatch` / `CoordinateMismatch` error.
- Against the real Sweetie HD `Gameexe.ini` `MOUSEACTIONCALL` declaration: the substrate test loads the area definition, simulates a click inside the rectangle, and asserts the emitted `AreaHit::area_id` matches the Gameexe-derived label.
- Sourcing-required note in the summary explicitly says "second RealLive title required before this node can complete"; orchestrator does not approve completion until the second corpus is exercised against the same code path.
- `rg -n 'InputEvent::Raw.*mouse|raw_mouse' crates/utsushi-reallive/` returns zero hits in production code paths once this node lands (Raw mouse remains only in `utsushi-fixture` and any non-RealLive engine).

verification.value: KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH=/scratch/itotori-research/sweetie-hd/extracted direnv exec . cargo test -p utsushi-core input_area_hit_real_bytes -- --include-ignored
Second RealLive title sourced and staged at /scratch/itotori-research/<game>/extracted before completion can be approved

audit_focus: Completion approved with only one real RealLive game exercised — violates the multi-game validation rule.
`InputEvent::Raw` retained as a parallel path for RealLive mouse clicks after this lands — must be deleted on the RealLive side.
`area_id` derived heuristically rather than from the Gameexe declaration — would not round-trip across two ports.

nodes.title: Substrate M.5 — frame-as-layer-composition sink
nodes.spec:
Extend the frame sink so a frame carries a structural `LayerComposition` (ordered Vec of `(layer_index, asset_id, transform, blend_mode)`) alongside any PNG artifact URI. Sweetie HD's `OBJECT_MAX=256` graphics layers is the ground-truth frame model — capturing only an artifact URI loses the layer-level evidence the conformance comparison wants. Adds `LayerCompositionSink::emit_composition(FrameComposition)` or extends `FrameArtifactSink` with `composition: Option<LayerComposition>`. Substrate does not render; the port produces both the composition and (separately) the PNG. E2 or E3 ceiling because the layer list is structural evidence. Legacy URI-only frame events are removed for RealLive; non-RealLive engines that don't have a layer model emit `composition: None`. Requires ≥2 real-world RealLive scenes (Sweetie HD scene 1 + one from a second title) to validate. From docs/audits/substrate-honesty.md §M.5.

Deliverables:
- `LayerComposition` struct in `utsushi-core::sinks::frame` carrying `Vec<LayerEntry>` where `LayerEntry { layer_index: u16, asset_id: AssetId, transform: AffineTransform, blend_mode: BlendMode }`
- `FrameArtifactSink::emit` signature extended with `composition: Option<LayerComposition>` (no separate sink; one drain path)
- RealLive-port-skeleton produces a `Some(composition)` payload for each frame; non-RealLive paths produce `None`
- Conformance ceiling for layer-composition evidence raised to E2 (replay-comparable) or E3 (frame-equivalent) per port declaration
- Old URI-only `emit` overload removed; all consumers updated to the unified signature in the same change
- Test against simulated Sweetie HD scene 1 layer stack (4-8 active layers) producing a composition that round-trips through serialize → deserialize → byte-equal
- Second-corpus test (RealLive title TBD; node stays planned until sourced)
nodes.acceptance:
- `FrameArtifactSink::emit(frame_id, artifact_uri, composition)` is the only frame emit method; no `emit_uri_only` overload remains.
- `LayerComposition` round-trips through serde JSON byte-equal across `serialize → deserialize → serialize` for a frame with ≥4 layers.
- Against simulated Sweetie HD scene 1 with `OBJECT_MAX=256` declared: a frame with 4 active layers (1 BG, 2 character sprites, 1 textbox) produces a `LayerComposition` whose entries are ordered ascending by `layer_index`, with each `asset_id` resolvable via the `CompositeAssetPackage` from M.1.
- Conformance check between two ports producing the same frame composition reports byte-equal layer lists or surfaces typed `LayerComposition::OutOfOrder` / `AssetIdMismatch` errors.
- `rg -n 'emit_uri_only|frame_emit_uri|fn emit_frame_uri' crates/` returns zero hits after this lands.
- Sourcing-required note in summary explicitly says "second RealLive title required"; completion not approved until the second corpus is exercised.

verification.value: KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH=/scratch/itotori-research/sweetie-hd/extracted direnv exec . cargo test -p utsushi-core layer_composition_real_bytes -- --include-ignored
Second RealLive title sourced and staged before completion can be approved

audit_focus: Completion approved with only one real RealLive scene exercised — violates the multi-game validation rule.
URI-only emit overload preserved for back-compat — must be deleted.
`LayerComposition::None` accepted where `Some(...)` is required for RealLive ports — silent down-grade hiding the layer evidence.
Conformance ceiling left at E0/E1 (smoke-only) — defeats the purpose of the structural evidence.

Deliverables:
- Cross-engine conformance fixture covering RealLive and Siglus ports against one substrate API surface
- Conformance evidence that both engines share VFS/render/snapshot semantics

- Conformance evidence proves shared VFS, render, and snapshot semantics across both engines without engine-specific facade exceptions
- Substrate API drift between the two engines fails the conformance fixture with a semantic diagnostic
- Lineage extension notes record the AVG32->RealLive->Siglus port-scope reasoning and any boundaries that do not extend

nodes.title: ReplayLog structural immutability hardening
nodes.spec:
Privatize ReplayLog and ReplayMetadata fields behind accessor methods so the substrate's read-only-after-build guarantee is enforced by the type system, not by convention. The serde wire form must not change.

Deliverables:
- Privatize ReplayLog::schema_version and ReplayLog::metadata; expose `schema_version()` and `metadata()` accessors returning `&ReplaySchemaVersion` and `&ReplayMetadata`.
- Privatize every field on ReplayMetadata and expose stable accessor methods (run_id, adapter_name, adapter_version, clock_origin, seed, source_label).
- Update all in-tree call sites (replay.rs unit tests, replay_round_trip.rs integration tests) to use accessors.
- Preserve the existing serde wire form (camelCase keys, same positions) — re-run the golden round-trip test and assert byte-equal JSON.
nodes.acceptance:
- ReplayLog and ReplayMetadata expose no public fields; all reads go through accessor methods.
- The replay_log_golden.json fixture continues to deserialize and re-serialize byte-for-byte equal to the pre-refactor form.
- An immutability inventory test asserts there is no `&mut self` method on ReplayLog and no `pub` field accessible for reassignment.
audit_focus: ReplayLog or ReplayMetadata fields remaining public.
Wire-form drift relative to the checked-in golden.

- Add a unit test that serializes a log containing every variant and asserts every JSON key matches the chosen convention.
nodes.acceptance:
- The serialized replay-log document uses a single case convention across all keys, including InputEvent payload fields.
- REPLAY_LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION is bumped if any external consumer has read the prior form; otherwise the change is in-place under the alpha pin.
- The case-convention assertion test passes against a log that exercises every InputEvent variant.
audit_focus: Any remaining snake_case key in the serialized form.

Implement length-changing patch-back for RealLive Scene/SEEN: offset-table rewrite when any edited scene's total byte length changes, jump-target recalculation for opcodes whose byte ranges shift, and a re-parse self-check that confirms the patched archive remains structurally identical to the source minus the edited slots.

nodes.title: RealLive protected-span catalogue expansion under Sweetie HD evidence
nodes.spec:
Expand the kaifuu-reallive ProtectedSpanKind catalogue when ALPHA-006 surfaces real-game control-byte shapes outside the bounded 9-kind set; each new kind requires a paired synthetic fixture line plus an RLDEV citation.

Deliverables:
- Per new ProtectedSpanKind variant: one synthetic fixture line, one RLDEV citation in the readiness-record addendum, one test in crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests/protected_spans.rs.
- Update crates/kaifuu-reallive/src/protected_spans.rs catalogue table in the module doc-comment to mirror the new kinds.
nodes.acceptance:
- kaifuu-reallive::detect_protected_spans recognizes each new kind without warnings.
- kaifuu.reallive.protected_span.unknown_control warning rate against the ALPHA-006 evidence pack drops to zero (or to a documented residual).

verification.value: ALPHA-006 evidence pack: confirm protected-span unknown_control warning rate against Sweetie HD bytes.

audit_focus: Catalogue expansion outpacing per-kind evidence (no silent additions).
Existing UnknownControl byte-preservation regressing as new kinds are added.

nodes.title: Reconcile BridgeBundle bridge_id stability convention across engine-fixture adapters
nodes.spec:
Either ratify deterministic_id as the bridge_id convention across Siglus/XP3/fixture/RealLive sibling adapters (and update the KAIFUU-174 plan + readiness record to match), or migrate all four to UUIDv7 simultaneously and update the byte-equality golden-snapshot tests to mask the field.

Deliverables:
- Decision recorded in docs/kaifuu-adapters/reallive.md addendum (and Siglus/XP3 equivalents).
- Either all four adapters use deterministic_id (plan §4.1 updated to match) or all four use uuid::Uuid::now_v7 with golden tests masking the field.
- Single PR migrating the four adapters atomically so the convention is uniform.
nodes.acceptance:
- Grep for `deterministic_id\(.*bridge.*\)` across kaifuu-engine-fixture returns matches in all four adapters or none.
- Grep for `Uuid::now_v7` against the bridge_id field returns matches in all four adapters or none.
- Plan §4.1 of KAIFUU-174 and the equivalent sections in the Siglus/XP3 readiness records agree with the implementation.
audit_focus: Mixed convention surviving the migration.
Golden-snapshot tests breaking when UUIDv7 is adopted without the masking update.

nodes.title: Tighter EvidenceRef locator shape for Fixture / Doc / ReferenceImplAnchor
nodes.spec:
Constrain EvidenceRef locator validation to require known root prefixes (`fixtures/`, `docs/`) for Fixture / Doc kinds and a colon-anchored URI shape for ReferenceImplAnchor, so audit-channel triage of research evidence-refs is mechanical instead of human.

Deliverables:
- Tightened is_evidence_locator_valid branches for Fixture, Doc, ReferenceImplAnchor
- Three new negative-shape tests and one positive-shape test per kind
- Updated JSON Schema artifact (re-blessed via BLESS_IMPL_MAP_SCHEMA) reflecting the tighter shape
- Positive fixture corpus updated if needed to satisfy the new shape
nodes.acceptance:
- Evidence-ref locators that point outside `fixtures/` (Fixture) or `docs/` (Doc) are rejected with ImplMapError::ResearchEvidenceLocatorMalformed.
- ReferenceImplAnchor locators that are neither http(s):// URLs nor `<scheme>:<path>:<token>` triples are rejected.
- JSON Schema mirrors the Rust validator's per-kind shape and the BLESS-gated drift guard passes.
audit_focus: Triage-cost of evidence-ref locators
Engine-neutrality (the tightened shape must not encode per-engine paths)
Schema parity with the Rust validator

nodes.title: Single-source EngineFamily -> manifest-id prefix vocabulary
nodes.spec:
Replace validator.rs's hard-coded engine_family_manifest_prefix `match` with a single source of truth (an associated method on EngineFamily) so adding a variant is a compile-time exhaustive-match obligation and the cross-validator cannot silently drift from the wire-name vocabulary.

Deliverables:
- EngineFamily::manifest_id_prefix(self) -> Option<&'static str> associated method (or equivalent const slice keyed off the enum)
- validate_against_manifest calls the new method instead of the local helper
- Exhaustive round-trip test over every EngineFamily variant
nodes.acceptance:
- Adding a new EngineFamily variant produces a compile error until the prefix surface is updated.
- validate_impl_map_against_manifest behavior is unchanged for the current variants.
- The engine_family_manifest_prefix helper in validator.rs is removed or trivially delegates.
audit_focus: Single source of truth between EngineFamily and manifest-id grammar
Cross-validator behaviour parity across the refactor
Compile-time exhaustiveness for future engine families

nodes.title: Outdated-detection algorithm for implementation-map status
nodes.spec:
Land an engine-neutral outdated-detection algorithm that flips a previously-Validated implementation map to Status::Outdated when its declared schemaVersion or referenced PortManifest version drifts from the live values, completing the Draft/Validated/Outdated typestate cycle UTSUSHI-025 ships as a variant.

Deliverables:
- An is_outdated(&map, &PortManifest, &str /* schema_version */) -> bool helper in utsushi_core::port::impl_map
- Integration with at least one engine-port consumer (the first to land in UTSUSHI-031..039 or UTSUSHI-146) so the algorithm is exercised against a real port
- Tests covering schema-version drift, manifest-version drift, and the no-drift case
nodes.acceptance:
- Maps with a schemaVersion older than IMPL_MAP_SCHEMA_VERSION (within the same major) are flagged Outdated.
- Maps whose PortManifest::version differs from a recorded baseline are flagged Outdated.
- Validated maps with no drift remain Validated.
- Outdated maps preserve their Outdated status through re-validation.
audit_focus: Engine-neutrality of the detection algorithm
Interaction with the BLESS schema drift guard
Correct preservation of Outdated through repeated validation

nodes.title: Fixture snapshot adoption (UTSUSHI-023 Slice B)
nodes.spec:
Refactor utsushi-fixture so its runtime adapter implements `Inspectable` + `Restorable` against the UTSUSHI-023 substrate, providing the first non-synthetic snapshot round-trip proof and the canonical reference port that downstream engine crates (UTSUSHI-146 utsushi-reallive) imitate.

Deliverables:
- Inspectable impl on the fixture adapter exposing tick + frame + asset id + metadata
- Restorable impl on the fixture adapter consuming the same paths and declaring metadata.* ignored_by_design
- Integration test crates/utsushi-fixture/tests/snapshot_roundtrip.rs asserting take-mutate-restore-retake byte equality
- Documentation note in the fixture crate root pointing at the in-test FixtureInspectable as the design reference
nodes.acceptance:
- Fixture adapter passes a snapshot round-trip test against the production surface (not an in-test FixtureInspectable).
- Existing fixture trace, capture, and smoke_validate goldens continue to pass.
- utsushi-cli runtime registry compiles and runs unchanged.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: moved from alpha to real-game-testing-ready — the old "alpha" framing was renamed; substance unchanged. See docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Fixture inspect/restore implementation matches the substrate's Inspectable/Restorable contract verbatim (no silent best-effort branch).
Round-trip determinism holds against the production fixture surface, not just an in-test stub.
No regression in existing trace, capture, or smoke_validate goldens.

nodes.title: Snapshot replay-tail integration test against ReplayLog
nodes.spec:
Strengthen the round-trip determinism claim by exercising a real ReplayLog through the snapshot/restore/diff substrate, replacing the placeholder observe() helper in tests/snapshot_round_trip.rs with a log-driven flow that asserts observation-sequence equality through the replay infrastructure.

Deliverables:
- Integration test constructing a ReplayLog with at least two ReplayEntry records
- Test driving the fixture from the log, snapshotting at a known tick, mutating, restoring, and replaying the tail
- Byte-equality assertion on the observation sequence collected through the replay infrastructure
nodes.acceptance:
- ReplayLog is constructed and driven explicitly (no ad-hoc observation helper).
- Snapshot restore at tick T followed by tail replay produces an observation sequence equal to the pre-mutation from-zero replay tail.
- Test is hermetic — no host paths, no clock side effects, no external fixtures.
audit_focus: ReplayLog is the actual source of observations (not a stub).
Tail replay starts at the snapshot's anchor tick, not from zero.
Observation sequence equality is byte-level, not field-level.

nodes.title: Direct positive-rejection test for snapshot 16 KiB ceiling
nodes.spec:
Add a direct positive-rejection test for `SnapshotError::SnapshotTooLarge` covering the 16 KiB serialized-snapshot ceiling enforcement path in `Snapshot::validate`. The current test surface only asserts that a clean snapshot stays under the ceiling; the rejection branch itself is not directly exercised (the state-tree ceiling test reaches the same JSON-size-rejection mechanism on a sibling path).

Deliverables:
- Unit test under crates/utsushi-core/src/snapshot/snapshot.rs constructing a Snapshot whose serialized form exceeds SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES
- Assertion that SnapshotError::SnapshotTooLarge is returned with size > ceiling
nodes.acceptance:
- Test exercises the SnapshotError::SnapshotTooLarge rejection branch in Snapshot::validate directly.
- Constant SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES is referenced from the test so a future ceiling change updates both the validator and the test together.
audit_focus: Test asserts the rejection branch, not just that a clean snapshot is under the ceiling.
No clippy / fmt regressions introduced.
Constant SNAPSHOT_MAX_SERIALIZED_BYTES is referenced from the test.

nodes.title: MaxMismatchesPerEvent truncation cap for trace conformance
nodes.spec:
Add an opt-in per-event truncation cap to `TraceCheckOptions` so production engine-port runs against large traces emit bounded mismatch lists without losing the exhaustive default that UTSUSHI-027's alpha-track fixtures rely on.

Deliverables:
- `TraceCheckOptions.max_mismatches_per_event: Option<NonZeroU32>` (default None preserves current behaviour)
- `TraceMismatchKind::TruncatedPerEvent` typed sentinel that carries the suppressed-count detail
- New stable code `utsushi.conformance.trace_per_event_truncated` registered in `codes::ALL`
- Unit + integration tests for the unbounded and bounded paths
nodes.acceptance:
- Setting `max_mismatches_per_event = None` reproduces the current exhaustive emission (regression-tested).
- Setting `max_mismatches_per_event = Some(N)` caps per-event emissions at N and appends exactly one `TruncatedPerEvent` sentinel when suppression occurred.
- The new code is in `codes::ALL` and the mod.rs parity test exhaustively covers it.
audit_focus: Default-preserving behaviour for existing callers
Truncation observability via the typed sentinel kind
Codes-registry parity for the new code

nodes.title: Optional bridge-unit-id normalisation modes for trace conformance
nodes.spec:
Add an opt-in normalisation mode to bridge-unit-id equality so engine adapters that emit stable UUIDs in different surface forms (uppercase vs lowercase hex) do not trip spurious BridgeUnitDivergent mismatches.

Deliverables:
- `BridgeUnitIdNormalisation` enum with `Exact` (default) and at least one folding variant
- Symmetric application in the per-event comparison loop
- Tests covering exact + folding paths
nodes.acceptance:
- Default (`Exact`) reproduces UTSUSHI-027's byte-for-byte behaviour.
- `LowercaseHex` folds both sides before comparison; UUIDv7 inputs with case-only differences match.
- The new option is plumbed through the fixture loader (`TraceFixtureOptions`).
audit_focus: Default preservation
Symmetric normalisation
Wire-shape impact on the fixture JSON (additive only)

nodes.title: Real terminal-bridge-unit-id evidence on branch conformance Pass
nodes.spec:
Replace the UTSUSHI-027 placeholder where branch-conformance Pass evidence cites `branch_id` as a `bridge_unit_id` with a real `terminal_bridge_unit_id` field on `GoldenBranch` / `ObservedBranch`, so ingestion can join discovered branches to their terminal bridge units without inferring identity from the discovery key.

Deliverables:
- `terminal_bridge_unit_id: String` field on both golden and observed branch types
- Updated `build_branch_evidence` to cite the new field
- Fixture corpus updated (additive `terminalBridgeUnitId` on every branch)
- Tests asserting the evidence ref carries the terminal id, not the branch id
nodes.acceptance:
- The new field is required at construction with the shared id-validation rules.
- `BranchCheckResult::Pass.evidence_refs` carries `terminal_bridge_unit_id` values that are distinct from `branch_id` in at least one positive fixture.
- UTSUSHI-030 round-trip parity (when it lands) sees the new field through the JSON wire shape.
audit_focus: Schema impact (additive field, no version bump)
Round-trip parity with UTSUSHI-030 ingestion
Test coverage for the distinct-id case

nodes.title: Typed ConformanceError for inadmissible evidence-refs on trace and branch conformance
nodes.spec:
Replace the four call sites in `trace_branch::{trace,branch}::into_conformance_result` that emit `Err(MalformedSemanticCode { code: TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM })` with a typed `ConformanceError::EvidenceRefNotAdmissibleForProfile` variant whose `semantic_code()` returns the trace-specific overclaim code, so downstream code-based triagers see the diagnostic the plan promised instead of the generic malformed-code bucket.

Deliverables:
- New `ConformanceError::EvidenceRefNotAdmissibleForProfile { profile, evidence_kind }` variant
- `semantic_code()` mapping for the new variant
- All four call sites updated in trace.rs / branch.rs
- Tests covering `.semantic_code()` for the rejection path on both Pass and Fail input to both helpers
nodes.acceptance:
- Calling `TraceCheckResult::Pass { evidence_refs: vec![FrameArtifactRef{..}] }.into_conformance_result(...)` returns an Err whose `.semantic_code()` is `TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM`, not `MALFORMED_SEMANTIC_CODE`.
- The same is true for the branch helper.
- `MalformedSemanticCode` is no longer used as a carrier for evidence-ref rejections; it goes back to signalling input-code-shape failures only.
audit_focus: semantic_code() surface accuracy
Backward-compatibility of MalformedSemanticCode for its original purpose
Symmetry across the four call sites

nodes.title: Persist ingested PatchResultV02 in the itotori project repository
nodes.spec:
Add durable storage for PatchResultV02 records ingested via ProjectWorkflow.ingestPatchResult so failures, output hash, and partialWrite disposition survive across CLI invocations and are joinable from the dashboard and finding ledger.

Deliverables:
- ProjectRepository.savePatchResult (or recordPatchResultArtifact) writing patch_result rows keyed by patchResultId
- ingestPatchResult wired to call the new repository method in the same actor transaction as recordFinding
- Dashboard read-model surface exposing patchResult status + failureCategories
nodes.acceptance:
- After ingestPatchResult, the patch result row is queryable from the DB by patchResultId with status, outputHash, and failureCategories.
- retained_partial finding rows reference a patch_result row that already exists in the same transaction.
- Dashboard exposes patchResult ingestion state by patchResultId.
nodes.status_reason: Re-tier 2026-06-24: moved from alpha to real-game-testing-ready — the old "alpha" framing was renamed; substance unchanged. See docs/proposals/dag-retier-2026-06-24.md.
audit_focus: Silent loss of patch result state between CLI runs
Finding referencing a patch result that was never persisted
Dashboard joins that fail because patch_result rows are missing

nodes.title: Hello-game v0.2 patch-result fixture regenerator
nodes.spec:
Replace the hand-rolled deterministic hello-game v0.2 patch-result fixture with a regenerator and CI check that also cascades hashes through the alpha-vertical-proof and public manifest.

Deliverables:
- scripts/regenerate-hello-game-patch-result-v0.2.mjs (or a just-target) that writes the expected/patch-result-v0.2.fr-FR.json from bridge + byte-size table
- Hash cascade through hello-game-alpha-proof-v0.2.fr-FR.json and hello-game.manifest.json
- CI check that the committed fixture matches the regenerator output
nodes.acceptance:
- Running the regenerator with no source change is a no-op (idempotent).
- A bridge-v0.2.json edit that adds, removes, or rehashes an asset triggers a deterministic update to all three downstream files.
- CI fails if any committed file drifts from the regenerator output.
audit_focus: Hand-edited fixture drift between expected, alpha-vertical-proof, and public manifest
Asset count changes that escape the dedup-by-assetId rule
Output hash rollup hand-calculations that go stale

nodes.title: Engine-neutralize UTSUSHI-024 embed diagnostics test fixture
nodes.spec:
Replace the engine-evocative `"reallive"` string in the `EmbedError::SnapshotAdapterIdMismatch` test fixture at `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/diagnostics.rs:224` with an engine-neutral string so the audit-focus grep `'xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker|reallive|siglus'` returns empty across the whole embed module.

Deliverables:
- One-line edit replacing `"reallive"` in the diagnostics test fixture with `"other-adapter"` (or equivalent engine-neutral string).
- Confirmation grep run in the audit report verifying zero hits across `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/`.
nodes.acceptance:
- The audit-focus grep returns zero hits across `crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/`.
- `cargo test -p utsushi-core embed::diagnostics` continues to pass.
audit_focus: Embed module must not reference any engine subproject by name, even in test data.
Audit-focus grep posture stays strict end-to-end.

nodes.title: Distinguish stage-time vs promote-time terminal states in PatchTransaction
nodes.spec:
Add a distinct `TransactionState` variant (e.g. `StageFailed`) so callers inspecting `outcome.final_state` can distinguish a stage-time write failure (no rename ever attempted) from a promote-time rename failure (rename failed after verify), without having to re-parse the v0.2 PatchResult diagnostic list.

Deliverables:
- New `TransactionState::StageFailed` variant added to the enum.
- `record_stage_failure` updated to use the new variant.
- `is_terminal` and v0.2 mappers updated to treat the new variant identically to `PromoteFailed` for wire-form compatibility.
- Regression test asserting stage-write failure terminates in the new state.
nodes.acceptance:
- Stage-time write failures terminate in a distinct state from promote-time rename failures.
- Existing v0.2 PatchResult wire form is preserved (the new state maps to the same v0.2 category as before).
- Existing `cargo test -p kaifuu-core` continues to pass.
audit_focus: New variant must not change the v0.2 wire form (no schema bump).
Existing kaifuu-reallive consumers continue to compile and pass.
Stage-time vs promote-time distinction observable from `outcome.final_state` alone.

nodes.title: Character relationship agent end-to-end coverage: recorded replay + DB round-trip + CLI snapshot
nodes.spec:
Close the three end-to-end gaps deferred by ITOTORI-014: a recorded-provider fixture replay test that exercises generateCharacterRelationships against a sealed provider trace; a DB-backed persistence round-trip test (gated on DATABASE_URL) that applies migration 0031 and round-trips bios+relationships through the repository; and a CLI snapshot smoke that pins the generate-character-relationships / check-character-relationships handler text shape.

Deliverables:
- apps/itotori/test/character-relationship-agent-recorded.test.ts exercising a committed recorded provider trace.
- apps/itotori/test/character-relationship-persistence-round-trip.test.ts gated on DATABASE_URL.
- apps/itotori/test/character-relationship-cli-snapshot.test.ts pinning CLI text shape against a committed snapshot.
nodes.acceptance:
- Recorded-provider replay asserts the bios+relationships pack matches a committed snapshot byte-for-byte.
- DB round-trip applies migration 0031, persists a Fresh bio+relationship with non-empty citations, loads it back, and asserts citation arrays + source hashes are equal.
- CLI snapshot asserts the human-readable output of generate-character-relationships and check-character-relationships matches committed snapshots; the test must use FakeModelProvider (no live LLM call) and must not require DATABASE_URL.
audit_focus: Recorded fixture diverges from agent's expected pack shape.
Migration 0031 schema drift caught only at live INSERT, not in CI.
CLI text-shape regression surfaces only in operator review.

nodes.title: Wire ingest-conformance CLI to itotori_artifacts insertion
nodes.spec:
Close the gap between the CLI's file-path input and the conformance repository's FK requirement: ingest the report and manifest files into `itotori_artifacts` (with `kind = 'conformance_report'` / `'conformance_manifest'`) and pass the resulting IDs through to the workflow.

Deliverables:
- CLI handler resolves report/manifest paths to artifact rows before calling `ingestConformanceReport`.
- A DB integration test that drives the CLI handler end-to-end against `just db-up` and asserts the FK joins succeed.
nodes.acceptance:
- Running `itotori ingest-conformance --project ... --report-file ...` against a fresh migrated DB persists a row whose `report_artifact_id` references an existing `itotori_artifacts` row.
- Removing the artifact insertion path causes the integration test to fail.

verification.value: just db-up && just db-migrate && pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db

audit_focus: FK integrity between conformance rows and itotori_artifacts.
Idempotency on rerun (same report file should not double-insert artifact rows).
