nodes.title: Thread per-pair capability sheet into provider.descriptor so agents see 'supported' not 'untested'
nodes.spec: DEV_PAIR (deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash, fireworks) declares structuredOutputs.jsonSchema='supported' in apps/itotori/src/providers/dev-pair.ts:108-157 and OpenRouterModelProvider registers those sheets into globalCapabilityGuard at openrouter.ts:1650-1653. BUT the speaker-label agent (and presumably other agents) reads provider.descriptor.capabilities directly at agent.ts:208-218; that descriptor falls back to openRouterDefaultCapabilities (openrouter.ts:391-423) where jsonSchema='untested', and the agent refuses with 'provider openrouter does not support structured output'. Result: every live OR invocation via the alpha closer refuses pre-flight, BEFORE any HTTP fires. This is the actual gate blocking UTSUSHI-231 — the prior 429 and pair_mismatch errors were from older builds that didn't have this pre-flight check. Fix: thread the per-pair capability sheet into the request-scoped descriptor that agents see. Two paths: (a) OpenRouterModelProvider.invoke does getModelCapabilities({modelId, providerId}) per request and synthesizes a descriptor override; (b) the speaker-label agent (and any other agent reading descriptor.capabilities) instead consults globalCapabilityGuard for the (modelId, providerId) pair. Pick (a) for blast-radius minimization (changes one site instead of N agents). DO NOT change openRouterDefaultCapabilities to 'supported' (those defaults must stay safe for unknown pairs).
nodes.acceptance: Live OR call against DEV_PAIR succeeds (passes speaker-label pre-flight) because provider.descriptor.capabilities.structuredOutputs.jsonSchema returns 'supported' for the pair. Unknown-pair behavior is unchanged: a request against (some/random, model) still sees 'untested' in descriptor.capabilities. New test mocks the per-request descriptor synthesis. UTSUSHI-231 retry passes phase 2 (agentic-loop fires).

nodes.title: M.1 'Reusable' anchors cite substrate types not consumed by the inline Siglus scaffold
nodes.spec: docs/research/reallive-engine.md:910-948 §M.1 table names SnapshotStore, TextSurfaceSink, AudioEventSink, FrameArtifactSink, Inspectable, Restorable, ReplayLog, LogicalClockTick, StateTree, StatePath, ChoiceIndex as substrate-facade carriers proving reusability. crates/utsushi-reallive/tests/cross_engine_facade_only_imports.rs:62-67 inline Siglus scaffold imports only the bare scaffold-contract subset (no SnapshotStore, no TextSurfaceSink, no Inspectable/Restorable, no ReplayLog, no StateTree/StatePath, no ChoiceIndex). The audit-prompt criterion 'verify each reusable anchor in Appendix M.1 cites a concrete substrate type the inline Siglus scaffold imports' is therefore strictly satisfied only for: AssetPackage, EnginePort, PortManifest, REQUIRED_LIFECYCLE_STAGES, SinkSet, EvidenceTier, FidelityTier, LifecycleStage, PortCapability. Substrate facade DOES export the deeper anchors (verified at crates/utsushi-core/src/substrate.rs:28-48), so the reusability claim holds at the substrate-API surface, but it is proven by RealLive's consumption + facade existence rather than by inline-Siglus consumption. The §M.4 'scaffold-contract level' caveat and the test's SUBSET-not-EQUALS posture (cross_engine_facade_only_imports.rs:580-594) disclose this stance honestly.

nodes.title: Inline Siglus scaffold is inert by design — 'second engine family' is contract-level only
nodes.spec: crates/utsushi-reallive/tests/cross_engine_facade_only_imports.rs:80-87, 162-166: scaffold is single-file, lifecycle methods all return EnginePortError::Lifecycle{Unimplemented}, MANIFEST.limitations explicitly says 'every lifecycle method returns a typed Lifecycle error' and 'Real Siglus VM behaviour is out of alpha scope'. Per feedback_multi_game_validation.md, substrate work means ≥2 engine families with ≥2 real games per family. The inline scaffold satisfies the ≥2-families requirement only at the scaffold-contract level; no Siglus real-bytes evidence is exercised. The spec explicitly acknowledges this is intentional ('the alpha tier targets a single engine family, RealLive against Sweetie HD; the full Siglus VM stays research-only') and the substrate-API extensibility claim is the load-bearing one for UTSUSHI-221.

nodes.title: apply runtime guard: refuse delta packages whose source extract had partial:true
nodes.spec: Surfaced as KAIFUU-193 audit P1: PartialAdapterReport schema (KAIFUU-193) emits partial:true on the envelope; documentation says 'apply MUST refuse any envelope with partial:true', BUT apply_delta + the CLI apply arm never inspect partial provenance. The natural enforcement point is at extract-time provenance carried THROUGH the delta package: the delta has to carry forward a partial:true bit (or richer provenance) from the originating extract envelope. Then apply reads it and refuses.
nodes.acceptance: kaifuu-cli apply against a delta package whose source extract had partial:true exits non-zero with typed PartialSourceRefused error; existing apply tests still pass; KAIFUU-193's documented contract is now enforced not just asserted.

PartialAdapterReport (crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs:13388-13402) has no `status` field; the envelope encodes partial-ness via the boolean `partial: true` plus `detected: false` (test assertions match this at crates/kaifuu-cli/tests/partial_extract.rs:225-227). Functionally equivalent for dashboard ingestion but literal acceptance language is unmet.

nodes.title: Adopt qdcli as the sole orchestration surface
nodes.spec:
Restore the roadmap gate after qdcli adoption made roadmap/spec-dag.json a qd export. The repo must have one declared canonical roadmap shape, qd check/CI must run the same roadmap validation that just check trusts, and legacy spec-dag validator entry points must not reject the committed qd export.

Deliverables:
- scripts/spec-dag.mjs qd export validation path for roadmap/spec-dag.json
- justfile and .qd/config.toml gate wiring that includes just roadmap-validate through just check/just ci
- docs/spec-dag.md, docs/orchestration-operating-model.md, and docs/worktree-lifecycle.md updates naming qd export as canonical
nodes.acceptance:
- just roadmap-validate passes from the spec/itotori-300 worktree
- just check passes, or any residual failure is unrelated to roadmap validation and recorded with exact output
- qd check_command and ci_command invoke the canonical repo gate instead of duplicating a validator-free command list
- ITOTORI-300 no longer contains placeholder spec, acceptance, verification, or audit-focus text

nodes.title: Spec acceptance text writes Shift-JIS title trailing space as \u{8140} but implementation/test use \u{3000}
nodes.spec: Spec acceptance criterion writes `"オシオキSweetie＋Sweets!! HD Edition\u{8140}"`. The literal codepoint U+8140 is a Han ideograph (腀), which would be wrong. The two bytes `81 40` are the Shift-JIS encoding of U+3000 IDEOGRAPHIC SPACE; the implementation correctly decodes via encoding_rs::SHIFT_JIS to `\u{3000}` (src/save.rs:411-413 doc comment + tests/save_real_sweetie_hd.rs:72 + tests/save_synthetic.rs:57). The implementation is correct; the spec text is wrong/misleading. The acceptance assertion as literally worded would fail, but the spirit (round-trip of the Shift-JIS title) is met. Recommend amending the spec text rather than the code.

nodes.title: Doc claims writable-path KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH override is rejected, but no runtime check exists

nodes.title: EvidenceTier import in test bypasses the substrate facade
nodes.spec: tests/save_synthetic.rs:25 uses `use utsushi_core::EvidenceTier;` (root path) rather than `utsushi_core::substrate::EvidenceTier`. EvidenceTier is re-exported via the substrate facade (utsushi-core/src/substrate.rs:93) so the facade-discipline rule is satisfied logically but the import-site grep would flag it. lib.rs documents a known exception for CaptureOutcome with a written justification; EvidenceTier here has no such justification.

nodes.title: GlobalSave / ReadFlags do not validate file size against any cross-check (by design)
nodes.spec: src/save.rs:366-380 GlobalSave::decode and 423-440 ReadFlags::decode skip the leading_u32-vs-bytes.len() cross-check. The doc-comment (src/save.rs:355-358) explains that leading_u32 for these formats is a per-format constant (0xA4 / 0x98) not a file size, so the SystemSave check would not apply. This is correct behaviour, but means the audit-focus 'silently truncating slots' protection covers only SystemSave; GlobalSave/ReadFlags rely on the magic-string termination check for slot-end safety. A truncation that severs the payload but leaves the null-terminated magic intact would decode silently. Recommend documenting this asymmetry in the audit-focus pin.
nodes.acceptance:
- Resolve promoted P3 audit finding 1ae48816-0ddf-4d71-8dbb-e3ed09d9ffe1 from UTSUSHI-218: GlobalSave / ReadFlags do not validate file size against any cross-check (by design)

nodes.title: supportBoundary text always emitted as [REDACTED] in the proof report due to '/' tokens tripping local-absolute-path heuristic
nodes.spec: XP3_PROFILE_PROOF_SUPPORT_BOUNDARY (crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs:5419) contains the substring ' / extract / patch_back'. When passed through redact_for_log_or_report() the slash tokens (' /', '/ ') are picked up by text_contains_local_absolute_path() -> is_local_absolute_path() (starts_with('/')), causing the entire boundary string to be replaced with '[REDACTED:kaifuu.secret_redacted]'. Reproduced by running `kaifuu xp3 profile-proof` against fixtures/kaifuu/kirikiri/xp3-profile.json: supportBoundary in the resulting proof.json is `[REDACTED:kaifuu.secret_redacted]`. This silently strips public-safe boundary text from every proof and weakens the spec acceptance criterion 'The proof report includes ... semantic remediation' — consumers cannot read the boundary. Suggested fix: rewrite the constant without lone '/' tokens (e.g. 'detect, extract, patch_back'), or run support_boundary through a non-redacting copy.

nodes.title: xp3.patch_capability.overclaim message and xp3.crypt_profile.missing message are redacted, hiding the human-readable failure reason from the report
nodes.spec: Diagnostic messages such as 'fixture declared patch_back but only plain XP3 may claim extract or patch_back' (lib.rs:5985) and 'encrypted XP3 fixtures must declare a crypt profile' (lib.rs:6107) survive the redactor for synthetic fixtures, but for real-bytes runs the same messages emerge as '[REDACTED:kaifuu.secret_redacted]' in the final report (see /scratch real-bytes Bukkake Ranch proof). The cause is the same slash-tokenisation heuristic firing on words like 'patch_back' adjacent to '/' tokens that appear earlier in the input via redact_for_log_or_report. Net effect: the proof communicates only the diagnostic code and remediation; the message field is opaque. Not a leak risk, but an information-quality issue that hides the routing reason from consumers.

nodes.title: Pair-policy v0.3: enumerate evidence-validated alternate providers for deepseek-v4-flash with explicit 429-keyed failover predicate
nodes.spec: 3 of 6 UTSUSHI-231 alpha-validation rerun attempts have been blocked by Fireworks HTTP 429 on (deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash, fireworks). This is structural, not transient — Fireworks has insufficient marketplace quota for that pair to support our alpha rerun cadence. Per (modelId, providerId) pair rule: providers are NOT auto-fallback. We must enumerate acceptable alternate ZDR-allowed providers that host deepseek-v4-flash, evidence-validate each via ITOTORI-224-style toy call, then define an EXPLICIT failover predicate keyed on 429 from primary. Pair-policy schema v0.2 → v0.3: add alternateProviders[] array of explicit pairs (each with its own capability sheet) + failoverPredicate (e.g. http_429_from_primary). Driver retries up to N alternates in order before raising AlphaRerunBlockedExternal. No auto-broadening; every alternate is explicitly enumerated.
nodes.acceptance: presets/localize-sweetie-hd.pair-policy.json schemaVersion=itotori.pair-policy.v0.3. Top-level alternateProviders[] enumerates ≥1 evidence-validated alternate for deepseek-v4-flash. localize-sweetie-hd-stage-command driver attempts primary; on 429 retries each alternate in order. UTSUSHI-231 retry passes phase 2 (agentic-loop fires past first OR request) at least once.

nodes.title: Closed taxonomy collapses queue.read denial into permission_denied_manage status
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/batch-preview.ts:381 buildDeniedPreview sets status=permissionDeniedManage with requiredPermission="queue.read" and diagnostic code reviewer_batch_permission_denied_read. The closed status enum (reviewerBatchPreviewStatusValues, lines 89-98) lacks a permissionDeniedRead member; the single permissionDeniedManage value is overloaded to mean two distinct denials (read vs. manage). Downstream consumers cannot distinguish a read-gate refusal from a manage-gate refusal by status alone, weakening the closed-taxonomy guarantee for per-item diagnostics.

nodes.title: Atomicity claim weaker than "no partial writes": per-item dispatch loop can leave items 1..N-1 applied if item N races
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/batch-execute.ts:208-239 dispatches one action per selection in a sequential loop. Each per-item dispatch is itself a single-row DB transaction, but inter-item atomicity is NOT guaranteed: if item k throws ReviewerQueueRepositoryError mid-loop (e.g., concurrent move, repository-level refusal), items 0..k-1 are already persisted. apps/itotori/test/reviewer-batch-execute.test.ts:210-256 explicitly asserts this behavior (result.applied[0]?.kind === "applied" alongside result.applied[1] refused). The header comment (batch-execute.ts:1-19) admits this and frames it as acceptable, but the focus item demands "fully apply OR fully rollback" — there is no rollback path for already-written transitions on a mid-loop refusal.

nodes.title: Bake max_connections=400 into db-compose-env to prevent pg pool exhaustion under parallel worktree CI
nodes.spec: Shared postgres container itotori-itotori_postgres_1 ships with default max_connections=100. Workers running just ci in parallel across worktrees each spin ~10-15 vitest forks, each with its own pg pool. 3 parallel worktrees easily overflow → FATAL 53300 too many clients → workers spin retrying. Patched live to 400 on 2026-06-26 ~18:08Z but the value will reset on next container recreate. Fix: update scripts/itotori-db-compose-env.mjs (or wherever the pg service config lives) to set max_connections via POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS or docker-compose command override. Also raise shared_buffers proportionally.

nodes.title: Style-guide live smoke requires OpenRouter ZDR account assertion
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/style-guide-provider-smoke.ts currently permits an opted-in live OpenRouter style-guide smoke with ITOTORI_STYLE_GUIDE_LIVE_PROVIDER_SMOKE=1 and OPENROUTER_API_KEY, then constructs OpenRouterProvider directly. Route this smoke through OpenRouterModelProvider or call assertOpenRouterZdrAccount(env) before constructing OpenRouterProvider.

A regression test proves no fetch is attempted without the account assertion.

nodes.title: Live alpha path must persist provider-run artifacts
nodes.spec: Broad audit 2026-06-27 found that OpenRouterProvider.invoke requires live opt-in with a provider-run artifact recorder, but OpenRouterModelProvider makes artifactRecorder optional and defaults to NoopArtifactRecorder. The localize-sweetie-hd live factory constructs OpenRouterModelProvider with only costCapUsd, so successful live alpha runs can omit provider-run artifacts carrying routing posture and usageResponseJson evidence. Evidence: apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts around OpenRouterProvider live recorder requirement and NoopArtifactRecorder fallback; apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/localize-sweetie-hd-stage-command.ts constructs the live provider without a persistent recorder; packages/localization-bridge-schema/src/agentic-loop-bundle.ts keeps summary telemetry/providerProofId but not the full provider-run artifact.
nodes.acceptance: Live localize-sweetie-hd/OpenRouterModelProvider path either wires a persistent ProviderRunArtifactRecorder under the run artifact directory or refuses live construction without one. Regression tests assert each live invocation writes provider-run artifacts containing routingPosture and usageResponseJson, without raw prompt/response or secret leakage.

nodes.acceptance: ci-kaifuu and ci-utsushi cover all matching workspace members or affected.mjs emits crate-specific cargo commands; a verifier parses Cargo.toml workspace members and fails if any crates/kaifuu-* or crates/utsushi-* member is uncovered; affected tests prove kaifuu-reallive, kaifuu-vault-source, utsushi-reallive, and utsushi-siglus changes are gated.

nodes.title: Canonicalize audit-dispose roadmap exports
nodes.spec: Ensure the qd wrapper audit-dispose flow runs the same roadmap/spec-dag.json canonicalization and validation path used by normal qd export interception.
nodes.acceptance: Audit-dispose export invokes canonicalizeSpecDagExport or equivalent after writing roadmap/spec-dag.json; dispose-flow regression tests cover canonical ordering/formatting; node scripts/spec-dag.mjs validate and vp check on roadmap/spec-dag.json pass.

nodes.title: Count public fixture evidence in opportunity readiness
nodes.spec: Fix catalog opportunity runtime readiness aggregation so public_fixture adapter-matrix and key-validation evidence contributes to publicFixtureEvidenceCount and public_fixture readiness instead of being rejected by the private-local-only predicate.
nodes.acceptance: Public-only and mixed public/private opportunity fixtures increment publicFixtureEvidenceCount; readiness explanations include public_fixture when public evidence exists; private-local aggregate behavior is unchanged; catalog opportunity read-model tests cover the split predicates.

nodes.title: Make compose DB defaults safe for parallel worktrees
nodes.spec: Avoid fixed-port and shared-database hazards when multiple worktrees run just db-up/db-reset concurrently. Compose project names are already per-worktree; host ports and DATABASE_URL defaults must also be unique or fail loud.
nodes.acceptance: Two distinct worktree basenames produce distinct default Postgres host ports or require explicit DATABASE_URL; db-up in one worktree cannot cause another worktree's db-reset to truncate its DB; compose-config tests cover distinct worktree identities and fixed-port override behavior.

nodes.title: probe
nodes.spec: probe
nodes.acceptance: probe

nodes.title: Strengthen localize driver provider artifact validation beyond count-only
nodes.spec:
Final re-audit: suite/scripts/localize-sweetie-hd/run.mjs still counts provider-runs/*/provider-run.json and only checks artifact count >= invocation count after the stage. The offline verifier now performs strong ID/content validation, but run.mjs itself can still miss stale, duplicate, mismatched, fake/local, non-ZDR, or non-billed artifacts.

Add a future suite-hardening slice that shares provider-run validation with verify-artifacts.mjs or replaces the count-only check in run.mjs with equivalent ID/content validation.
nodes.acceptance: Driver-side provider artifact validation either calls the same strong verifier logic after telemetry exists or validates providerProofId/runId, live OpenRouter provenance, model/provider pairs, ZDR routing posture, and billed successful live costs instead of only count >= invocation count.
nodes.status_reason:
Promoted from finding e9815e5e-321f-48ee-8110-c7a41b546f54 on node UTSUSHI-231.
[2026-06-28T05:32:10.386Z] BROAD AUDIT 2026-06-28: also cover tracked ignored provider artifact hygiene. Quality audit found artifacts/openrouter-live-smoke/2026-06-25.json is tracked despite artifacts/ being ignored and contains a captured OpenRouter request/response envelope. Acceptance should include removing or sanitizing tracked live-provider artifacts, documenting any committed artifact allowlist, and adding a check that fails on tracked ignored live/provider artifacts.

nodes.title: Make Sweetie HD localize workflow self-proving with telemetry and verifier outputs
nodes.spec:
Final re-audit: just localize-sweetie-hd still only builds and runs suite/scripts/localize-sweetie-hd/run.mjs. The main recipe does not automatically run telemetry-summary or verify-artifacts.mjs, so a successful live workflow can still omit telemetry-summary.json and post-run-verification.json unless the orchestrator manually runs the post-run commands.

Add a future suite workflow slice that either extends just localize-sweetie-hd or adds a companion recipe to generate telemetry summary and run verify-artifacts.mjs against the latest run directory.
nodes.acceptance: The Sweetie HD workflow exposes an explicit post-run recipe or self-proving command path that captures telemetry-summary.json and post-run-verification.json for the run, or prints durable exact post-run commands with run start/end/project metadata.
nodes.status_reason:
Promoted from finding a044add0-dcaa-4ead-97f1-4fec418fac39 on node UTSUSHI-231.

Next action should rewrite this node to generic localize-project post-run automation or cancel it as superseded by UTSUSHI-231; do not implement old localize-sweetie-hd wording.

The generic localize-project workflow should capture telemetry-summary.json and post-run-verification.json under artifacts/localize-project/RUN_DIR, or print exact post-run commands with run start/end/project metadata. Live runs may load scoped local env/private inputs subject to no raw secret/private artifacting and provider/model/budget/ZDR controls.

nodes.title: Run localize-project suite tests in CI and affected gates
nodes.spec: Wire suite/scripts/localize-project node tests into the standard check or test surface, and route changes under suite/scripts/localize-project through an affected gate that runs those tests. Today full CI and affected suite changes can miss this validation.
nodes.acceptance: A change to suite/scripts/localize-project/run.mjs or verify-artifacts.mjs causes the affected gate to run the localize-project node tests; full CI runs the same tests; the gate fails on a seeded localize-project test failure.

nodes.title: Normalize alpha qd verification commands
nodes.spec: Refresh alpha node verification commands so they name existing recipes and executable tasks. Replace stale localize-sweetie-hd and openrouter-live-smoke task references, and make ignored real-byte tests use explicit include-ignored command forms.
nodes.acceptance: Every alpha verification command referenced by qd exists in this repo; real-byte Rust verification commands include the exact ignored-test invocation needed to run them; node validation rejects stale alpha command references.

nodes.title: Make local qd CI own DB lifecycle safely across worktrees
nodes.spec: Add a local qd/full-CI wrapper that owns db-up, db-wait, and db-down, and avoids fixed-port collisions between parallel worktrees. The GitHub workflow manages compose, but qd ci currently runs just ci directly and can collide on the default database port.
nodes.acceptance: Running qd CI from two worktrees either derives distinct database ports or fails before starting with a clear diagnostic; the local full-CI command starts and stops its own database stack; failure leaves no orphaned compose stack.

nodes.title: Retire legacy roadmap ready and pop read surfaces
nodes.spec: Remove or qd-delegate leftover roadmap-ready, roadmap-pop, roadmap:ready, and spec-dag ready/pop surfaces that survived the qd migration. Keep validator and issue-sync exceptions, but stop teaching operators to inspect or choose work through the legacy roadmap commands.
nodes.acceptance: Searching README, docs, justfile, and package scripts for roadmap-ready, roadmap-pop, roadmap:ready, spec-dag.mjs ready, and spec-dag.mjs pop returns no legacy operational surfaces except explicit qd-delegating replacements; just check remains green.

verification.value: rg -n 'roadmap-ready|roadmap-pop|roadmap:ready|spec-dag.mjs ready|spec-dag.mjs pop' README.md docs justfile package.json

nodes.title: Make qd import rebuild transactional and config-preserving
nodes.spec: Replace the destructive qd-import recipe that unlinks live .qd database files with a staged rebuild flow. The flow must validate import before swapping state and preserve committed qd config, including nix-wrapped check and CI commands.
nodes.acceptance: The qd import rebuild path never removes the live database before a validated replacement exists; failed rebuild leaves the prior database usable; qd config after rebuild matches committed .qd/config.toml for check and CI commands; read-only audit hydration is documented.
nodes.status_reason:
[2026-06-28T05:32:23.293Z] BROAD AUDIT 2026-06-28: confirmed still active. .qd/config.toml uses nix develop --command bash -lc 'just check' and 'just qd-full-ci', but just qd-import still sets plain 'just check'/'just qd-full-ci'. This can recreate qd check drift after a rebuild; keep config preservation and tests in acceptance.
[2026-06-28T08:21:37.826Z] BROAD AUDIT 2026-06-28 safety/privacy wave: fresh audit worktree had zero-byte .qd/qd.db and qd --root status/node show failed with 'no such table: nodes'. Current just qd-import path removes live .qd DB files before validated import, and qd-wrapper fallback refuses roadmap/spec-dag.json when an unusable .qd/qd.db merely exists. Keep this node active as the repair for read-only worktree qd hydration and transactional state rebuild.
[2026-06-28T09:40:24.192Z] BROAD AUDIT 2026-06-28 dashboard/benchmark wave: confirmed still active. just qd-import removes live .qd DB files before validation, can recreate plain just check/just qd-full-ci config instead of committed nix-wrapped commands, and wrapper fallback refuses roadmap hydration when an unusable .qd/qd.db exists. Add corrupt/zero-byte .qd/qd.db recovery and config preservation coverage to acceptance.

nodes.title: Export CaptureOutcome through the substrate facade
nodes.spec: Re-export CaptureOutcome through utsushi_core::substrate and update RealLive, Siglus, and relevant tests to import it through the facade instead of the crate root. This closes a facade reach-around that contradicts the stable substrate import boundary.
nodes.acceptance: No RealLive or Siglus production code imports CaptureOutcome from utsushi_core crate root; facade conformance tests cover CaptureOutcome availability through utsushi_core::substrate; cargo test for utsushi-core, utsushi-reallive, and utsushi-siglus passes.

nodes.title: Route utsushi replay commands through the engine registry
nodes.spec: Move utsushi-cli replay and replay-validate behind an engine-port or runtime-adapter registry capability instead of direct utsushi_reallive imports and reallive-only branches. Unsupported engines should fail through registry capability diagnostics.
nodes.acceptance: Adding a second replay-capable engine does not require editing a reallive-only branch in utsushi-cli replay code; replay and replay-validate use registry-dispatched capability selection; unsupported engines emit structured registry diagnostics; utsushi-cli tests pass.

nodes.title: Validate RealLive protected-span extraction on a second corpus
nodes.spec: Keep Sweetie HD support, but require a second RealLive corpus pass before claiming RealLive-general protected-span coverage. The bridge protected-span scanner should document which rules are RealLive documented and which are title-calibrated.
nodes.acceptance: Protected-span extraction has a second RealLive fixture or documented corpus result; Sweetie-specific vocabulary is isolated as alpha-target data or explicitly documented as title-calibrated; KAIFUU bridge tests show no regression for Sweetie HD scene 1.

nodes.title: Make Sweetie alpha presets explicit target data
nodes.spec: Keep Sweetie HD as the alpha validation target, but stop making Sweetie-specific metadata look like the implicit generic localize-project default. The generic runner should require explicit project metadata and pair policy or use neutral examples.
nodes.acceptance: Sweetie-specific project metadata remains available as allowlisted alpha target data; generic localize-project docs and defaults do not imply Sweetie HD is the normal project shape; non-Sweetie metadata tests continue to pass.

nodes.title: Triage QD wishlist repo actions into qd nodes
nodes.spec: Convert repo-actionable QD-WISHLIST wrapper-removal prerequisites into qd nodes, or mark each item as upstream-only or non-repo work. This keeps qd as the operational backlog instead of leaving process work only in the wishlist document.
nodes.acceptance: Every item in the QD-WISHLIST minimum wrapper-removal set has a qd node id, an upstream-only disposition, or a non-repo disposition; the document links those dispositions; qd status remains valid after export.

nodes.title: Reject nested target symlinks before RealLive patch writes
nodes.spec: Harden the localize-project and kaifuu-cli RealLive patch path so an existing TARGET tree cannot contain symlink entries or symlink components that redirect copy/write operations outside TARGET. The readonly-source hash check remains defense-in-depth after preflight, not the primary protection.
nodes.acceptance: kaifuu-cli patch rejects TARGET/REALLIVEDATA or any existing target-side path component that is a symlink before copy/write; suite localize-project rejects the same class before invoking patch; regression tests cover nested TARGET symlink to SOURCE/REALLIVEDATA and to another writable directory; diagnostics remain redacted; existing target-root symlink and source alias/nesting tests still pass.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T08:23:10.071Z] Source finding 9b1f09d2-2efa-45e8-b595-e9eedcb9abae from broad safety/privacy audit 2026-06-28: nested TARGET symlinks can redirect RealLive patch writes outside TARGET before source-hash detection.

nodes.title: Reject non-string expressions in permission SQL constraint
nodes.spec: Make the permission migration drift verifier enforce a narrow SQL IN-list grammar so the named grants-table permission check cannot include arbitrary expressions in addition to the expected string literals.
nodes.acceptance: Verifier rejects a registered migration where permission in (...) contains the bare permission expression or any other non-string token after expected literals; verifier still accepts the canonical registered constraint; block-comment and line-comment regressions still pass; diagnostics identify the source migration and invalid list content without dumping unrelated SQL.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T08:23:19.819Z] Source finding 6d4098f7-346d-4ec6-8bce-68f1cbc6ed4e from broad safety/privacy audit 2026-06-28: verifier accepts expected quoted permissions plus bare SQL expression permission in the IN list.

nodes.title: Count public fixture runtime evidence in catalog readiness
nodes.spec: Fix catalog opportunity runtime evidence aggregation so valid public_fixture evidence contributes to publicFixtureEvidenceCount and mixed public/private evidence reports the correct readiness source mix.
nodes.acceptance: DB-backed catalog opportunity tests cover public-only runtime evidence, private-only aggregate evidence, and mixed public/private evidence; publicFixtureEvidenceCount increments for valid public_fixture readiness rows; privateLocalAggregateEvidenceCount remains correct; opportunity evidence refs and readiness scoring reflect the corrected counts.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T08:23:29.750Z] Source finding 3e44d5fc-cbf6-4cd5-8319-63e787df657e from broad safety/privacy audit 2026-06-28: public_fixture runtime readiness evidence is filtered out before counting, making publicFixtureEvidenceCount unreachable.

nodes.title: Reject extra fields in catalog completeness and conflict API shapes
nodes.spec: Harden public catalog API response validation so completeness and conflict read models reject unknown fields instead of validating permissively and returning the original object with possible private metadata.
nodes.acceptance: Completeness and conflict API schema guards use strict record validation or construct sanitized public objects; negative API fixtures with extra localPath/rawPayload/private metadata fields fail as internal_error before serialization; existing valid completeness/conflict fixtures still pass; benchmark seed and opportunity strictness behavior remains unchanged.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T08:23:38.028Z] Source finding b0916460-228f-4d3f-aaa6-5613e0134b96 from broad safety/privacy audit 2026-06-28: completeness/conflict API guards use permissive records and return original objects, so extra private fields can serialize.

nodes.title: Make qd CI evidence portable across audit worktrees
nodes.spec: Persist qd CI evidence in a form that read-only audit worktrees can inspect after roadmap export, instead of recording only local .qd/logs paths that may not exist outside the main checkout.
nodes.acceptance: Completed qd nodes that reuse an integrated CI run expose durable evidence through an exportable relative artifact, checked-in summary, or external URL; qd export preserves enough evidence metadata for auditors in a fresh worktree; tests or docs cover record-pass reuse semantics and stale/missing log path detection.

nodes.title: Remove qd-full-ci generated compose env files on exit
nodes.spec: Harden scripts/qd-full-ci.mjs so wrapper-owned temporary compose env files under .tmp/itotori-db are removed after CI teardown, while explicit user-supplied ITOTORI_DB_COMPOSE_ENV_PATH overrides are preserved.
nodes.acceptance: qd-full-ci deletes the generated .tmp/itotori-db/qd-full-ci-*.env file on success and failure after docker compose down; explicit external compose env override paths are not deleted; tests cover success, failure, and override preservation; no DATABASE_URL-derived password remains in wrapper-owned .tmp env files after CI exits.

nodes.title: Hash Cargo target dirs by full worktree root
nodes.spec: Refine per-worktree Rust build cache isolation so CARGO_TARGET_DIR includes a short hash of the canonical worktree root, not only the sanitized basename, and document cleanup ownership.
nodes.acceptance: Two worktrees with the same basename under different parents derive distinct CARGO_TARGET_DIR values; main checkout keeps a stable target dir; AGENTS.md documents the hash scheme and safe cleanup command; tests or shellcheckable examples cover same-basename collision prevention.

nodes.title: Hash Cargo target dirs by full worktree root
nodes.spec: Refine per-worktree Rust build cache isolation so CARGO_TARGET_DIR includes a short hash of the canonical worktree root, not only the sanitized basename, and document cleanup ownership.
nodes.acceptance: Two worktrees with the same basename under different parents derive distinct CARGO_TARGET_DIR values; main checkout keeps a stable target dir; AGENTS.md documents the hash scheme and safe cleanup command; tests or shellcheckable examples cover same-basename collision prevention.

nodes.title: Backfill portable qd CI evidence in current export
nodes.spec: Rewrite or supplement existing qd run records that cite local .qd/logs paths so current roadmap/spec-dag.json is auditable from a fresh worktree. Existing reused CI records must use durable external ids, URLs, or checked-in repo-relative evidence instead of local log paths or summaries that paste .qd/logs.
nodes.acceptance: roadmap/spec-dag.json contains no reused/covered-by CI evidence that references .qd/logs or absolute checkout paths; reused CI run records have log_path null, a URL/external id in summary, or a repo-relative checked-in evidence file; add validator/test coverage that fails on non-portable reused CI evidence; qd export and node scripts/spec-dag.mjs validate pass.

nodes.spec: Add evidence-backed provider slug/name matching for every localize-project OpenRouter alternate, including atlas-cloud returning AtlasCloud, without weakening genuine provider swap detection.

nodes.title: Redact provider-run error messages for private inputs
nodes.spec: Stop persisting unsanitized upstream HTTP error text into provider-run artifacts for private_corpus or localize-project private inputs. Persist status/class/retryability and sanitized bounded diagnostics only.

nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T09:44:33.736Z] Broad-audit repair for finding 50702ea8-d222-4fe4-b843-1a8f34f5bb63. The fix should preserve useful operator diagnostics while preventing raw upstream messages from being persisted for private provider invocations.

nodes.title: Recover stale qd audit disposition locks
nodes.spec: Add stale-lock recovery to scripts/qd-wrapper.mjs audit disposition locking, using owner metadata, PID liveness, malformed-owner handling, age/force semantics, while preserving live lock refusal and transactional DB safety.
nodes.acceptance: Tests cover live lock refusal, dead-PID lock recovery, empty/malformed owner recovery, optional force behavior, and no live qd database mutation until staged replacement validates; node scripts/qd-wrapper.test.mjs passes.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T09:45:17.430Z] Broad-audit repair for finding bdec04c0-f159-4f07-99af-8c8f9c9d2d50. Preserve live-lock refusal, but recover clearly stale, malformed, or dead-owner locks so audit disposition cannot wedge future qd work.

nodes.title: Wire reviewer queue dashboard and routes to API-backed data
nodes.spec: Replace fixture/static reviewer route wiring with typed API-backed reviewer queue data. Add real reviewer queue read endpoints/services, surface queue state on the dashboard, and load reviewer detail/batch data through permission-aware API paths.
nodes.acceptance: Dashboard shows real pending/resolved/deferred/escalated/batch-applied queue rows; granted queue.read loads /reviewer-queue/:id evidence through API-backed data; denied read avoids evidence fetch; batch preview consumes dashboard selections and does not start from hard-coded empty selections; app tests and typecheck pass.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T09:44:52.840Z] Broad-audit repair for finding ae2be41d-d70c-4b22-bcbb-36ba9ff9ea62. This should move the integrated reviewer dashboard/detail/batch paths off static fixtures and onto the existing API/read-model path with tests for empty and populated DB-backed states.

nodes.title: Reject unknown benchmark seed finder query params
nodes.spec: Make /api/catalog/benchmark-seeds reject unknown query parameters consistently with adjacent catalog opportunity parsing.
nodes.acceptance: ?typo=1 on /api/catalog/benchmark-seeds returns 400; all documented benchmark seed filters still parse; regression tests cover unknown parameter rejection and existing valid query behavior.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T09:45:00.113Z] Broad-audit repair for finding aa264ce3-a98e-476e-aeea-1a84348a81a6. Match the stricter catalog opportunity query parser behavior so typos do not silently return unfiltered benchmark seed data.

nodes.title: Align benchmark seed API schema with DB read model
nodes.spec: Make benchmark seed API response validation, tests, repository type, and repository output agree on whether runtimeEvidenceReadiness is part of CatalogBenchmarkSeedRow.
nodes.acceptance: Either runtimeEvidenceReadiness is intentionally added to CatalogBenchmarkSeedRow and repository output, or it is removed from benchmark seed API guard/tests; type, repository output, API guard, fixtures, and tests agree on one shape; api handler tests and db typecheck pass.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T09:45:07.298Z] Broad-audit repair for finding e6e45cb1-f680-493b-926c-bfd107ade935. Tighten the public API guard to the DB read model instead of accepting optional fields that no repository returns.

nodes.title: Make localize-project proof artifacts path-portable
nodes.spec: Make suite/scripts/localize-project run-summary.json and post-run-verification.json portable by recording repo-relative or run-relative paths only. Proof artifacts must not expose absolute checkout roots such as /home, /scratch, /Users, or drive-letter paths while still preserving enough context for auditors to locate artifacts inside the run directory.
nodes.acceptance: Tests reject absolute paths in run-summary.json and post-run-verification.json; generated localize-project proof artifacts use relative paths for runDir, artifact, telemetry, and verifier references; current localize-project run and artifact-verifier tests pass.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-28T09:45:12.484Z] Broad-audit repair for finding 939e0c32-5873-47af-967a-b0fd5e130415. Keep proof artifacts useful from any checkout by using paths relative to the run directory or repository root, and add tests that reject absolute private roots.

nodes.title: Scope qd stats and snapshot status to milestone filters
nodes.spec: Fix qd stats --json --milestone <name> and qd snapshot --json --milestone <name> so the status/ready/stats portions are milestone-scoped consistently with criticalPath/eta. The command output must not mix global counts with filtered critical-path data.
nodes.acceptance: Regression tests prove alpha-scoped stats and snapshot output report only alpha nodes/ready counts while unfiltered output remains global; criticalPath/eta stay milestone-scoped; resolve finding 1a333f8d-9994-472d-8127-e0f190fd0c3a.

nodes.title: Tighten benchmark seed runtime evidence schema
nodes.spec: Remove runtimeEvidenceReadiness from the benchmark seed finder API contract unless the repository read model emits it. The public validator and fixtures must match CatalogBenchmarkSeedRow exactly so API tests fail on extra runtimeEvidenceReadiness in benchmark seed rows.
nodes.acceptance: assertCatalogBenchmarkSeedFinderReadModel rejects benchmark seed rows containing runtimeEvidenceReadiness; api-handler tests and fixtures no longer accept or emit that field for /api/catalog/benchmark-seeds; catalog opportunity runtimeEvidenceReadiness remains unchanged; resolve finding e6e45cb1-f680-493b-926c-bfd107ade935.

nodes.title: Fix Itotori SPA fallback routes for reviewer and asset-decision pages
nodes.spec: Extend the production Itotori dashboard fallback route detection so reviewer queue detail, reviewer batch, and asset-decision SPA paths serve the dashboard index instead of 404ing outside the dev server.
nodes.acceptance: Server tests prove /reviewer-queue/batch, /reviewer-queue/:id, and /projects/:projectId/locale-branches/:localeBranchId/asset-decisions[/batch] serve webRoot/index.html; /api/*, runtime routes, static assets, traversal attempts, and unrelated paths keep current behavior.

nodes.title: Wire asset-decisions HTTP API behind the DB service
nodes.spec: Add typed HTTP API routes for asset-decision page reads so the SPA endpoints it fetches are registered through the central API handler and backed by the existing database asset-decision service.
nodes.acceptance: GET asset-decision and candidate-asset endpoints validate project/locale branch path shape, enforce permissions, return schema-checked read models, and have API tests for success, malformed path/query, denied access, and not-found behavior. The asset-decisions page no longer renders only API fetch errors in production.

nodes.title: Wire reviewer batch confirm execution end to end
nodes.spec: Add a queue-manage-gated reviewer batch execution API and browser confirm path so the reviewer batch preview page's Confirm control actually applies through ReviewerBatchActionService instead of stopping at read-only preview.
nodes.acceptance: Preview remains queue.read-only; confirm requires queue.manage; stale revision and denied permission cases fail closed without partial writes; successful confirm mutates rows through ReviewerBatchActionService and renders applied/refused results; API and route/browser tests cover the flow.

nodes.title: Expose requiredCapabilities on benchmark seed finder API
nodes.spec: Add HTTP query support for benchmark seed requiredCapabilities so API callers can use the repository and benchmark-set selector's exact capability filtering instead of only minCapabilityLevel.
nodes.acceptance: The benchmark seed query parser accepts repeated and comma-list requiredCapabilities values, validates capability enums, forwards the array to the repository filter, preserves existing minCapabilityLevel behavior, rejects malformed values, and has API tests for valid/invalid cases.

nodes.title: Make catalog read query validation consistent
nodes.spec: Apply the same unknown-query-parameter rejection used by catalog benchmark seed and opportunity parsers to catalog completeness and conflict read endpoints.
nodes.acceptance: Unknown params on catalog completeness and conflict reads return 400 with specific diagnostics; documented params still parse; tests match benchmark/opportunity query validation behavior.

nodes.title: Complete milestone-scoped qd JSON consistency
nodes.spec: Harden the repo qd wrapper shim for stats/snapshot --json --milestone so all milestone-sensitive JSON fields are derived from one consistent export snapshot or explicitly filtered, rather than only replacing status/ready counts.
nodes.acceptance: Regression tests prove snapshot.openFindings, ready nodes, P0/P1 counts, and status exclude other milestones; stats and snapshot keep criticalPath/eta milestone data; tests cover a single captured snapshot fixture so parallel qd mutations cannot mix moments.

nodes.title: Zero-dimension SCREENSIZE_MOD (e.g. 999,0,720) parses to a degenerate Some(ScreenSize) that silently routes pointer to a collapsed axis instead of emitting MissingScreenSize
nodes.spec: The fix only triggers SyscallDispatchError::MissingScreenSize when self.screen_size is None (syscall.rs:546-560). But parse_screen_size accepts width/height of 0 because u32::try_from(0) succeeds (syscall.rs:703-704), yielding Some(ScreenSize{width:0,...}). A pointer event then takes the success path: pointer_to_pixel returns 0 for the zero axis (syscall.rs:241-250) and route_for_pointer_pixel matches against the degenerate coordinate. So a malformed `#SCREENSIZE_MOD=999,0,720` does NOT emit the diagnostic, even though both the constant doc ('missing or malformed', syscall.rs:87-91) and the error message/doc ('SCREENSIZE_MOD is missing or malformed', syscall.rs:312-318) claim malformed input is covered. Note render_pipeline.rs:622 already has render_pass_rejects_zero_screen_size — the syscall dispatcher has no equivalent zero guard, so the two consumers of ScreenSize disagree on whether a zero dimension is valid. Out of the original finding's scope (which pinned the None / missing case specifically) and not a silent total-disable (the pointer still routes, just to a degenerate axis), hence P3 follow-up rather than in-scope. Wrong-arity and negative SCREENSIZE_MOD values DO return None and are correctly caught; only the zero-dimension case slips the 'malformed' claim.
nodes.acceptance: parse_screen_size in crates/utsushi-reallive/src/syscall.rs treats a zero width or height SCREENSIZE_MOD (e.g. 999,0,720) as malformed — emitting the MissingScreenSize (or an equivalent typed malformed) diagnostic consistent with render_pipeline's zero-screen-size guard — instead of routing pointers to a collapsed (0,0) axis; a test asserts the zero-dimension case yields the diagnostic.
nodes.status_reason: Promoted from finding f5d742b2-8e83-4a52-bb4e-f22efe8368b6 on node missing-screensize-mod-silently-disables-pointer-dispatch-syscal.
audit_focus: Degenerate zero-dimension screen size silently collapsing pointer routing

nodes.title: Batch reviewer surface records importRuntimeFeedback tier verbatim with no persisted-vs-supplied check (parallel recurrence vector)
nodes.spec: The new enforcement lives only in the single ReviewerQueueActionService.importRuntimeFeedback method (action-service.ts:283-296, which calls repository.getItem + assertMatchesPersisted*). The batch surface does NOT route through that method: applyPreparedBatch -> repository.applyActionsAndEnqueueJobs, and the action input for batch is built in batch-execute.ts:423-439 which only does shape checks (assertNonEmptyPayload/assertNonEmptyPayloadArray on evidenceTier/observationEventIds/artifactHashes) and then records the SUPPLIED values onto the transition metadata verbatim. applyActionInTransaction (reviewer-queue-repository.ts:573-648) validates only the state transition + optimistic lock; its UPDATE .set({...}) never compares the metadata evidence against the persisted evidence_tier/observation_event_ids/artifact_hashes columns. So for a runtime_evidence item, a caller using the batch path can still record a different (e.g. higher) tier on the audit-trail transition log than the item actually carries -- exactly the drift the single-method fix prevents. The item row's evidence columns stay correct (immutable, SQL discriminant), but the transition metadata (the audit trail this whole feature exists to protect) can be falsified via batch. This is PRE-EXISTING and out of the audited change's declared scope (the batch method's docstring makes no enforcement claim, so it is not a doc/behavior mismatch and not a regression), but the fix's stated goal is to 'prevent recurrence' and the batch surface is an obvious open recurrence vector. Recommend a DAG follow-up to apply the same persisted-vs-supplied assertion on the batch path (ideally a shared chokepoint, e.g. inside applyActionInTransaction gated on item_kind = runtime_evidence).
nodes.acceptance: The batch reviewer importRuntimeFeedback path (apps/itotori/src/reviewer/batch-execute.ts ~423) enforces the same persisted-vs-supplied evidence comparison for runtime_evidence items as the single-action service path — ideally via a shared chokepoint (e.g. inside applyActionInTransaction or a shared assert helper) so audit-trail transition metadata cannot drift from the persisted tier on either path; a test asserts the batch path rejects a tier mismatch for a runtime_evidence item.

audit_focus: Evidence-tier drift between persisted item and audit-trail metadata on the batch path

nodes.title: importRuntimeFeedback now also requires queue.read (latent permission coupling)
nodes.spec: The added getItem call runs requirePermission(queueRead) (reviewer-queue-repository.ts:514), whereas the action itself requires queueManage (line 481). Permissions are non-hierarchical exact-match grants (authorization.ts:54-73) -- queue.manage does NOT imply queue.read. So importRuntimeFeedback now requires BOTH queue.read and queue.manage, a behavior change from before. Currently harmless: the only grant site is bootstrapLocalUser (authorization.ts:81-86) which grants ALL permissions to local-user, and no code path grants queue.manage without queue.read, so a manage-without-read actor is not reachable. Noting as a latent coupling to be aware of if a read-restricted manage role is ever introduced.
nodes.acceptance: importRuntimeFeedback's permission requirement is made intentional and documented: it either correctly requires queue.read alongside queue.manage (documented because getItem needs read), or the persisted-item read is performed under manage scope so a future read-restricted manage role is not silently blocked from runtime-evidence import; the decision is recorded in code/docs.

nodes.title: Pre-fetch is outside the action transaction; safe only because evidence fields are immutable
nodes.spec: getItem is called before (not inside) the transactional applyActionInTransaction, which re-reads the row independently. This is NOT a real TOCTOU because evidence_tier/observation_event_ids/artifact_hashes are create-only: the only UPDATE path (reviewer-queue-repository.ts:630-648 .set({state, updatedAt, resolvedAt, affectedArtifactIds})) never mutates them, and the SQL discriminant (migrations/0043:item_kind<->evidence non-null) is fixed at create. The only divergence window (item absent at fetch -> enforcement skipped, then present at apply) requires the deterministic review_item_id to be created between fetch and apply, which the reviewer-acts-on-an-existing-queued-item workflow does not produce; and even then apply validates the transition from pending. Defense-in-depth: moving the comparison into applyActionInTransaction would make it fully race-free and also close the P2 batch gap at one chokepoint. Optional.
nodes.acceptance: The importRuntimeFeedback persisted-vs-supplied comparison is made race-free within the action transaction (moved inside applyActionInTransaction or a shared in-transaction chokepoint) rather than relying solely on evidence-field immutability; if implemented as a shared chokepoint it also closes the batch-path drift. A test covers the in-transaction enforcement.

nodes.title: Int-arg skip semantics itself remains unvalidated against real games (pre-existing, separate concern)
nodes.spec: The fix correctly aligns the emitted index with the stored choices Vec GIVEN the existing design decision that an interleaved ExprValue::Int arg is dropped from the choice list (module_sel.rs:386-396, `continue`). That skip behavior is pre-existing and outside this node's scope, but it is worth a DAG follow-up: in RealLive the conditional variants (notably select_w) carry interleaved condition/flag operands that are NOT user-visible labels, and silently dropping them (rather than e.g. pairing a condition with the following label) may misrepresent conditional choice lists. The store-register value written on resume (vm.rs:661 set_store(index)) is the contiguous post-skip Vec index, which only matches original-engine choice numbering if the engine likewise renumbers after dropping non-label args. Recommend validating the select/select_w arg shape against >=2 real games (per multi-game-validation policy) before relying on the skip semantics; this audit confirms only the internal index-alignment invariant, not the skip's fidelity to the original engine.
nodes.acceptance: The SelectOp Int-arg skip-then-renumber choice semantics in crates/utsushi-reallive/src/rlop/module_sel.rs is validated against the select_w usage of >=2 real RealLive games to confirm the drop-then-contiguous-renumber matches original-engine choice numbering; if it diverges from any real title, a corrective node is filed. (Multi-game-validation rule; needs real RealLive game bytes.)

nodes.title: Sibling spec-dag node count-public-fixture-evidence-in-opportunity-readiness (P2, ready) proposes the OPPOSITE and would reintroduce the false advertisement
nodes.spec: roadmap/spec-dag.json contains node id 'count-public-fixture-evidence-in-opportunity-readiness' (status=ready, priority=P2). Its spec: 'Fix catalog opportunity runtime readiness aggregation so public_fixture adapter-matrix AND key-validation evidence contributes to publicFixtureEvidenceCount and public_fixture readiness instead of being rejected by the private-local-only predicate.' The 'adapter-matrix ... contributes to publicFixtureEvidenceCount' clause directly contradicts the audited resolution (isRuntimeReadinessEvidence in catalog-repository.ts:3336-3347 deliberately excludes public_fixture adapter_matrix because the static capability matrix must not masquerade as runtime readiness) and is exactly the false-advertisement CATALOG-061 warns about. This node is ALSO partially stale: it claims public_fixture evidence is currently 'rejected by the private-local-only predicate', but the sibling node 'count-public-fixture-runtime-evidence-readiness' (status=done) already implemented public_fixture key_validation counting (locked by catalog-repository.test.ts:1616). RECONCILIATION: the key_validation part is already DONE; the remaining adapter_matrix-counting part is WRONG and should be cancelled (or corrected to drop the adapter_matrix clause). NOTE ON IDS: the audit brief referenced numeric ids 31918 & 32543, but roadmap/spec-dag.json uses slug ids and exposes exactly ONE open conflicting node (the slug above). The numeric ids are qd-internal; I did not run qd (per audit constraint), so the orchestrator must map 31918/32543 to slug nodes -- one is almost certainly this slug node. I could not locate a SECOND distinct conflicting open node in spec-dag.json; if 32543 is a separate qd row not yet exported to spec-dag.json, it needs the same cancel/correct treatment.
nodes.acceptance: Finding is addressed and verified.

nodes.title: Missing key_validation public-fixture producer leaves public_fixture / public_and_aggregate runtime-readiness states unreachable in production (warrants a follow-up node)
nodes.spec: The production producer apps/itotori/src/services/catalog-local-capability-evidence.ts declares CatalogCapabilityEvidenceKind = 'adapter_matrix' | 'local_corpus_sidecar' (line 30) -- key_validation is NOT a producible kind. public_fixture evidence is hard-coded to evidenceKind 'adapter_matrix' (lines 353-354) and the validator actively REJECTS any other public_fixture kind (lines 392-396: 'evidenceKind is not supported'). private_local_aggregate emits 'local_corpus_sidecar' (lines 188-189, 218-219). Consequently no production path ever produces public_fixture key_validation evidence, so capabilityEvidenceCountsByAdapter never increments publicFixtureEvidenceCount in production and the public_fixture / public_and_aggregate states are currently unreachable except in tests. This confirms the implementer's 'missing producer' diagnosis and is consistent with the doc block (catalog-repository.ts:3300-3303). It is a genuine feature gap, not a defect in the audited change. RECOMMEND a follow-up DAG node to add a key_validation public-fixture producer (e.g. validating decryption keys against a public fixture) so the advertised public runtime-readiness states become DB-reachable in production.
nodes.acceptance: apps/itotori/src/services/catalog-local-capability-evidence.ts gains a producer path that emits public_fixture key_validation runtime-evidence (the kind catalogOpportunityRanking already counts toward publicFixtureEvidenceCount) when a public fixture's key validation genuinely passes — currently key_validation is not a declarable kind and is rejected (~lines 392-396), so the public/public_and_aggregate runtime-readiness states are production-unreachable. A test asserts a passing public-fixture key_validation yields publicFixtureEvidenceCount>0 end-to-end. (Beta-tier encrypted-engine relevant; may require a real public fixture.)

nodes.title: New boundary test is a real, discriminating contract (adapter_matrix public -> unknown/0); doc block is accurate
nodes.spec: catalog-repository.test.ts ('does not count public_fixture adapter_matrix evidence as runtime readiness') inserts a public_fixture row with status=present (opportunityRuntimeEvidenceWeight=1, i.e. NON-zero so it clears the weight gate at catalog-repository.ts:3315) and evidenceKind=adapter_matrix via the new publicFixtureKind helper param (test helper lines 1854-1856, 1877). It asserts runtimeEvidenceReadiness === {status:'unknown', publicFixtureEvidenceCount:0, privateLocalAggregateEvidenceCount:0} and that runtimeEvidenceFactor yields weightedScore 0 / explanationCode 'runtime_evidence_readiness:unknown'. Because the row has weight>0, the test proves the EXCLUSION is driven by isRuntimeReadinessEvidence (kind check), not merely by a zero-weight skip -- a genuine contract. The helper default for publicFixtureKind remains key_validation, so the positive test at line 1616 (status public_fixture/public_and_aggregate, count 1) is unchanged -- no regression. The doc block at catalog-repository.ts:3294-3308 accurately describes the source split, names both locking tests, and correctly states production keeps the count at 0 until a key_validation producer exists. Doc-only-clarify + negative boundary test is the right resolution for this P3: removing the public dimension would delete a live, tested capability (locked by line 1616 and by the done node count-public-fixture-runtime-evidence-readiness) and force a golden rescore for no correctness gain, since no false advertisement actually exists.
nodes.acceptance: Finding is addressed and verified.
