nodes.title: Cost stored as integer micros cannot represent sub-micro OpenRouter costs (ledger CHECK rejects real cheap-model costs)
nodes.spec:
apps/itotori/src/providers/cost.ts:46 — decimalUsdStringToMicros() rounds usage.cost to integer micros (1e-6 resolution). For the repo's own live evidence value 0.00000602 (docs/openrouter-integration-evidence/2026-06-25.json) it yields 6 micros = 0.000006 USD. recorded.ts:678 asserts |cost.amountMicrosUsd/1_000_000 - usageResponseJson.cost| < 1e-9 and migration 0041 enforces abs(cost_amount - (usage->>'cost')::numeric) < 1e-9. |0.000006 - 0.00000602| = 2e-8 = 20x the tolerance, so the bundle validator throws and/or the DB CHECK rejects the row. ProviderCost retains NO full-precision representation (only micros), so the loss is irreversible. Draft fixtures pass only because they use micro-exact round numbers (0.0125 = 12500 micros) — an author-fixture-masks-the-bug pattern; real cheap-model costs (DEV_PAIR deepseek-v4-flash) are sub-micro and will fail.

This is a defect in the merged implementation of ITOTORI-232 on the alpha live-LLM path (Oshioki Sweetie HD e2e with live OpenRouter). The types.ts:417-419 comment claims the micros↔cost equality "holds by construction" — it does not, because amountMicrosUsd has already rounded. Directly violates the project's "cost is read from the real call, never approximated" rule.
nodes.acceptance:
- The originating OpenRouter usage.cost is stored at full decimal precision (e.g. cost_amount written directly from the usage.cost string, or a nano/pico-USD integer field), so no sub-micro precision is lost between the real call and the ledger/recorded bundle.
- ProviderCost (or the persisted row) carries enough precision that |stored - usage.cost| < 1e-9 for arbitrary real OpenRouter cost values, satisfying recorded.ts:678 and migration 0041's CHECK.
- A test feeds usageResponseJson.cost = 0.00000602 through the recorder and asserts the row inserts and the DB CHECK passes (no RecordedCostMismatchError). Observable: the recorded-cost-replay test case with cost 0.00000602 succeeds instead of throwing.
- No hardcoded/approximated cost is introduced; the no-hardcoded-cost guard stays green.
audit_focus: Sub-micro cost precision loss between real OpenRouter call and the ledger/recorded bundle

nodes.title: no-hardcoded-cost CI guard misses the repo's primary cost shapes (amountMicrosUsd, object-form, bare cost)
nodes.spec:
scripts/audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs:83 — the only hardcoded-cost pattern is /\bcost[_]?[Uu]sd\b\s*:\s*\d/, which fires only on costUsd:/cost_usd: immediately followed by a digit. It does NOT catch `amountMicrosUsd: 12_500` (the canonical in-repo cost field, explicitly excluded by the lines 79-84 comment), `costUsd: { unit: "usd", amount: "0.01250000" }` (object form), or a bare `cost: 0.0125` inside a usageResponseJson literal. All three live shapes exist in tracked, scanned src (e.g. draft-attempt-fixtures.ts). The script also lists `.rs` in shouldScan but feeds it zero rust files (scope is `git ls-files apps packages scripts fixtures`).

The standing rule "no hardcoded model cost anywhere, ever" is enforced by this script in `just ci`. The gap means the rule is unenforced for the repo's own primary cost representation, giving false CI assurance — there is at least one live violation (fabricated fixture costs) that slips through it.
nodes.acceptance:
- FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS is extended to flag numeric or numeric-string literals assigned to amountMicrosUsd:, cost:, and amount: (object-form costUsd) outside the documented allow-list, in addition to the existing costUsd:/cost_usd: pattern.
- A regression fixture proves each of the three previously-missed shapes is caught. Observable: `node scripts/audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs` exits 1 on a file containing `amountMicrosUsd: 12_500` (and on the object-form and bare `cost:` shapes).
- The `.rs` scope inconsistency is resolved (either scan rust files or drop `.rs` from shouldScan), with the chosen behavior documented.
- Any live violation the strengthened guard now surfaces in tracked src is either fixed or explicitly allow-listed with a rationale, so `just ci` passes.
audit_focus: Cost-guard regex coverage gaps giving false CI assurance

nodes.title: 32 RealLive patch-back/inventory/smoke contract tests are compiled out via #![cfg(any())]
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests/patchback.rs:23, smoke.rs:24, inventory.rs:21 each begin with `#![cfg(any())]` (always-false), compiling out 11+10+11 = 32 tests. patchback.rs is the only dedicated coverage of the exported apply_patches/SlotEdit error contracts (OffsetOverflow, UnsupportedLengthPolicy, UnknownSlotId, StaleSourceHash, ShiftJisEncodeFailure, ProtectedSpanLost). The module docstrings still claim the suites are active, and the replacement *_real_bytes.rs tests are all #[ignore], so on a stock `just ci` these production error contracts have effectively zero coverage. apply_patches is production surface (extract->patch->verify is the Kaifuu core promise); a regression in PatchBackErrorCode mapping ships green.
nodes.acceptance: Add default-running unit tests (in crates/kaifuu-reallive/src/patchback/mod.rs) asserting each PatchBackErrorCode variant is returned for its trigger, OR migrate the byte builders and remove the #![cfg(any())] attribute. Observable: `cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive patchback` reports >0 tests run (not '0 filtered out'), and `rg -n 'cfg\(any\(\)\)' crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests` returns no hits.
audit_focus: Documented PatchBackErrorCode error contracts have zero default-running coverage; a mapping regression ships green

nodes.title: Utsushi substrate slices (embed ABI, conformance, reference recorder, state-diff, runner) have zero non-test consumers
nodes.spec: NEEDS DECOMPOSITION: this is a slice-by-slice wiring/defer decision, not a single bounded fix; the orchestrator should split it per slice before claiming. Full-repo grep confirms these substrate types are referenced only in utsushi-core/{src,tests} and */tests/, with no engine adapter or product binary driving them: EmbedState/EmbedTrace/embed_state/embed_capabilities (UTSUSHI-024); ConformanceManifest/ConformanceResult/TraceConformanceCheck/BranchConformanceCheck/FrameCaptureConformanceCheck/SnapshotConformanceCheck/RecordingConformanceCheck (UTSUSHI-025..030); ReferenceRecorder/ReferenceTrace/InMemoryReferenceRecorder; StateDiff/StateChange/diff_snapshots; RunnerCancellation/RunnerObservation/RunnerOutcome. These are the substrate's validation product surface yet validate nothing in any shipping flow — the 'substrate types with no production consumer' anti-pattern in docs/orchestration-operating-model.md §DAG Anti-Patterns. Note: the EnginePort/ConformanceManifest consumer slice is partially tracked by open nodes UTSUSHI-154 (fixture port adoption) and UTSUSHI-160 (first production engine port consumes ConformanceManifest); this node covers the remaining unowned slices (embed ABI, reference recorder, state-diff, runner).
nodes.acceptance: Per slice, either land a non-test consumer or explicitly mark the slice deferred in docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md with the import-line citation. Observable: for any slice claimed 'consumed', `rg -lw embed_state crates/utsushi-reallive/src crates/utsushi-siglus/src crates/utsushi-cli/src crates/utsushi-fixture/src` (substitute the slice symbol) returns a non-test src file; for any slice claimed 'deferred', docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md names it.

verification.value: Re-audit each enumerated substrate slice: confirm it has either a non-test consumer or a deferred-marker in docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md

audit_focus: Substrate validation surface that ships with no production consumer (scaffolding without load); audits may mistake it for shipping validation

nodes.title: Utsushi CLI (product entry point) bypasses the EnginePort/Runner/Conformance substrate entirely
nodes.spec: NEEDS DECOMPOSITION: reconciling two parallel runtime abstractions is architectural; split into (a) decide canonical path and (b) route or delete the other before claiming. crates/utsushi-cli/src/main.rs:7 imports RuntimeAdapterRegistry/RuntimeOperation/RuntimeRequest (a separate core abstraction) and replay_validate.rs:21 drives utsushi_reallive::{replay_scene, validate_log_contains} directly. No EnginePort, Runner, or ConformanceManifest reference exists anywhere in utsushi-cli/src. There are two parallel runtime abstractions in utsushi-core — the RuntimeAdapterRegistry path (what ships) and the EnginePort/Runner/conformance substrate (UTSUSHI-020..120, implemented by reallive/siglus but never executed). The substrate's conformance checks validate nothing in the actual replay-validate flow; the implemented-but-unrun path is dead weight an auditor may mistake for shipping validation.
nodes.acceptance: Route one CLI subcommand through Runner + a *ConformanceCheck producing a ConformanceResult-schema artifact, OR document in docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md the two-path split and which is canonical (and delete the non-canonical path per the no-legacy-compat rule). Observable: either a `cargo run -p utsushi-cli -- replay-validate ...` invocation emits a ConformanceResult-schema JSON artifact, or `rg -n 'EnginePort|Runner|ConformanceManifest' crates/utsushi-cli/src` is non-empty.

verification.value: Confirm docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md names which of RuntimeAdapterRegistry vs EnginePort/Runner is canonical and that the other is wired or removed

audit_focus: Hidden runtime fork: implemented-but-unrun EnginePort/conformance substrate mistaken for the shipping validation path

nodes.title: Fabricated hardcoded model costs in tracked, scanned src/draft/draft-attempt-fixtures.ts
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/draft/draft-attempt-fixtures.ts:86 `amountMicrosUsd: 12_500`, :103 `cost: 0.0125`, :158 `costUsd: {amount: "0.01250000"}`, :193 `amountMicrosUsd: 8_500`, :199 `cost: 0.0085`, :214 amount "0.00850000". The header calls these a 'live cost' but they are invented round numbers tied to no captured OpenRouter call (model anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet, no recorded bundle). File is under src/ (NOT the allow-listed apps/itotori/test/fixtures/recorded-bundles/) and survives CI only because of the audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs regex gap. Directly violates the absolute 'no hardcoded model cost anywhere, ever' rule in scanned production source. Note: this is a live regression of the now-done ITOTORI-225 intent and is unblocked by the hardcoded-cost CI-guard tightening (already node-ified separately).
nodes.acceptance: Source these costs from a captured recorded-bundle artifact, or move the file under apps/itotori/test/fixtures/recorded-bundles/ with a comment pinning the capture. Observable: after the tightened audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs regex lands, `node scripts/audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs` exits 0 and `rg -n 'amountMicrosUsd: 12_500|cost: 0.0125' apps/itotori/src/draft/draft-attempt-fixtures.ts` returns no hits.
audit_focus: Hardcoded/fabricated model costs in scanned production src violating the no-hardcoded-cost rule

nodes.title: RealLive real-bytes tests self-report PASS when ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT is unset, despite a 'no silent pass' comment
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests/scene_1_dispatch_real_bytes.rs:55 is `#[ignore = "real-bytes; requires ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT"]` then `let Some(seen_path) = real_seen_txt_path() else { eprintln!("...no silent pass..."); return; };` — the early return reports the test as passed having asserted nothing. Same pattern across patchback_real_bytes.rs, bridge_real_bytes.rs, gameexe_real_bytes.rs, parse_archive_real_bytes.rs, detect_real_bytes.rs. Combined with the #![cfg(any())] disabled suites, the substantive parser/bridge/patchback validation against real Sweetie HD bytes is invisible-by-default and green when not executed; 'no silent pass' is contradicted by the control flow. Note: a prior fix (done node real-bytes-test-silently-no-ops-when-kaifuu-real-sweetie-hd-path) only pinned one utsushi-reallive save test under KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH; this kaifuu-reallive family under ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT remains unaddressed.
nodes.acceptance: Under an explicit ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES=1 flag, make the unset-env branch panic/fail instead of returning. Observable: `ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES=1 cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive -- --ignored` fails when ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT is unset, naming the missing corpus.
audit_focus: False-green real-bytes coverage: env-unset early-return reports PASS with zero assertions

nodes.title: Test named '..._at_least_90_percent_opcode_recognition' asserts only >= 0.75
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests/scene_1_dispatch_real_bytes.rs:56 `fn dispatches_sweetie_hd_scene_1_with_at_least_90_percent_opcode_recognition` but the body asserts `recognition_rate >= 0.75` at :262; the inline comment (:250-261) concedes the real rate is ~84% and the 90% target 'requires a deeper ExpressionPiece evaluator'. A reader or grep auditing for the spec's 90% gate sees a passing test named for it while the enforced floor is 15 points lower — name/threshold drift advertising a guarantee the code does not enforce.
nodes.acceptance: Rename the fn to encode the actually-asserted floor (e.g. ..._at_least_75_percent...), OR raise the assertion literal to >= 0.90. Observable: the numeric in the test fn name and the numeric in its `assert!`/`>=` literal agree, checkable via `rg -n 'percent_opcode_recognition|recognition_rate >=' crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests/scene_1_dispatch_real_bytes.rs`.
audit_focus: Test name advertises a 90% guarantee the assertion (0.75) does not enforce

nodes.title: Gameexe.ini read errors silently swallowed into empty inventory via unwrap_or_default in kaifuu CLI bridge
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs:388 `let gameexe_bytes = fs::read(&gameexe_path).unwrap_or_default();` then `parse_gameexe_inventory(&gameexe_bytes)`. Every other read in this fn is `?`-propagated or bounds-checked (lines 360-380); this one conflates file-absent / permission-denied / I/O-error / genuinely-empty into a zero-entry inventory with no diagnostic — producing a structurally-valid-but-wrong bundle that downstream patch-back trusts. Contradicts the codebase's no-silent-fallback posture.
nodes.acceptance: Propagate the error (`fs::read(&gameexe_path)?` with a kaifuu.reallive.gameexe_unreadable context) or branch explicitly on ErrorKind::NotFound. Observable: running the bridge subcommand against a game root with `chmod 000 Gameexe.ini` exits non-zero with a named error instead of emitting an empty-inventory bundle.
nodes.status_reason:
[2026-06-29T14:36:21.132Z] Verification sign-off (manual): Audit PASS: structured gameexe_absent/unreadable diagnostic replaces silent unwrap_or_default; distinction correct; test covers both.
Value: Run kaifuu bridge against a root with chmod 000 Gameexe.ini and confirm non-zero exit with a typed gameexe-unreadable error
Evidence: .tmp/audit-kaifuu-cli-gameexe-read-unwrap-or-default-swallow.json

verification.value: Run kaifuu bridge against a root with chmod 000 Gameexe.ini and confirm non-zero exit with a typed gameexe-unreadable error

audit_focus: Silent I/O-error fallback yields a wrong-but-valid inventory bundle that patch-back trusts

nodes.title: OpenRouter provider.max_price built with a discarded conversion and a non-standard {request:...} key (possibly inert wire cap)
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts:582 calls `maxPriceUsdToDecimalString(request.maxPriceUsd);` purely for its throw side-effect and discards the result; :583 then sets `provider.max_price = { request: request.maxPriceUsd }` to the raw number. The discarded conversion implies the decimal string was meant to be the wire value, and `{ request }` is a suspect shape. If OpenRouter ignores the unknown key, the on-wire price cap is inert and only the post-hoc cost_cap_exceeded reject (after the call is already billed) protects spend. Note: this is a correctness gap in the implementation produced by the now-done node pair-policy-max-price-not-enforced.
nodes.acceptance: Assert the exact provider.max_price JSON emitted against docs/openrouter-integration.md in apps/itotori/test/openrouter-provider.test.ts, and either use the converted decimal string or remove the dead conversion call. Observable: a test asserts the serialized request body's provider.max_price equals the documented shape, and `node --test` over openrouter-provider.test.ts passes.
nodes.status_reason:
[2026-06-29T14:36:21.255Z] Verification sign-off (manual): Audit PASS: max_price cap genuinely on-wire (provider.max_price.request documented); discarded-conversion cleaned up; pre-request validation preserved+tested.
Value: Confirm openrouter-provider.test.ts asserts the emitted provider.max_price JSON against the shape documented in docs/openrouter-integration.md
Evidence: .tmp/audit-openrouter-max-price-discarded-conversion-inert.json

verification.value: Confirm openrouter-provider.test.ts asserts the emitted provider.max_price JSON against the shape documented in docs/openrouter-integration.md

audit_focus: Wire price cap may be inert (unknown key + discarded conversion), leaving only post-billing cost-cap to protect spend

nodes.title: Batch-planner resolves to providerId 'unknown' via ?? fallbacks, defeating the (model, provider) pair pin
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/batch-planner/model-profiles.ts:18 `fallbackModelProfile.providerId: "unknown"`, :149 `providerId: input.providerId ?? "unknown"`, :163 `?? fallbackModelProfile.providerId`. A model profile can carry providerId:'unknown', persisted on every batch (the caller persists the result so audits can replay sizing decisions). It is reached through a soft `??` rather than a loud failure — exactly the 'provider not declared' state the model-provider-pair rule forbids. An unknown provider in persisted planning rows defeats per-pair attribution and undermines the pair-pin invariant.
nodes.acceptance: Require an explicit providerId when a modelId/descriptor is supplied (throw, mirroring DevPairUnknownError), OR document+test that batch profiles are non-routing metadata never used to attribute a real invocation. Observable: `rg -n '"unknown"' apps/itotori/src/batch-planner/model-profiles.ts` returns no provider-fallback hits, or a test asserts a missing providerId throws.
audit_focus: Soft providerId:'unknown' fallback persisted in planning rows defeats (model,provider) pair attribution

nodes.title: g00 LZSS decoder reserves Vec capacity from an untrusted u32 size field (allocation amplification / OOM)
nodes.spec: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/g00.rs:926 `let mut dst = Vec::with_capacity(uncompressed_size);` where uncompressed_size is read directly from the file's LZSS preamble (parse_lzss_section, g00.rs:859, a u32 up to ~4.29 GB). utsushi reads attacker-controllable game bytes; a malformed g00 with a huge uncompressed_size triggers a multi-GB up-front allocation before any payload is read. The decode loop itself is correctly bounds-checked (OutputOverflow guard :979) — only the pre-allocation is unguarded. A single malformed asset can OOM/abort the validation tool before parsing begins.
nodes.acceptance: Cap the reserve to min(uncompressed_size, input.len().saturating_mul(K)) or a hard ceiling. Observable: add a test feeding a 16-byte g00 with uncompressed_size=0xFFFFFFFF and assert it returns a typed G00DecodeError (not an abort/OOM); `cargo test -p utsushi-reallive g00` passes including this case.
audit_focus: Unbounded pre-allocation from an untrusted size field on attacker-controllable bytes (OOM/abort before parse)

nodes.title: utsushi-siglus manifest over-claims four capabilities it cannot perform; suite is ~100% tautological
nodes.spec: crates/utsushi-siglus/src/lib.rs:212-216 declares `capabilities: &[Launch, Observe, Capture, Shutdown]` and `evidence_tier_max: E1` while every lifecycle method (:256-263) returns `Err(unimplemented_lifecycle(...))`. Separately, tests/cross_engine_substrate_alignment.rs assert hardcoded MANIFEST constants equal hardcoded constants and that a scaffold returns the exact UNIMPLEMENTED_MESSAGE it is defined to return; substrate_conformance.rs is 'a compile-time test'; no real Siglus bytes are exercised. Per the operating model's >40% tautological-test rejection rule the suite is essentially all mirror tests, and the manifest is a claimed-support framing the implementation satisfies for none of the four capabilities. This is the standalone utsushi-siglus crate, distinct from the inline Siglus scaffold in utsushi-reallive tests (tracked by inline-siglus-scaffold-is-inert-by-design-...).
nodes.acceptance: Set siglus `capabilities: &[]` (or a Scaffold marker) until a lifecycle method does real work; gate any non-empty capability behind a real-bytes Siglus test. Observable: `rg -n 'capabilities' crates/utsushi-siglus/src/lib.rs` shows an empty capability slice while launch still returns Unimplemented, OR a real-bytes Siglus test backs each declared capability.

verification.value: Confirm utsushi-siglus declared capabilities match what a lifecycle method actually performs against real Siglus bytes

audit_focus: Capability manifest claims Launch/Observe/Capture/Shutdown while all lifecycle methods return Unimplemented; tautological suite

nodes.title: RealLive real-bytes corpus is concentrated on a single title (Sweetie HD), below the >=2 games/engine requirement
nodes.spec: crates/utsushi-reallive/tests has 15 *_real_bytes.rs files; SWEETIE_HD_* constants appear in 21 files (175 mentions). A second title appears only via gameexe_real_bytes.rs:81 (REGNAME HADASHI/OSHIOKIHD, 1 file). The decoders (g00 types 0/1/2, NWA, OVK, decompressor, bytecode, scene index/header) are validated against Sweetie HD bytes only. Violates the project's >=2-real-games-per-engine-family validation rule; format invariants inferred from one packer's output risk over-fitting.
nodes.acceptance: Add a second RealLive title's bytes to at least the g00 + decompressor + scene-index real-bytes tests asserting documented values. Observable: each of those decoders' real-bytes tests references >=2 distinct title constants, checkable via `rg -n 'SWEETIE_HD|OSHIOKI|<second-title>' crates/utsushi-reallive/tests/g00_real_bytes.rs` showing >=2 title constants.

verification.value: Confirm g00, decompressor, and scene-index real-bytes tests each exercise >=2 distinct RealLive titles

audit_focus: Single-title (Sweetie HD) corpus risks over-fitting format invariants to one packer; violates >=2-games-per-engine rule

nodes.title: Operating-model doc claim 'zero non-test substrate consumers outside utsushi-core' is factually stale
nodes.spec: docs/orchestration-operating-model.md:167 says 'The current substrate (UTSUSHI-020..120) is in a deferred state: zero non-test consumers exist outside utsushi-core.' Reality: utsushi-reallive production code consumes a substrate subset — lib.rs:105 (EnginePort/PortManifest/SinkSet/AssetPackage) + impl at :562; vm.rs:45 / var_banks.rs:42 (Inspectable/Restorable/StateTree/SnapshotError); replay.rs:42 (Snapshot/TextSurfaceSink/take_snapshot/restore_snapshot); rlop/module_{msg,str,sel}.rs (TextLine/TextSurfaceSink). The anti-pattern audit gate keys off this doc; a false premise means the gate mis-evaluates which slices are scaffolding vs consumed (see node utsushi-substrate-slices-no-production-consumer).
nodes.acceptance: Update docs/orchestration-operating-model.md to list which substrate slices have production consumers (snapshot/sink/port) vs which remain deferred, citing the import lines. Observable: `rg -lw EnginePort crates/utsushi-reallive/src` is non-empty AND the doc no longer asserts 'zero non-test consumers'.

verification.value: rg -n 'zero non-test consumers' docs/orchestration-operating-model.md; rg -lw EnginePort crates/utsushi-reallive/src

audit_focus: Stale doc premise drives the anti-pattern gate to mis-evaluate which substrate slices are scaffolding vs consumed

nodes.title: Opcode arg/expression byte-framing desyncs on legal expression bytes, with no pinning/xfail test
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-reallive/src/opcode.rs:377-403 arg-list loop treats raw bytes 0x2C and 0x29 as structural delimiters (`b')' => { closed = true; break; }`). The module itself documents (:290-304) that expression bodies legally contain 0x2C separators and 0xFF+4-byte int literals whose payload bytes can equal 0x28/0x29/0x2C. So a command whose argument is an int literal containing 0x29 terminates the arg list early, mis-sets `consumed`, and desyncs `pos` for the rest of the stream — inflating the Unknown count and potentially corrupting offsets used by patch-back. Disclosed as a 'follow-up node' but no xfail/regression test pins the known-bad scene positions; a future 'fix' could silently change framing with nothing red.
nodes.acceptance: Add a test over the real Sweetie HD scene-1 decompressed stream asserting the exact byte offsets where framing currently desyncs (a known-unknown manifest), anchoring recognition to byte ranges not a single aggregate >= 0.75. Observable: `cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive` includes a test naming specific desync byte offsets that fails if framing changes.

verification.value: Confirm a pinning test asserts exact desync byte offsets on the real scene-1 stream

audit_focus: Arg/expression mis-framing on legal expression bytes propagates forward, corrupting patch-back offsets, with no pinning test

nodes.title: vault-source swallows SQL errors on optional fields into None / dropped candidates
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-vault-source/src/discovery.rs:223-237 (engine_version filter) and :284-300 (load_candidate) use `.query_row(...).ok()` — a real SQL error (corrupt DB, schema drift) is indistinguishable from 'fact absent', and at :233-237 a candidate whose engine_version query errors is silently dropped from the filtered set. A schema/DB-integrity problem manifests as 'release not resolved' rather than a typed DB error, hard to diagnose, and could silently exclude valid releases. Lower severity: facts are genuinely optional and the catalog is read-only.
nodes.acceptance: Distinguish QueryReturnedNoRows (-> None) from other rusqlite::Error (-> propagate map_query_err). Observable: a test pointing the resolver at a DB with a dropped v_current_facts view returns VaultSourceError mentioning the DB, not ReleaseNotResolved.

verification.value: Confirm a test with a dropped v_current_facts view yields a DB-named VaultSourceError, not ReleaseNotResolved

audit_focus: SQL errors on optional fields collapse into 'fact absent', silently dropping candidates and masking DB integrity faults

nodes.title: Graphics RLOps discard typed GraphicsStackError with `let _ =`, inconsistent with var_banks' no-silent-swallow flow
nodes.spec: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/rlop/module_obj.rs:793/826/912 and module_grp.rs:327/383/473/529/585/908 do `let _ = stack.set(...)` / `let _ = stack.clear(...)`. set/clear return Result<_, GraphicsStackError::SlotOutOfRange> (graphics_objects.rs:346/362). Currently unreachable (slot_index pre-validates 0..256), but the discard masks future divergence — directly opposite to var_banks.rs:402 which surfaces the analogous out-of-range as a typed VarBanksWarning. Two sibling modules treat the identical invariant oppositely.
nodes.acceptance: Route GraphicsStackError into the runtime diagnostic sink (mirroring var_banks) or `debug_assert!(result.is_ok())`. Observable: a unit test forcing an out-of-range slot observes a diagnostic (not a silent no-op), and `rg -n 'let _ = stack\.(set|clear)' crates/utsushi-reallive/src/rlop` returns no hits.

verification.value: Confirm an out-of-range graphics slot surfaces a diagnostic mirroring var_banks rather than a silent discard

audit_focus: Sibling modules treat the identical 256-slot invariant oppositely; discarded GraphicsStackError hides future divergence

nodes.title: Cost-cap comparison uses `?? 0`, a latent silent-pass on the spend guard if amount ever becomes absent
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts:340 `const actualMicrosUsd = normalized.cost.amountMicrosUsd ?? 0;` then compares `actualMicrosUsd > maxPriceMicrosUsd`. amountMicrosUsd is a required number (types.ts:344) so the `?? 0` is dead today, but if a future change yields an absent amount the cost-cap treats it as $0 and always passes — the opposite of fail-loud. Latent silent-undercount path on the spend guard.
nodes.acceptance: Drop the `?? 0` (let a missing amount throw) or call assertBilledCost before comparing. Observable: a test feeding a cost with no amount throws rather than passing the cap, and `rg -n 'amountMicrosUsd \?\? 0' apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts` returns no hits.

verification.value: Confirm a test proves a missing cost amount throws instead of passing the cost cap

audit_focus: Latent `?? 0` on the spend guard treats a missing cost as $0 and always passes the cap

nodes.title: gameexe.rs silently drops malformed numeric tokens via `.parse().ok()?`
nodes.spec: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/gameexe.rs:561/:574/:600-602 use `token.parse().ok()?` — a non-numeric value in an int/array/tuple entry makes the whole key silently resolve to None rather than a typed parse diagnostic. Consumers compound it: syscall.rs:704 then does `u32::try_from(...).ok()?` again. A missing key and a malformed key are indistinguishable to the caller. Contradicts utsushi's 'no silent fallback' posture; a corrupt Gameexe entry vanishes instead of surfacing a diagnostic in a validation tool.
nodes.acceptance: Return a typed GameexeParseWarning { key, raw } collected into a warnings vec (mirroring VarBanksWarning). Observable: a test feeding `KEY = abc` asserts the warning is emitted (parsing a malformed int key yields a diagnostic, not a silent None).

verification.value: Confirm parsing `KEY = abc` yields a typed GameexeParseWarning rather than a silent None

audit_focus: Malformed numeric Gameexe tokens silently vanish to None, indistinguishable from absent keys

nodes.title: 256-byte AVG32_XOR_MASK constant copy-pasted across decompressor, compressor, and a test with no single source of truth
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-reallive/src/decompressor.rs:30, compressor.rs:38, and tests/scene_1_dispatch_real_bytes.rs:282 each define the same 256-byte mask (compressor :34-37 notes the duplication is deliberate). A transcription error in any copy diverges encode from decode (or test from production) and is only caught by round-trip tests that themselves use the encoder's copy — so the safety net shares one of the copies. Divergence risk for a load-bearing constant.
nodes.acceptance: Expose one `pub(crate) const AVG32_XOR_MASK` and have compressor + tests reference it. Observable: `rg -c 'const AVG32_XOR_MASK' crates/kaifuu-reallive` shows a single definition.
audit_focus: Triplicated load-bearing 256-byte mask; round-trip safety net shares one copy, hiding transcription divergence

nodes.title: Protected-span detection indexes whole-slot decoded text by a prefix-decode length (unproven Shift-JIS prefix-stability invariant)
nodes.spec: crates/kaifuu-reallive/src/protected_spans.rs:472-475 decoded_byte_offset_for_raw_offset computes `decoded_offset = decode_shift_jis_slot(&raw_bytes[..raw_offset]).text.len()`, then indexes decoded_text (decode of the whole slot) at :152/:170/:207/:224/:242/:317/:415/:444. This relies on decode(prefix).len() always equaling the byte position of that boundary inside decode(whole) — not guaranteed for lossy/replacement SJIS across a split double-byte pair, risking a str index panic (P0 if reachable) or wrong raw_text. Likely safe today (span starts are control bytes <0x20) but untested against adversarial bytes.
nodes.acceptance: Add a unit test feeding detect_protected_spans a slot with a truncated SJIS lead byte immediately before a 0x1f color code and assert a typed result (no panic). Observable: `cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive protected_span` includes the adversarial-prefix case and passes without a char-boundary panic.

verification.value: Confirm a test feeds a truncated SJIS lead byte before a 0x1f color code and asserts no panic

audit_focus: Unproven SJIS prefix-length-equals-boundary invariant; a char-boundary index panic on adversarial bytes would be a crash

nodes.title: Object-form costUsd literal slips through when formatted across multiple lines
nodes.spec:
The audit is strictly line-based (findViolations splits on /?
/ and tests each pattern per line). Three of the four costLiteral patterns are single-key (amountMicrosUsd, bare cost, bare costUsd:number) and therefore always occupy one line, so they are robustly caught. But the fourth, 'hardcoded non-zero costUsd object amount literal', requires `costUsd:` AND its inner `amount: "<digit>"` on the SAME line: regex / ["'`]?costUsd["'`]?\s*:\s*\{[^}]* ["'`]?amount["'`]?\s*:\s*["'`](?=[\d.]*[1-9])/. PROBE: a file containing
  costUsd: {
    unit: "usd",
    amount: "0.0125",
  }
over separate lines was scanned with `node scripts/audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs <probe>` -> 'audit passed', exit 0 (MISS). This is the natural prettier output once the object exceeds printWidth, and `amount:` on its own line carries no `cost` token so the bare-cost pattern cannot catch it either. A production file hardcoding ONLY the decimal-string costUsd object (without the single-line amountMicrosUsd that would otherwise trip the guard) would pass CI silently. It is rated P2 rather than P1 because the PRIMARY integer field (amountMicrosUsd) and the single-line object form are caught, and a realistic hardcoded cost usually also carries amountMicrosUsd; but a production hardcoded cost CAN slip through this specific multi-line shape, so a reviewer could reasonably argue P1. Suggested fix: match the decimal-string `amount:` standalone (the rip-out rule forbids any hardcoded decimal-USD amount regardless of enclosing key) or normalize the costUsd object across newlines before matching.
nodes.acceptance: scripts/audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs catches a hardcoded object-form costUsd cost even when formatted across multiple lines (costUsd: {\n amount: "0.0125"\n}) — e.g. by matching a standalone decimal-string amount: or joining the object across newlines before matching. A regression fixture with a multi-line costUsd object makes node scripts/audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs exit 1.
nodes.status_reason: Promoted from finding 2706b80c-8dce-46ab-b8b8-8dec4640f024 on node fix-hardcoded-cost-guard-misses-primary-shapes.
audit_focus: Multi-line-formatted hardcoded cost objects slipping past the line-based guard

nodes.title: draft-attempt-fixtures.ts lives in src/ while being cost-literal-exempt
nodes.spec: apps/itotori/src/draft/draft-attempt-fixtures.ts is explicitly allow-listed for cost literals yet sits in the production src/ tree (and is the only src-tree path so exempted). Today it is unreachable from production (sole importer is apps/itotori/test/draft-attempt-recorder.test.ts; no barrel re-export), so there is no live leak. The latent risk: a future production import would ship its synthetic costs (amountMicrosUsd 12_500, cost 0.0125, costUsd "0.01250000") while the guard stays silent. Lower-entropy alternative would be relocating the fixture under apps/itotori/test/ so it is covered by the generic test/ exemption and cannot be production-imported, removing the need for a bespoke src-path allow-list entry.

nodes.title: Agentic-loop bundle per-invocation/stage costUsd remains micros-derived (loses sub-micro tail)
nodes.spec: costUsd=microsToAmount(telemetry.costMicros) / microsToAmount(stage.costMicros); costMicros=assertBilledCost(providerRun.cost) (micros). microsToAmount pads to exactly 6 fractional digits, so a 0.00000602 invocation reports '0.000006'. The AgenticLoopInvocation.costUsd doc (localization-bridge-schema/agentic-loop-bundle.ts:98-101) claims 'the real billed USD cost as a decimal string', which is now imprecise for sub-micro costs — the full-precision authority lives in ProviderCost.amountUsd / ledger cost_amount. This bundle is not persisted under the migration-0041 CHECK and is not the ledger authority, so it is not a P0/P1 integrity bug; it is a parallel-representation fidelity/doc gap out of this node's scope.

A test asserts a 0.00000602 invocation's bundle costUsd is full-precision.

nodes.title: Patch-back: non-Textout slots carry instruction-opener offset, corrupting structure on legacy apply_patches
nodes.spec:
From general-audit-1 + the reallive-patchback-contract-tests audit: `make_slot`/`parser.rs` set every slot's `byte_offset_within_scene` to the INSTRUCTION opener offset. That is correct for Textout (slot bytes == instruction bytes), but WRONG for other roles: for Choice options `byte_len` = choice-text length while offset points at the opcode opener, so the legacy `apply_patches` -> `apply_slot_edit` reads/splices over the opcode HEADER → structural corruption of the patched scene. `inventory.rs:225` extracts source text/hash at the same wrong offset, so the StaleSourceHash gate passes consistently and only a post-patch re-parse (counts/lengths, not byte-identity) backstops it — a silent-corruption risk.

Severity P2 (not P1) because the canonical KAIFUU-211 `bundle_driven::apply_translated_bundle` path is IMMUNE (it re-walks and computes authoritative Choice/Textout ranges, ignoring byte_offset_within_scene). The bug only affects the LEGACY `apply_patches` surface, reachable from the `binary-patch-smoke` CLI and the `kaifuu-engine-fixture` PatchBack adapter (lib.rs:4527). **Escalates to P1 if the engine-fixture `apply_patches` route — not `bundle_driven` — is what the alpha Oshioki Sweetie HD e2e patch-back actually invokes** (determine this).
nodes.acceptance:
- First determine whether the alpha Oshioki Sweetie HD e2e patch-back invokes the legacy `apply_patches` route (engine-fixture PatchBack adapter / binary-patch-smoke) or the canonical `bundle_driven::apply_translated_bundle`; record the finding. If alpha uses `apply_patches`, treat this as P1.
- Per no-legacy-compat, EITHER fix the parser so `byte_offset_within_scene` (and `byte_len`) point at the actual editable ARG bytes for ALL slot roles (Choice, Textout, TextDisplay, Speaker), making `apply_patches` byte-correct — OR delete the legacy `apply_patches` surface and route the engine-fixture PatchBack adapter through `bundle_driven`.
- A default-running test patches a Choice-option slot via the chosen path and asserts the result is byte-correct (the opcode header is NOT overwritten); `cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive` covers it.
- Validate against >=2 real RealLive games that contain `sel`/Choice opcodes (multi-game-validation rule), since single-game (Sweetie HD) Choice layout may not generalize.

verification.value: Determine if alpha Oshioki patch-back uses apply_patches vs bundle_driven; validate Choice-slot patchback byte-correct on >=2 real RealLive games

audit_focus: Non-Textout slot offset pointing at opcode header causing silent patch-back corruption on the legacy apply_patches path

nodes.title: Delete unused embed STATE/envelope surface (keep capability surface — recorder consumes it)
nodes.spec:
P1 decomposition (parent: utsushi-substrate-slices-no-production-consumer), slice = Embed ABI (UTSUSHI-024). Per-slice decision: DELETE. Evidence: embed_state()/embed_capabilities() and EmbedState/EmbedTrace/EmbedArtifactRef/EmbedSnapshotRef/EmbedCapability are referenced only in crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/{state,capability}.rs and their own #[cfg(test)] modules; full-repo grep finds no engine adapter, CLI subcommand, or product binary that calls embed_state/embed_capabilities. The slice's stated purpose ('host-readable snapshot of the substrate') has no agentic-host consumer on any milestone tier (real-game-testing-ready / alpha / beta / full). Per the anti-scaffolding and no-legacy-compat rules, remove the slice now rather than carry a deferred marker; if a host-embed consumer is later specced it returns evidence-first.

Deliverables:
- Delete crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/ (state.rs, capability.rs, artifact.rs, diagnostics.rs, redaction.rs, mod.rs) and its module declaration in lib.rs.
- Remove the Embed ABI block from crates/utsushi-core/src/substrate.rs (EMBED_SCHEMA_VERSION, EmbedArtifactRef, EmbedCapability, EmbedCapabilityId, EmbedCapabilityStatus, EmbedError, EmbedSchemaVersion, EmbedSnapshotRef, EmbedState, EmbedTrace, EmbedTraceLine, embed_capabilities, embed_state) and the matching const-assertion pin.
- Remove the Embed ABI row from docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md section 2 and the EMBED_SCHEMA_VERSION row in section 3.
- Drop any embed-only entries from the substrate conformance test (crates/utsushi-core/tests/substrate_conformance.rs).
nodes.acceptance:
- DELETE only the genuinely-unused embed STATE/ENVELOPE surface in crates/utsushi-core/src/embed/ — the symbols referenced ONLY within embed/ and #[cfg(test)] (EmbedState, EmbedTrace, EmbedTraceLine, EmbedSnapshotRef, EmbedArtifactRef, EmbedError, EmbedSchemaVersion, EMBED_SCHEMA_VERSION, embed_state, embed_capabilities) plus their now-orphaned tests (embed_redaction.rs, embed_fixture_state.rs, and the embed-state portion of substrate_conformance.rs).
- KEEP the embed CAPABILITY surface (EmbedCapability, EmbedCapabilityId, EmbedCapabilityStatus, sort_capabilities, validate_capability_list, EMBED_MAX_CAPABILITIES): it has a real non-test consumer — the recorder substrate (crates/utsushi-core/src/recorder/{trace,builder}.rs). Its eventual deletion is deferred to and gated by utsushi-reference-recorder-reconcile-or-delete (if the recorder is deleted, the capability surface can then go too).
- `rg -n 'EmbedState|EmbedTrace|EmbedSnapshotRef|EmbedArtifactRef|EmbedSchemaVersion|EMBED_SCHEMA_VERSION|embed_state' crates/utsushi-core` returns nothing after deletion; `cargo build -p utsushi-core` + `cargo test -p utsushi-core` pass (the recorder still compiles against the retained capability surface). No shim/dead re-export of the deleted state symbols remains.
audit_focus: Confirm no non-test consumer existed at deletion time (the slice was scaffolding without load)
No orphaned facade re-export or const-assertion pin left referencing the deleted embed symbols
Deletion is total (no #[deprecated] shim, no dual plumbing) per no-legacy-compat

nodes.title: Reconcile the reference-recorder trace surface with ReplayLog/observation-hooks (delete the redundant one)
nodes.spec:
P1 decomposition (parent: utsushi-substrate-slices-no-production-consumer), slice = Reference recorder (UTSUSHI-060/062: ReferenceRecorder, InMemoryReferenceRecorder, ReferenceTrace, RecordingTextSink). Per-slice decision: DECIDE then DELETE-or-WIRE, defaulting to DELETE. Evidence: these types are referenced only in crates/utsushi-core/src/recorder/* and tests; grep confirms the conformance check family does NOT consume ReferenceTrace (no ReferenceTrace/ReferenceRecorder hits under crates/utsushi-core/src/conformance), and the two shipping trace surfaces are utsushi_reallive::ReplayLog (CLI replay/replay-validate) and the fixture observation-hook events (CLI smoke). The reference recorder is therefore a third, orphaned trace surface with no consumer and no conformance golden role. Two parallel trace abstractions are not allowed (no-legacy-compat): pick the shipping ReplayLog/observation-hook surface and DELETE the reference recorder, UNLESS utsushi-cli-route-conformance-result wires a *ConformanceCheck whose golden input is a ReferenceTrace (in which case wire the recorder as that golden producer instead). Because that routing node was scoped to ReplayLog/ConformanceResult, the default outcome is DELETE.

Deliverables:
- Decision recorded in docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md: ReplayLog + observation-hook events are the canonical reference-trace surface; ReferenceRecorder is removed.
- Delete crates/utsushi-core/src/recorder/ (builder.rs, mod.rs, serialize.rs, sink_bridge.rs, trace.rs) and its lib.rs module declaration.
- Remove the Reference recorder block from substrate.rs and its row from docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md section 2 (and REFERENCE_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION from section 3).
nodes.acceptance: Either rg -n 'ReferenceRecorder|InMemoryReferenceRecorder|ReferenceTrace|REFERENCE_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION' crates docs returns no hits (deleted), OR utsushi-cli-route-conformance-result emits a ConformanceResult artifact whose golden input is a ReferenceTrace produced by a non-test ReferenceRecorder call (wired). docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md states which outcome was taken. just ci is green.
audit_focus: Exactly one canonical reference-trace surface remains after this lands (no two parallel trace abstractions)
If deleted: confirm no conformance golden silently depended on ReferenceTrace
Facade doc names the canonical trace surface explicitly

nodes.title: Decide and document the canonical runtime path (RuntimeAdapterRegistry vs EnginePort/Runner)
nodes.spec:
P2 decomposition (parent: utsushi-cli-bypasses-engineport-runner-substrate), node 1 of 4 and the gate for the rest. Establish the single canonical runtime architecture in writing before any code moves. Evidence of the current fork: (1) crates/utsushi-cli/src/main.rs drives capabilities/trace/capture/smoke through RuntimeAdapterRegistry + hand-rolled RuntimeAdapter impls (FixtureRuntimeAdapter, BrowserLaunchAdapter, NwjsLaunchAdapter); (2) replay.rs/replay_validate.rs drive utsushi_reallive::replay_scene directly, bypassing both abstractions; (3) the EnginePort/Runner/EnginePortAdapter/conformance substrate is implemented by FixtureEnginePort (real) and UtsushiReallivePort/UtsushiSiglusPort (stubs returning EnginePortError::Lifecycle on every method), and Runner only executes inside EnginePortAdapter::run (never instantiated in src) and tests. Key structural fact: EnginePortAdapter

already implements RuntimeAdapter (crates/utsushi-core/src/port/mod.rs:129), so the two are not symmetric competitors — RuntimeAdapter is the thin CLI dispatch seam and EnginePort/Runner is the canonical port substrate that should sit BEHIND it. Deliverables: - A new 'Canonical runtime path' section in docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md asserting: EnginePort + Runner is the canonical engine-port substrate; the RuntimeAdapter registry is the thin CLI dispatch seam and every registered adapter is an EnginePortAdapter; hand-rolled RuntimeAdapter impls and the standalone reallive replay_scene CLI driver are the non-canonical paths to be folded in or deleted. - An explicit scope table naming, for each of the three current paths, the resolution node (utsushi-cli-route-conformance-result, utsushi-delete-parallel-runtime-adapter, utsushi-reallive-replay-through-port) and whether it is wire / delete / defer. - A cross-reference to UTSUSHI-154 (FixtureRuntimeAdapter becomes EnginePortAdapter, the first product execution of Runner) and UTSUSHI-160 (first ConformanceManifest consumer).
nodes.acceptance: docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md contains a 'Canonical runtime path' section that (a) names EnginePort/Runner as canonical and RuntimeAdapter as the dispatch seam, (b) tabulates each of the three current runtime paths against its resolution node and a wire/delete/defer verdict, and (c) cross-references UTSUSHI-154 and UTSUSHI-160. No code change is required by this node.
nodes.status_reason:
[2026-06-29T14:15:50.048Z] Verification sign-off (manual): Audit PASS: §7.2 scope table assigns all 3 runtime paths a resolution node + wire/delete/defer verdict (RuntimeAdapter hand-rolled→delete node3, EnginePort/Runner→wire node2, engine-VM replay→defer node4); no 4th path. Evidence: .tmp/audit-utsushi-runtime-canonical-path-decision.json
Value: Confirm the scope table assigns every one of the three current runtime paths (RuntimeAdapterRegistry, EnginePort/Runner, reallive replay_scene) a resolution node and a wire/delete/defer verdict, with no fourth path left unmentioned.
Evidence: .tmp/audit-utsushi-runtime-canonical-path-decision.json

verification.value: Confirm the scope table assigns every one of the three current runtime paths (RuntimeAdapterRegistry, EnginePort/Runner, reallive replay_scene) a resolution node and a wire/delete/defer verdict, with no fourth path left unmentioned.

audit_focus: Exactly one path is declared canonical; the decision does not preserve both abstractions (no-legacy-compat)
The standalone reallive replay_scene CLI path is explicitly accounted for, not silently ignored
Decision is consistent with the existing EnginePortAdapter: RuntimeAdapter direction (adapter wraps port, not the reverse)

nodes.title: Route a CLI subcommand through Runner + a ConformanceCheck emitting a ConformanceResult artifact
nodes.spec:
P2 decomposition node 2 of 4, and the convergent consumer for the P1 conformance-check + state-diff slices (parent: utsushi-substrate-slices-no-production-consumer). This is the load-bearing wire: it gives the conformance check family its first non-test (product-binary) consumer and makes Runner-produced RunnerObservation/RunnerOutcome flow into a CLI artifact. Builds on UTSUSHI-154 (which rebases FixtureRuntimeAdapter onto EnginePortAdapter<FixtureEnginePort> so Runner executes in the shipping CLI trace/capture/smoke path). On top of that foundation, route one CLI subcommand so it drives a port through Runner and evaluates a *ConformanceCheck, writing a ConformanceResult-schema JSON. Use the fixture port (FixtureEnginePort) as the first real subject, since the reallive/siglus ports are still unimplemented stubs. Covers these P1 slices: ConformanceManifest, ConformanceResult, TraceConformanceCheck/SnapshotConformanceCheck/the *ConformanceCheck family, cross_validate_results_against_manifest, and state-diff (diff_snapshots — whose only consumer is SnapshotConformanceCheck) by having the routed ConformanceResult exercise a snapshot diff.

Deliverables:
- A CLI subcommand (e.g. `utsushi conform --adapter <name> ... --output <path>`) that builds a ConformanceManifest, runs the adapter through Runner, evaluates at least one *ConformanceCheck (including a SnapshotConformanceCheck that calls diff_snapshots), and writes a ConformanceResult-schema JSON artifact via write_json.
- crates/utsushi-cli/src imports EnginePort/Runner/ConformanceManifest/ConformanceResult (the substrate executes from the product binary, not a test).
- An end-to-end test invoking the subcommand against the fixture source and asserting the ConformanceResult schema fields.
nodes.acceptance: cargo run -p utsushi-cli -- conform --adapter <fixture> ... --output result.json writes a ConformanceResult-schema JSON whose contents include at least one ConformanceCheck outcome and one StateDiff (diff_snapshots) result. rg -n 'EnginePort|Runner|ConformanceManifest|ConformanceResult' crates/utsushi-cli/src is non-empty. rg -lw 'diff_snapshots|ConformanceManifest' crates/utsushi-cli/src returns a non-test src file. just ci is green.
audit_focus: ConformanceResult artifact is produced by the real product binary, not only an integration test (the P1 'non-test consumer' bar)
The routed path actually drives Runner (RunnerObservation/RunnerOutcome) rather than re-deriving results out of band
diff_snapshots / SnapshotConformanceCheck is genuinely exercised, not stubbed, so the state-diff slice gains a real consumer

nodes.title: Delete the non-canonical hand-rolled RuntimeAdapter path superseded by EnginePortAdapter
nodes.spec:
P2 decomposition node 3 of 4. Enforce no-legacy-compat once UTSUSHI-154 makes FixtureRuntimeAdapter an EnginePortAdapter<FixtureEnginePort>: delete the hand-rolled RuntimeAdapter impl for the fixture path so only the canonical EnginePort/Runner-backed adapter remains. Evidence: crates/utsushi-fixture/src/lib.rs:35-51 currently hand-rolls `impl RuntimeAdapter for FixtureRuntimeAdapter` (trace/capture/smoke) in parallel with crates/utsushi-fixture/src/engine_port.rs FixtureEnginePort; after UTSUSHI-154 the adapter must be a thin alias/newtype over EnginePortAdapter<FixtureEnginePort>, and the duplicate hand-rolled lifecycle code must be removed (not left behind a feature flag or #[deprecated]). The CLI registration in crates/utsushi-cli/src/main.rs:14-46 keeps registering FixtureRuntimeAdapter by the same NAME so trace/capture/smoke run unchanged through the canonical path.

Deliverables:
- Remove the hand-rolled `impl RuntimeAdapter for FixtureRuntimeAdapter` body in utsushi-fixture, leaving FixtureRuntimeAdapter as an alias/newtype delegating to EnginePortAdapter<FixtureEnginePort>.
- No dual trace/capture/smoke implementation remains in the crate (the EnginePort lifecycle is the single source).
- Existing utsushi-cli and utsushi-fixture goldens (smoke/trace/capture) pass unchanged through the canonical adapter.
nodes.acceptance: rg -n 'impl RuntimeAdapter for FixtureRuntimeAdapter' crates/utsushi-fixture/src returns no hits (the hand-rolled impl is gone). cargo test -p utsushi-cli and cargo test -p utsushi-fixture pass with unchanged trace/capture/smoke goldens. just ci is green.
audit_focus: No parallel hand-rolled trace/capture/smoke lifecycle survives alongside the EnginePort one
Deletion lands in the same change-set posture as the EnginePortAdapter replacement (no shim window)
CLI behaviour and goldens are byte-stable through the canonical adapter

nodes.title: Route the reallive replay CLI path through a real UtsushiReallivePort (deferred behind reallive port impl)
nodes.spec:
P2 decomposition node 4 of 4 — DEFER with rationale. The third runtime path is the standalone reallive VM driver: crates/utsushi-cli/src/replay.rs and replay_validate.rs call utsushi_reallive::{replay_scene, replay_until_first_pause, validate_log_contains} directly, bypassing EnginePort/Runner. The canonical-path decision says these should eventually run through UtsushiReallivePort::launch/observe driven by Runner. But UtsushiReallivePort is currently a stub (every lifecycle method returns EnginePortError::Lifecycle 'unimplemented: utsushi-reallive scaffold'), so the EnginePort replacement does not yet exist. no-legacy-compat forbids deleting the shipping replay_scene path before its replacement lands in the same change; therefore this node is DEFERRED until the reallive engine port is implemented (UTSUSHI-146 family). When that port is real, fold the replay/replay-validate driver into UtsushiReallivePort (replay_scene becomes the port's observe internals, surfaced through SinkSet/TextSurfaceSink) and delete the free-function CLI driver path in the same change.

Deliverables (when unblocked):
- UtsushiReallivePort::launch/observe drive the existing VM (replay_scene logic) and emit TextLine observations through its SinkSet.
- utsushi-cli replay/replay-validate dispatch through Runner + EnginePortAdapter<UtsushiReallivePort> instead of the free functions.
- The standalone utsushi_reallive::replay_scene/validate_log_contains free-function CLI entry is deleted in the same change.
- Until then, this node records the defer rationale and the blocking dependency in docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md.
nodes.acceptance: While blocked: docs/utsushi-substrate-facade.md names the reallive replay_scene path as deferred and cites UTSUSHI-146 as the unblocking dependency. When delivered: rg -n 'replay_scene|validate_log_contains' crates/utsushi-cli/src returns no hits and the replay/replay-validate subcommands drive EnginePortAdapter<UtsushiReallivePort> through Runner (rg -n 'UtsushiReallivePort|Runner' crates/utsushi-cli/src non-empty), with replay/replay-validate goldens unchanged.

verification.value: Confirm the reallive EnginePort is no longer a stub before claiming delivery; until then verify the defer rationale + UTSUSHI-146 dependency are recorded in the facade doc.

audit_focus: The shipping replay path is not deleted before its EnginePort replacement exists (no broken-command window)
When delivered, replay_scene is internalised into the port rather than left as a parallel free-function entry
Defer rationale is honest and time-bounded by the named UTSUSHI-146 dependency, not open-ended

nodes.title: itotori-db compose host port is fixed (55433), not per-worktree — concurrent worktree CI collides on db-up
nodes.spec: scripts/itotori-db-compose-env.mjs assigns a FIXED Postgres host port (55433, and 55444) rather than deriving it per-worktree (unlike CARGO_TARGET_DIR which IS per-worktree per AGENTS.md). So concurrent worktrees/agents running just db-up / qd-full-ci collide on the port — the source of the recurring 5s/30s DB-test timeout flakes seen across this session's parallel CI. qd-full-ci already reserves a unique port for its disposable Postgres; the gap is the plain just db-up / db-backed test path.
nodes.acceptance: The itotori-db compose host port is derived per-worktree (e.g. hashed from the worktree root like CARGO_TARGET_DIR, or an allocated free port) so >=2 worktrees can run db-backed tests / just db-up concurrently without port collision; a doc or test demonstrates two concurrent worktree db-up invocations succeed on distinct ports. No hardcoded shared 55433/55444 for the per-worktree path.
audit_focus: Fixed shared DB host port causing cross-worktree CI contention/flakes

nodes.title: Require-flag contract is not consumed by any CI/just target; enforcement is operator-manual
nodes.spec: grep for ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES across the repo (excluding crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests and target/) returns only the node's own definition in roadmap/spec-dag.json:34326,34344 — no justfile/nix/CI yaml sets the flag. Consequence: the false-green is eliminated only when an operator explicitly sets ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES=1. A bare `cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive -- --ignored` with env unset and the flag unset still early-returns and reports a green PASS (now preceded by a 'SKIP (no silent pass)' eprintln). This matches the node's stated acceptance (which only required the flag contract to exist and fail-loud under the flag), so it is NOT a regression against scope — recorded as a follow-up: wire ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES=1 into the CI/just target that stages Sweetie HD so the contract is actually enforced rather than dormant.

Observable: the relevant just/CI target exports ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES=1 when ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT is provided, and corpus-gated tests fail-loud if the corpus is missing in that context.

nodes.title: Fix OpenRouter single-provider-pin routing (no fallback) + prove a real live ZDR call
nodes.spec: No live-mode OpenRouter invocation has ever succeeded anywhere in the repo: every artifacts/localize-project/* run is project=fixture-alpha (sourceGame.gameId=fixture-reallive-game), the newest agentic-loop-bundle.v0.json has 0 attempts, `find artifacts -name provider-runs` returns nothing, and no run-summary has mode=live or cost>0. The intended primary DEV_PAIR (deepseek-v4-flash) 429'd from BOTH fireworks AND deepinfra (ITOTORI-239 spec, retry 7), so the chosen pair is unroutable. The single most load-bearing alpha gate (a real LIVE OpenRouter call with cost read from the real response) is undemonstrated. This node tracks PROVING the live path end to end. Related but not duplicated: UTSUSHI-231 (live localize-project run, still ready) and ALPHA-008 (sanitized proof bundle) are the delivery vehicles; this node is the falsifiable live-proof gate they must satisfy. Because DEV_PAIR 429s, the acceptance explicitly permits selecting an ALTERNATE routable (model,provider) pair from the account-wide ZDR allow-list rather than blocking on deepseek-v4-flash.
nodes.acceptance:
- The OpenRouter routing posture stops hard-pinning a single provider: `buildOpenRouterRoutingPosture` emits `allow_fallbacks:true` with `zdr:true` + `data_collection:"deny"` and maps the requested provider to `order:[providerId]` (a PREFERENCE), not `only:[providerId]`+`allow_fallbacks:false`. The `openRouterRoutingPostureFromBlock` invariant that throws when `allow_fallbacks!==false` is removed (it currently makes fallback impossible); replace with validation that `zdr===true` for private input. Per no-legacy-compat, the ITOTORI-220 "single deterministic provider pin / no fallback" justification in dev-pair.ts is deleted in the same change; DEV_PAIR becomes a dated preferred-provider + ZDR-fallback-set, not a single pin.
- A REAL live OpenRouter call succeeds end-to-end through the itotori provider path: mode=live, a provider-run artifact is written, real cost>0 is recorded from the actual call (not approximated), ZDR posture is honored, and router-metadata (openrouter_metadata.summary/attempts) is persisted to the ledger so future failures show whether fallback engaged. Proven against deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash with the ZDR-fallback posture (deepinfra/fireworks/etc. on the ZDR allow-list).
- The recorded ledger captures the (model, provider-actually-selected) pair + cost; sub-micro costs (e.g. ~$0.0000016) persist correctly (relies on the ITOTORI-232 amountUsd precision fix).

nodes.title: Cost-path comments/docstrings still name amountMicrosUsd as the cost-equality basis, contradicting the post-ITOTORI-232 amountUsd-authoritative body
nodes.spec: After ITOTORI-232, the verbatim decimal amountUsd is the authoritative cost compared at 1e-9 and amountMicrosUsd is a derived cap/telemetry mirror that CANNOT represent sub-micro costs (e.g. 0.00000602) — the ledger CHECK rejects the micros-rounded value. Four comment/doc sites still assert the obsolete micros-based contract and contradict their own code: (1) apps/itotori/src/providers/recorded.ts:34-36 module header ('usage.cost matching the captured ProviderCost.amountMicrosUsd'); (2) recorded.ts:652-656 docstring ('must equal ProviderCost.amountMicrosUsd to within 1e-9') vs body :692-699 comparing amountUsd; (3) apps/itotori/src/providers/types.ts:436-438 ProviderRunRecord doc asserting micros equality; (4) apps/itotori/src/providers/cost.ts:1-7 header ('we just convert it to integer micros'). These are the highest-reach cost-contract docs and invite reintroducing the sub-micro rounding loss the alpha cost gate forbids.
nodes.acceptance: All four sites describe amountUsd (verbatim decimal) as authoritative within 1e-9 and amountMicrosUsd as derived/cap-only: recorded.ts:34-36 header, recorded.ts:652-656 docstring, types.ts:436-438 ProviderRunRecord doc, and cost.ts:1-7 header. `rg -n 'amountMicrosUsd' apps/itotori/src/providers/recorded.ts apps/itotori/src/providers/types.ts apps/itotori/src/providers/cost.ts` shows no remaining claim that micros is the cost comparison/equality basis (only derived/cap/telemetry mentions). The recorded cost verification test still passes.
audit_focus: Cost-contract docs must match the amountUsd-authoritative body (post-ITOTORI-232)
No comment may invite reintroducing sub-micro rounding loss that the ledger CHECK rejects
Highest-reach type doc (types.ts) and module headers must carry the correct contract

nodes.title: Dead DecompressError::OutputOverflow variant with docs claiming it is emitted — unreachable twin of the g00 variant already removed in 867aead
nodes.spec: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/decompressor.rs declares DecompressError::OutputOverflow (variant doc :142-145 says it 'is emitted', fn doc :296-299 says 'an output overflow ... produce a typed DecompressError') but the back-reference branch clips the final run (`let remaining = declared_uncompressed_size - dst.len(); for i in 0..remaining`) under loop guard `while dst.len() < declared_uncompressed_size`, so dst can reach but never exceed declared size. `rg OutputOverflow decompressor.rs` shows only the enum def (:145) and Display arm (:189) — no Err(...OutputOverflow) construction site exists. Dead code the no-dead-code/no-legacy rule rejects.

nodes.title: Agentic structured output unroutable under ZDR (json_schema 404) — route via json_object
nodes.spec: Live testing of the ITOTORI-241 routing fix surfaced that `response_format: json_schema` (strict AND non-strict) is UNROUTABLE under ZDR for deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash: OpenRouter returns HTTP 404 "No endpoints found that can handle the requested parameters" because no ZDR-allow-list provider for that model advertises json_schema support, and `require_parameters:true` correctly narrows the routable pool to empty. The proven-routable deterministic structured mode under ZDR is `json_object` (served by Fireworks, billed $0.00001708 in the live proof). The existing `style-guide-provider-smoke --live` path and any agentic structured-output call that force `json_schema` therefore 404 in live ZDR mode today. Evidence: alpha-live EVIDENCE.json finding_json_schema_unroutable. This pre-dates the routing change but is only observable live.
nodes.acceptance: The agentic/structured-output path (style-guide + localization draft loop) uses a ZDR-routable structured mode (json_object) when the active pair's ZDR providers do not advertise json_schema — NO code path forces json_schema under require_parameters:true on a pair whose ZDR pool lacks it. A LIVE call exercising the agentic structured path succeeds under ZDR with real billed-cost evidence (costKind:billed, sanitized, no key) — i.e. style-guide-provider-smoke --live no longer 404s. If json_schema is used, it is gated on a per-pair capabilitySheet that is validated (not assumed) to have ZDR-provider support. Multi-game/multi-pair: the gating is pair-driven, not hardcoded to one provider. `pnpm --filter @itotori/app exec vitest run openrouter` green incl. a test asserting the structured-mode selection falls back to json_object when the pair's ZDR pool lacks json_schema.

nodes.title: pair_mismatch check rejects genuine ZDR fallback — accept any ZDR-allow-list provider
nodes.spec: The ITOTORI-220/236 post-response check throws `pair_mismatch` when the upstream provider != requested order[0]. With the ITOTORI-241 routing fix now setting allow_fallbacks:true, a GENUINE ZDR fallback (preferred provider 429s -> OpenRouter routes to the next ZDR-allow-list provider) would be REJECTED by that check, so end-to-end 429-resilience is not actually realized — the live proof only worked because the preferred provider served attempt=1 (no fallback fired). Root fix: reframe the post-response invariant from "upstream == order[0]" to "upstream ∈ ZDR allow-list (zdr:true enforces the privacy gate)". Must NOT widen into a silent provider swap — ITOTORI-238 silent-swap-forbidden still holds: any fallback must be recorded in adapterMetadata.openrouterRouting (attempt/summary already persisted).
nodes.acceptance: The post-response provider check ACCEPTS a response whose upstream provider is any member of the ZDR allow-list (privacy preserved via zdr:true), not only order[0]; it still REJECTS a non-ZDR-allow-list provider. A test simulates preferred-provider-429 -> fallback-to-second-ZDR-provider and asserts the response is accepted (no pair_mismatch throw) AND the fallback is recorded in adapterMetadata.openrouterRouting (attempt>1 / summary names the served provider) so the swap is auditable, not silent. The ITOTORI-238 silent-swap-forbidden invariant test still passes. `pnpm --filter @itotori/app exec vitest run openrouter` green.
nodes.status_reason: [2026-06-30T01:37:05.888Z] SUPERSEDED by remove-pair-mismatch-guard-accept-zdr-served-provider per Trevor's 2026-06-29 decision: the pair_mismatch guard is a needless formality (no operational security; ZDR enforced by posture). This node refined the guard to accept ZDR fallbacks, but the guard is now being REMOVED entirely + the helpers deleted. The refinement code is replaced by guard-removal (no-legacy).

nodes.title: ALPHA-006a: source the Sweetie HD alpha corpus through kaifuu-vault-source
nodes.spec:
WHAT: Wire the existing-but-unwired `kaifuu-vault-source` adapter into the alpha extraction entrypoint so the Oshioki Sweetie HD corpus is resolved through the read-only vault (catalog.db by-sha -> 7z extract to /scratch -> REALLIVEDATA tree) per docs/itotori-vault-source-adapter.md, instead of an operator-supplied raw filesystem path.

WHY: Re-measured on main, kaifuu-vault-source compiles as a workspace member but has ZERO non-test consumers (only its own crates/kaifuu-vault-source/tests/*.rs reference it). The alpha extract path (kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs:336 + resolve_reallive_seen_path main.rs:642-692, gated on ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT) hardcodes `<root>/REALLIVEDATA/Seen.txt` and never touches catalog.db/by-sha. Alpha must source through the curated read-only vault.

SCOPE/NO-LEGACY: the alpha SOURCING entrypoint (the suite localize / e2e corpus resolution) must go through the adapter; the bespoke vault-bypassing corpus-discovery it used before is deleted in the same change. The shared `--game-root` raw-path helper that serves env-gated unit tests is a separate, legitimate surface and is intentionally NOT removed here.
nodes.acceptance:
- A non-test source file in a production crate imports kaifuu_vault_source (adapter is wired, not merely compiled).
- The alpha extract resolves Sweetie HD by its catalog.db sha and yields a Seen.txt whose sha256 equals the bytes the prior REALLIVEDATA path produced -- proven against the real vault at /archive/vault, not a synthetic fixture.
- catalog.db and /archive/vault artifacts are opened strictly read-only; all extraction output lands under /scratch only (no writes under /archive/vault).
- kaifuu-vault-source build and its contract test-suite pass.
nodes.status_reason: Wiring complete + verified (kaifuu-cli main.rs:42 first non-test consumer; read-only; ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT fallback deleted; commit e908f79 on branch). Headline sha-equality proof BLOCKED by two read-only vault-curation-owned blockers: (1) catalog schema drift — adapter pins v1, live /archive/vault/catalog.db is v3 -> handled by precursor kaifuu-vault-source-schema-v3-support (internal edge); (2) EXTERNAL: Oshioki Sweetie HD is not in the vault by-sha content store — it exists only as a by-id repack (content sha 3f337d5b), and its catalog artifacts.sha256 (5fafb7f0) is the original_sha256 of a download that no longer exists in by-sha, so materialize_by_sha fails ArtifactMissing. The sibling vault-curation project must promote Sweetie HD into by-sha under a catalog sha that matches the by-sha file. Direct-path reference Seen.txt sha256=903f538b (test asserts equality, passes once both clear).

The sibling vault-curation project must promote Sweetie HD into by-sha under a catalog sha that matches the by-sha file. Direct-path reference Seen.txt sha256=903f538b (test asserts equality, passes once both clear).

nodes.title: ALPHA-006b: rasterized + redacted scene-1 screenshot for render-validate
nodes.spec:
WHAT: Make alpha render-validate emit an actual rasterized PNG screenshot of the localized Sweetie HD scene-1 text frame through the substrate frame sink (EvidenceTier >= E2), with a hard redaction guarantee that no copyrighted game-image (g00) pixels are embedded.

WHY: Re-measured on main, render/replay validation is TEXT-ONLY: utsushi-cli/src/replay_validate.rs only asserts an expected substring appears in a captured ReplayEvent::TextLine; utsushi-cli/src/main.rs:324-350 explicitly declares Screenshot/Recording `unsupported` and frame capture a dispatch-report, not a screenshot. A real headless rasterizer exists (utsushi-reallive/src/render_pipeline.rs, UTSUSHI-214) but it is synthetic, unwired to the substrate FrameArtifactSink (it emits in-crate at E1), and its GraphicsObjectKind::Image branch is a deliberate no-op. Alpha needs a real frame artifact.

NO-LEGACY: when the screenshot path replaces the text-only/dispatch-report capture stub for the alpha surface, the stubbed `unsupported` capture surface it supersedes is deleted, not left alongside.

NON-COPYRIGHT: the redaction is structural -- the rasterizer must continue to NOT dereference copyrighted g00 bitmaps into the framebuffer; only our own translated text layer + synthetic fills are painted. The emitted PNG must provably contain none of the source g00 byte content.
nodes.acceptance:
- Running render-validate on real Sweetie HD scene 1 writes a deterministic PNG artifact to disk (a real, hashable file under the artifacts/scratch dir), announced through the substrate FrameArtifactSink at E2 or higher.
- A test proves the emitted PNG embeds zero bytes from any source g00 asset (copyright-redaction), and that GraphicsObjectKind::Image is not dereferenced into the framebuffer.
- The PNG renders the localized (English) text layer for scene 1, not the Japanese source.
- The prior text-only/`unsupported` capture stub for the alpha surface is removed.

nodes.title: ALPHA-006c: prove bundle-driven patchback byte-correct + delete legacy apply_patches
nodes.spec:
WHAT (two coupled parts):
1. BYTE-FIDELITY: prove the alpha patchback output is byte-correct, not merely smaller. Re-measured on main, the alpha patch test crates/kaifuu-cli/tests/patch_real_sweetie_hd.rs asserts only (a) patched Seen.txt non-empty + >=80,000-byte directory, (b) re-parses with same scene count, (c) source sha256 unchanged. It does NOT assert per-scene byte-identity. Add assertions that every NON-translated scene blob is byte-identical to source after patch, that translated scenes keep their opcode headers intact (only text-body bytes change), and that the patched scene re-decompiles with ZERO new unknown opcodes.
2. NO-LEGACY DELETE: alpha uses the canonical bundle_driven::apply_translated_bundle (confirmed: kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs:488 via `patch --engine reallive`). The legacy `apply_patches`/`apply_slot_edit` surface (kaifuu-reallive/src/patchback/mod.rs:149,238; exported lib.rs:176) is still present and is structurally BROKEN for non-Textout roles (parser.rs sets byte_offset_within_scene to the opcode opener while byte_len=choice-text length, so apply_slot_edit splices over the opcode header). Per no-legacy-compat, delete apply_patches/apply_slot_edit, reroute the kaifuu-engine-fixture RealLive adapter (kaifuu-engine-fixture/src/lib.rs:4527) through bundle_driven, retire binary-patch-smoke's apply_patches use, and DELETE the already-#[ignore]'d broken test `synthetic_seen_txt_round_trips_with_length_preserving_edit` (binary_patch_smoke.rs:585). This subsumes candidate FIX-2.

WHY: a 0.547x-shrink-only check cannot catch silent structural corruption; and keeping a known-buggy legacy splice path alongside the canonical one is exactly the dual plumbing no-legacy forbids.

MULTI-GAME: byte-fidelity must hold on Sweetie HD AND a 2nd RealLive title (depends on fix-second-reallive-corpus).
nodes.acceptance:
- patch_real_sweetie_hd.rs (or a sibling real-bytes test) asserts: untranslated scenes byte-identical pre/post patch; translated scene opcode headers byte-identical (only text body differs); patched scene re-decompiles with 0 unknown opcodes.
- `apply_patches` and `apply_slot_edit` no longer exist anywhere under crates/*/src; the kaifuu-engine-fixture RealLive patch adapter and binary-patch-smoke route through bundle_driven::apply_translated_bundle.
- The broken `synthetic_seen_txt_round_trips_with_length_preserving_edit` test is deleted (not re-ignored).
- Byte-fidelity passes on Sweetie HD and a 2nd RealLive title.

nodes.title: ALPHA-006e: RealLive decompiler to 100% (full ExpressionPiece eval, 0 unknown opcodes)
nodes.spec:
WHAT: Complete the kaifuu-reallive Command-argument / ExpressionPiece evaluator so real RealLive scene bytecode decompiles with ZERO unknown opcodes and full expression evaluation, and raise the test floor to demand it.

WHY: Re-measured on main against the real Sweetie HD Seen.txt scene 1: total_opcodes=299, recognized=253, unknown=46 -> recognition_rate=84.62%. crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests/scene_1_dispatch_real_bytes.rs:258-265 asserts only `recognition_rate >= 0.75` -- a DEFECT floor that masks incomplete decompilation. The residual 46 unknown spans are dominated by byte-coalesced positions that cannot be aligned to a documented BytecodeElement boundary without a full ExpressionPiece evaluator (the comment at lines 246-257 admits this).

NO-LEGACY: the >=0.75 floor and its rationalizing comment are deleted in the same change as the 100% assertion; no second relaxed threshold remains.

MULTI-GAME: 100% (0 unknown) must hold on Sweetie HD AND a 2nd RealLive title (depends on fix-second-reallive-corpus); a single-game opcode catalogue does not generalize.
nodes.acceptance:
- On real Sweetie HD scene 1: unknown==0 / recognition_rate==100% via the full ExpressionPiece evaluator (every Unknown{opcode,...} span resolved to a typed BytecodeElement).
- crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests/scene_1_dispatch_real_bytes.rs asserts unknown==0 (or recognition_rate==1.0); the `>= 0.75` floor (line 259) and its rationalizing comment are DELETED; the test fn name (line ~56) no longer claims "90 percent" — it states 100%/0-unknown.
- No threshold relaxation remains anywhere in that test (no 0.75/0.9 floor).
- (Multi-game validation on a 2nd RealLive title is split to alpha-006e-multigame-validation, gated on FIX-4 — NOT required for this node.)

nodes.title: ALPHA-006d: Sweetie HD e2e (vault-extract -> 100%-decompile -> live-LLM localize -> byte-correct patch -> replay -> redacted screenshot)
nodes.spec:
WHAT: Integrate the alpha vertical end to end on real Sweetie HD bytes with exactly ONE live OpenRouter call. Re-wire the existing `just localize-sweetie-hd` chain (UTSUSHI-228, currently extract->translate->patch->replay-verify with text-only validation) to consume the new capabilities: source via kaifuu-vault-source (006a), decompile at 100%/0-unknown (006e), patch via byte-correct bundle_driven with the legacy path gone (006c), and render-validate to a redacted screenshot (006b).

WHY: the chain exists but on main it does not use the vault, decompiles at only 84.62%, has unproven patch byte-fidelity, validates text-only, and the 'translate' stage must be a real ZDR-routed OpenRouter call (model+provider pair, /generation cost telemetry) rather than a recorded stub.

This is the integration hub's concrete leaf: it produces the full alpha artifact set from real bytes + one real LLM call.
nodes.acceptance:
- A single suite command runs the full chain on the real Sweetie HD corpus and writes an artifact bundle containing: vault-sourced Seen.txt (sha logged), decompiled bundle with 0 unknown opcodes, a localized bundle produced by exactly ONE live OpenRouter call (recorded model id + provider id pair, /generation cost + ZDR metadata), a byte-correct patched Seen.txt, a replay text-match log, and a redacted scene-1 screenshot PNG.
- The live call is ZDR-routed and its real cost is recorded; the run fails closed if the provider is not ZDR.
- Replay text-match confirms the localized English line; screenshot contains no copyrighted g00 pixels.
- The chain consumes 006a/006b/006c/006e capabilities (no bypass back to the hardcoded path, the 84% decompile, the legacy patch, or text-only validation).
