nodes.title: FIX-1: structurally-faithful synthetic Seen.txt fixture (real opener-byte shape)
nodes.spec:
WHAT: Replace the unrealistic synthetic Seen.txt fixture/builder with one that is structurally faithful to real RealLive bytes: real post-KAIFUU-191 opcode opener-byte shape, real instruction/Command-arg layouts, real 10,000-slot 80,000-byte fixed-offset directory, and real AVG32 LZSS+XOR compression framing.

WHY: Re-measured on main, the binary_patch_smoke synthetic Seen.txt uses the pre-KAIFUU-191 0x23-opener shape (its round-trip test is #[ignore]'d for exactly this reason, binary_patch_smoke.rs:583). The KAIFUU-173/188 fixtures (tests/fixtures/README.md) are honest envelope-shape smokes but their bytecode bodies do not mirror the real opcode distribution. A faithful fixture lets non-real-bytes tests exercise the true parser shape.

NON-COPYRIGHT: authored from scratch from public RLDEV/format archaeology; real byte-counts/offsets/hashes of Sweetie HD MAY be referenced as numeric anchors, but NO verbatim retail bytecode or text is copied.

- No verbatim retail bytecode/text is committed; only numeric offsets/counts/hashes reference real bytes.
- The fixtures README documents the provenance (synthetic, CC0).

Verbatim retail bytes copied into fixture
Tautological fixture test (asserts nothing about parser shape)

WHY: utsushi-reallive g00 coverage leans on real-bytes tests (g00_real_bytes.rs, env-gated) plus thin in-module round-trips (g00.rs:1380). Alpha screenshot redaction (006b) needs a non-copyrighted, structurally-real g00 fixture to test the Image-no-op redaction path without retail assets.

NON-COPYRIGHT: synthetic, authored from public g00 format archaeology; real header/region byte-counts MAY be referenced numerically; NO verbatim retail pixel data copied.

- No verbatim retail g00 pixel bytes are committed.

nodes.title: FIX-4: source a 2nd real RealLive title via the vault for multi-game validation
nodes.spec:
WHAT: Identify and make available a SECOND real RealLive title (distinct from Oshioki Sweetie HD) via the kaifuu-vault-source adapter, and expose it to the real-bytes test harness (e.g. an ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT_2 corpus root) so multi-game validation can run.

WHY: multi-game-validation is project law: engine-family behavior must validate against >=2 real RealLive games. On main, /scratch/itotori-research holds only sweetie-hd as a RealLive title (the others are kirikiri/rpg-maker). 006c byte-fidelity and 006e 100%-decompile both need a 2nd RealLive corpus to prove the Sweetie-HD opcode/Choice layout generalizes.

NON-COPYRIGHT: the 2nd title's bytes stay read-only in the vault/scratch; nothing is copied into the repo.

RISK: if the vault does not yet hold a 2nd RealLive title, this depends on the sibling vault-curation project ingesting one; flag and coordinate rather than fabricating a corpus.

nodes.title: Bump kaifuu-vault-source catalog schema support v1->v3 (adapter cannot open live vault)
nodes.spec:
WHAT: Bump kaifuu-vault-source SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION from 1 to 3 (or accept a version range) after verifying the v3 catalog.db schema is query-compatible with the adapter's catalog queries.

WHY: ALPHA-006a re-measured that the adapter pins SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION=1 with exact-match, but the live read-only /archive/vault/catalog.db is schema_version=3. VaultSource::open fails immediately with CatalogSchemaUnsupported{observed:3,supported:1} — the adapter cannot open the CURRENT vault at all, blocking every vault-sourced path (006a sha-equality proof, FIX-4 2nd corpus, 006d e2e).

SCOPE: verify each column/table the adapter reads (works, releases, artifacts, by-sha/by-id stores) exists and is query-compatible in v3 vs v1; bump the supported version (exact 3, or a documented inclusive range) only after that verification. Do NOT widen blindly. This is necessary-but-not-sufficient for 006a (Sweetie HD by-sha promotion is a separate EXTERNAL vault-curation dependency).

READ-ONLY: /archive/vault opened strictly read-only; verification reads only.
nodes.acceptance:
- kaifuu-vault-source opens the live v3 /archive/vault/catalog.db without CatalogSchemaUnsupported (proven by an env-gated test that calls VaultSource::open against ITOTORI_VAULT_ROOT=/archive/vault and asserts Ok).
- Every adapter catalog query verified column-compatible with v3 (documented: which tables/columns, v1 vs v3 diff).
- SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION reflects the verified version(s); no blind widening.
- cargo build + test -p kaifuu-vault-source pass; clippy/fmt clean.

nodes.title: RecordingRuntimeAdapter capability contract overclaims FrameCapture/Screenshot vs its own capture()
nodes.spec: The test-only RecordingRuntimeAdapter capability contract (crates/utsushi-cli/src/main.rs:329-352) was flipped from partial/unsupported to supported/E2 for FrameCapture+Screenshot, citing the render-validate command. But that adapter's own capture() (main.rs:304-306) still returns only a JSON dispatch report — actual screenshots come from the SEPARATE render-validate subcommand, not through this adapter. So the adapter's capability declaration overclaims its own capture() surface. Honesty nit in a #[cfg(test)] fixture (not a shipped capability declaration), surfaced by the 006b audit. Fix: the RecordingRuntimeAdapter capability contract should describe what THAT adapter's capture() actually does (dispatch-report / partial), distinct from the render-validate screenshot surface; or the adapter's capture() should route through the E2 screenshot path so the declaration is true.
nodes.acceptance:
- RecordingRuntimeAdapter's capability declaration for FrameCapture/Screenshot accurately reflects its own capture() behavior (either downgraded to match the dispatch-report reality, or capture() is rewired to emit the E2 screenshot so supported/E2 is true).
- No test fixture asserts a capability the adapter's own method does not deliver.
- direnv exec . cargo test -p utsushi-cli green.

nodes.title: Re-validate 5 failover alternates' json_schema support under ITOTORI-241 ZDR posture (evidence is pre-241 only-pin)
nodes.spec: The localize-sweetie-hd pair-policy preset's five failover alternates (deepinfra, wafer, digitalocean, morph, atlas-cloud) still declare supportsStructuredOutputJsonSchema:true, each backed by ITOTORI-238/239 evidence refs that probed json_schema HTTP 200 under the OLD `only`-pinned single-provider posture. Under the new ITOTORI-241 posture (zdr + allow_fallbacks + order + require_parameters:true), the routable pool is the ZDR allow-list and json_schema-advertising endpoints may be excluded — the alpha-live EVIDENCE.json showed json_schema 404s for deepseek-v4-flash even on Fireworks. The agentic-json_object node fixed the PRIMARY pair (DEV_PAIR/fireworks -> jsonSchema:unsupported) and the selection mechanism is fully pair-driven, but the 5 alternates' capabilitySheets were NOT re-validated and may now be overclaiming json_schema support. Re-probe each alternate's json_schema support under the ITOTORI-241 ZDR posture (real live calls, sanitized) and flip any that 404 to jsonSchema:unsupported; the selector already honors the flag with no code change.
nodes.acceptance:
- Each of the 5 failover alternates' supportsStructuredOutputJsonSchema flag reflects a REAL probe under the ITOTORI-241 ZDR posture (not the old only-pin evidence): a sanitized live result (billed cost, no key/prompt/response bytes) per alternate, or a documented unsupported.
- Any alternate that 404s json_schema under ZDR is flipped to jsonSchema:unsupported in the pair-policy preset; its evidence ref updated to cite the ITOTORI-241-posture probe.
- pnpm --filter @itotori/app exec vitest run openrouter green; the failover chain selects json_object for any alternate whose ZDR pool lacks json_schema.

nodes.title: Remove pair_mismatch guard (needless formality); accept any ZDR-served provider + record real served pair
nodes.spec:
PRODUCT DECISION (Trevor, 2026-06-29): OpenRouter-side automatic fallback is the resilience mechanism; strict provider-pinning + the post-response pair_mismatch guard are a needless formality with NO operational security (ZDR is enforced by the request posture, so any ZDR-routed provider is a valid serve). Remove the formality.

WHAT:
1. DELETE the post-response pair_mismatch guard in apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts (the upstreamProvider != requested-provider throw, ~lines 305-320, plus the now-dead isAcceptedZdrFallback/openRouterFallbackRecorded helpers added by the pair-mismatch-check node which this SUPERSEDES). Instead, RECORD the real served (model, providerId) pair from the response (read whichever ZDR provider OpenRouter served) and extract the real billed cost from that response. Keep zdr:true/data_collection:deny/require_parameters:true posture and keep order+allow_fallbacks:true.
2. UPDATE the alpha verifier suite/scripts/localize-project/verify-artifacts.mjs (~lines 325-336) which still REQUIRES routingPosture.allow_fallbacks===false AND only===[providerId]. Change it to accept order:[...] + allow_fallbacks:true and to assert the recorded served-provider is a member of the ZDR allow-list (zdr:true), NOT that it equals a pinned provider.

WHY: the UTSUSHI-231 live run fail-closed with pair_mismatch because OpenRouter served SiliconFlow (a ZDR provider) when Fireworks was the order[0] preference. ZDR held (no privacy breach); the guard just broke a valid run. Removing it also fixes a cost-accounting leak (cost was zeroed because the rejection fired before cost extraction).

NO-LEGACY: delete the guard + its helpers, don't gate them behind a flag.
nodes.acceptance:
- The post-response pair_mismatch throw and its isAcceptedZdrFallback/openRouterFallbackRecorded helpers are DELETED from openrouter.ts; a ZDR-routed response from any provider is accepted and its real served (model, providerId) pair + real billed cost are recorded (costKind:"billed" from usage.cost, never zeroed by a rejection).
- verify-artifacts.mjs accepts routingPosture {order:[...], allow_fallbacks:true, zdr:true, data_collection:"deny", require_parameters:true} and asserts the served provider is ZDR-eligible (NOT == a pinned provider). It no longer requires allow_fallbacks===false or only===[providerId].
- A test proves a response served by a DIFFERENT ZDR provider than order[0] is accepted (no throw) and its served-pair + billed cost are recorded.
- pnpm --filter @itotori/app exec vitest run openrouter green; the suite localize-project verify-artifacts tests green.

nodes.title: localize-project-stage writes no telemetry ledger; telemetry-summary byPair is empty (alpha verifier fails)
nodes.spec:
UTSUSHI-231 second blocker: the alpha verifier verification command #2 (telemetry-summary --project sweetie-hd-alpha-1) reads the DB draft-attempt provider ledger, but localize-project-stage NEVER writes to it — the agentic loop is "pure of persistence side effects" (apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/agentic-loop.ts:217) and the stage builds its OpenRouter provider with only a file-artifact recorder, no DB ledger. So telemetry-summary returns empty byPair and verify-artifacts.mjs fails ("telemetry summary must include byPair evidence").

WHAT (pick the cleaner): EITHER wire the localize-project-stage provider to also persist each invocation to the DB draft-attempt/provider-run ledger (so telemetry-summary --project has real byPair rows), OR change verify-artifacts.mjs + the telemetry-summary source to read the per-run provider-run.json artifacts the stage DOES write (artifacts/localize-project/<RUN_DIR>/provider-runs/*.json) instead of the DB ledger. Whichever, telemetry-summary for the alpha run must reflect the REAL invocations with their real (model,provider) pairs + billed cost.
nodes.acceptance:

nodes.title: [audit-2] Command/expression argument scanner splits on raw '(' ')' ',' without honoring the 0xFF int-literal introducer, mis-terminating args and miscounting consumed bytes
nodes.spec:
WHY: decode_command scans the ArgList byte-by-byte (b')' closes, b',' splits) but never skips the 0xFF + 4-byte i32 LE literal payload that the same file decodes in first_arg_as_u16/u32 (517/531); a literal whose LE bytes contain 0x29/0x2C/0x28 closes/splits the arglist early, so `consumed` is wrong and pos+=consumed misaligns the rest of the scene stream — silent decompilation corruption, not an Unknown. advance_one_element in patchback mirrors the flaw.

SUGGESTED FIX: In both decode_command and advance_one_element, treat 0xFF as a 5-byte int-literal token (consume introducer + 4 payload bytes verbatim, never inspecting them for '(' ')' ','), matching the variant decoders' assumed encoding; add a synthetic test with a literal payload containing 0x29/0x2C.

SUGGESTED FIX: Make producer and consumer count identically — either emit a zero-length position for empty options in collect_text_unit_positions, or skip empty choice segments in the bridge; add a synthetic test with a `,,` choice.

nodes.title: [audit-2] draftAttemptProviderLedger.provider_id is NOT NULL with .default('unknown'), masking a missing pinned providerId
nodes.spec:
WHY: The column's own doc says 'required pinned providerId per the (modelId, providerId) pair rule' (ITOTORI-220), but `.default("unknown")` means any insert that omits the real served providerId silently records the fake pair ('model', 'unknown') as if it were real — a sentinel that masks missing real provider provenance, the exact no-fallback anti-pattern.

SUGGESTED FIX: Drop the .default('unknown') so an insert without a real providerId fails loudly; require callers to supply the served provider id (and backfill/migrate any legacy 'unknown' rows explicitly).

nodes.title: [audit-2] Schema-validation rule 'type' classified non-retryable, contradicting the spec/comment that type-coercion is retryable
nodes.spec:
WHY: MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD_RULES includes 'type', but translation-draft.ts emits rule 'type' for BOTH missing fields AND genuine type-coercion ('expected string'/'expected array'); so every recoverable coercion glitch is routed to manual triage instead of being retried, contradicting the header (15-18) and inline comment (155-156).

SUGGESTED FIX: Have the validator distinguish a missing-field rule ('required') from a wrong-type rule, or drop 'type' from the non-retryable set so coercion failures stay retryable per spec.

nodes.title: [audit-2] Patchback scene attribution adds a decompressed-bytecode cursor to a compressed-file offset; comment claims it uses 'only the file-offset half'
nodes.spec:
WHY: startByte = scene_blob_file_offset + unit.decompressed_byte_offset mixes the compressed file domain with a decompressed bytecode cursor; resolve_edit then locates the owning scene by containment within the compressed blob length, so any unit whose decompressed offset exceeds its scene's compressed byte_len resolves into a later scene → ProvenanceMismatch or wrong-scene splice. patchback_real_bytes only stays in range for Sweetie HD scene 1, masking it.

SUGGESTED FIX: Carry the scene's file offset (or scene-entry index) on the unit separately from the decompressed cursor and resolve the owning scene by that file offset alone; add a real-bytes patchback case over a scene whose decompressed size exceeds its compressed blob length.

nodes.title: [audit-2] Zero-dimension SCREENSIZE_MOD bypasses the MissingScreenSize guard and silently zeroes pointer dispatch
nodes.spec:
WHY: parse_screen_size accepts a present-but-zero width/height (e.g. SCREENSIZE_MOD=999,0,720) via u32::try_from, producing ScreenSize{width:0}; route_for_input_event only surfaces MissingScreenSize when screen_size is None, so a zero dimension bypasses the guard and pointer_to_pixel silently returns 0 for that axis, mis-routing or vanishing every pointer hot-region dispatch with no diagnostic.

SUGGESTED FIX: In parse_screen_size, return None (or a typed malformed error) when width==0 or height==0 so the existing MissingScreenSize path fires; add a test for SCREENSIZE_MOD with a zero dimension. Distinct from the prior absent-SCREENSIZE_MOD audit.
