nodes.title: Add served-provider cost breakdown to telemetry-summary (byPair keys on requested; surface real served split)
nodes.spec: Follow-up from localize-project-stage-telemetry-ledger-empty-bypair (telemetry Option B): the telemetry byPair aggregation keys on the REQUESTED (modelId, providerId) pair (for verify-artifacts.mjs startsWith compat + because OR returns served strings that differ by casing/version, e.g. Fireworks vs fireworks, deepseek-v4-flash-20260423). The REAL SERVED provider (upstreamProvider) + served model snapshot are preserved per-invocation in the provider-run.json artifact (asserted by the verifier's ZDR + billed-cost checks), but the byPair SUMMARY does not surface served-provider breakdown. Per the model-provider-pair law ("providers are not equivalent") + Trevor's record-the-real-served-provider decision, cost/quality analysis benefits from a byServedPair view. Consider adding a served-provider breakdown to telemetry-summary (alongside the requested-pair byPair), normalizing served strings (case/version) to canonical provider ids, so a run that OR-fallback-served via DigitalOcean(6)+Fireworks(5) shows the real per-served-provider cost split. Does NOT replace the requested-pair byPair (verifier depends on it); ADDS a served view.
nodes.acceptance:
- telemetry-summary reports a served-provider breakdown (real served upstreamProvider per invocation, normalized to canonical ids) IN ADDITION to the requested-pair byPair, with real billed cost split per served provider (verbatim usage.cost, never approximated).
- The existing verify-artifacts.mjs requested-pair byPair check still passes (served view is additive).
- A test on a multi-served-provider run (e.g. fixture mirroring the UTSUSHI-231 DigitalOcean+Fireworks split) asserts the served breakdown sums to the total billed cost.

nodes.title: Validate RealLive 100%-decompiler on a 2nd RealLive title (multi-game law)
nodes.spec: Multi-game validation of the RealLive 100% decompiler (split from alpha-006e-decompiler-100, which completes the ExpressionPiece evaluator + hits 0-unknown on Sweetie HD scene 1). Per the multi-game-validation law (engine-family behavior validates against >=2 real RealLive titles), confirm the completed evaluator reaches 0 unknown opcodes on a SECOND real RealLive title too (not just Sweetie HD), so the opcode/ExpressionPiece coverage generalizes. Gated on fix-second-reallive-corpus (a 2nd RealLive corpus via vault-curation) + alpha-006e-decompiler-100.
nodes.acceptance:
- The completed evaluator decompiles a 2nd real RealLive title (distinct from Sweetie HD) with unknown==0 / recognition_rate==100% on at least one real scene.
- An env-gated real-bytes test against the 2nd corpus (ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT_2) asserts 0 unknown.
- Confirms the single-game (Sweetie HD) opcode coverage generalizes; no game-specific special-casing masks an unknown.

nodes.title: Propagate OR-side-fallback decision to docs/presets/comments (still claim strict-pin)
nodes.spec:
The OR-side-fallback product decision (remove-pair-mismatch-guard-accept-zdr-served-provider, done) is NOT propagated to docs/presets/comments — they still present strict pinning as the model. Fix all of:
1. P1: docs/openrouter-integration.md §3.2/§3.3 (lines ~210-245, 146, 682) — the "canonical reference" still presents provider.only:[...] + allow_fallbacks:false as the CURRENT standard alpha posture ("the pair is the pin"). Rewrite to the live model: order:[providerId] + allow_fallbacks:true, zdr:true as the gate, real served provider recorded, pair_mismatch guard removed.
2. P2: presets/localize-project.pair-policy.json:68 notes — narrative still claims provider.only=[<alt>] / allow_fallbacks=false / "pair_mismatch ... silent provider swap forbidden" wire behavior the client no longer produces (the suite verifier now REQUIRES allow_fallbacks=true).
3. P3: packages/localization-bridge-schema/src/pair-policy.v0.3.ts:18 — still lists pair_mismatch as the leading must-surface failure mode after that guard was deleted; types.ts:550 documents provider-identity is no longer a failure axis.
nodes.acceptance:
- docs/openrouter-integration.md describes order+allow_fallbacks:true + zdr-gate + real-served-provider-recording as the current model; no remaining claim that only:[providerId]/allow_fallbacks:false is "the standard" or "the pin". rg confirms no stale strict-pin-as-current language.
- presets/localize-project.pair-policy.json notes match the current wire behavior (allow_fallbacks:true, no pair_mismatch silent-swap-forbidden claim).
- pair-policy.v0.3.ts:18 no longer lists pair_mismatch as a must-surface failure mode.
- A grep proves no doc/preset/comment presents the removed pair_mismatch guard or strict pin as current behavior.

verification.value: ! rg -q 'allow_fallbacks.*false|the pair is the pin|only:\[providerId\]' docs/openrouter-integration.md

nodes.title: Remove app-level 429-failover loop now redundant with OR-side fallback (or justify)
nodes.spec: DAG-OR-3 (repo-wide audit): apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/localize-project-stage-command.ts runs an APP-LEVEL manual 429-failover loop (failoverPredicate=http_429_from_primary over pair-policy alternates, presets/localize-project.pair-policy.json:65) that now COEXISTS with OpenRouter-side automatic fallback (post-ITOTORI-241: order+allow_fallbacks:true, OR routes within ZDR allow-list on 429). With OR handling 429s (UTSUSHI-231 live run: OR served DigitalOcean on fallback, run completed), the app-level alternate-chaining is the now-superseded ITOTORI-239/240 approach and is redundant (and could double-handle a 429 OR already resolved). Decide the layering explicitly: per Trevor's decision (OR-side fallback is the resilience mechanism), REMOVE the redundant app-level failoverPredicate/alternate-chaining loop (no-legacy), keeping the single pair-policy primary; OR document precisely why a thin app-level retry is still needed on top of OR fallback (e.g. all-ZDR-providers-429). Default to removal unless a concrete gap is shown.
nodes.acceptance:
- The app-level 429-failover/alternate-chaining loop in localize-project-stage-command.ts is removed (OR-side fallback handles 429s), OR a documented rationale + test justifies a minimal app-level retry layered on OR fallback.
- No-legacy: no dual failover machinery; the superseded ITOTORI-239/240 alternate-chaining is gone.
- A live or recorded run still completes under a primary-provider 429 (OR fallback serves an alternate ZDR provider), proving resilience without the app-level loop.
- pnpm --filter @itotori/app exec vitest run localize-project-stage green.

nodes.title: Bridge surfaces binary catch-all Textout as translatable dialogue (patchback would corrupt data table)
nodes.spec: From the alpha-006e-decompiler-100 audit: the bridge maps EVERY Textout element to surface_kind="dialogue" (crates/kaifuu-reallive/src/bridge.rs:237-258). After 006e's rlvm-faithful "non-structural byte -> Textout catch-all", some Textout runs are actually EMBEDDED BINARY DATA, not readable Shift-JIS dialogue (e.g. Sweetie HD scene 1 op[72] = 214 bytes of periodic 21-byte binary records, printable=false, sitting after a 2nd MetaEntrypoint). The bridge surfaces these as translatable dialogue units (the patchback test sentinel-replaces all 4 textouts). The archive round-trips, but a real translate+patchback run would OVERWRITE the binary data table -> corruption. Fix: the dialogue-surface selection must distinguish readable SJIS-text Textout (real dialogue, translatable) from binary/non-printable Textout runs (data, NOT translatable) — only surface real dialogue as a translatable unit; exclude/preserve binary catch-all runs verbatim through patchback.
nodes.acceptance:
- The bridge surfaces only readable Shift-JIS Textout runs as translatable dialogue units; non-printable/binary catch-all Textout runs are NOT surfaced as translatable and are preserved verbatim through patchback (a translate+patchback run does not overwrite the binary data table).
- A test (real or realistic bytes) proves: a binary Textout run (e.g. the Sweetie HD scene-1 214B data block) is excluded from translatable units and survives patchback byte-identical, while a real SJIS dialogue Textout is surfaced + translatable.
- direnv exec . cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive green; multi-game safety noted (a dialogue-bearing scene/2nd title is the real validator).
audit_focus: A binary/non-printable Textout run is NOT surfaced as translatable and survives patchback byte-identical
Real SJIS dialogue IS still surfaced + translatable (no false-negative)
No-legacy: single surface-selection path

findings.title: Choice-index misalignment when args contain non-Bytes variants
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/rlop/module_sel.rs:383-407 — when an ExprValue::Int arg appears mid-list, `continue` skips it from the stored `choices` Vec but the emitted `text_surface = "choice:<idx>"` uses the original argument index. A user pick maps cleanly to `set_store` (which the code does correctly) but the queued `SelectLongOp::choices()` no longer aligns with the index. Real-bytes RealLive `select_*` always pass Shift-JIS Bytes so this path is synthetic-test-only; still worth flagging as a follow-up cleanup.

findings.title: AC#2 literal wording ("deducts cache discount before refusing") and implementation (does NOT subtract — treats usage.cost as already-net) point opposite directions; orchestrator should reconcile the AC text with the DOC-AMBIGUOUS-6 / §5.3 treaty before merge so a future reader doesn't read AC#2 as "current code is wrong."
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts:1615-1631 — recordSpend adds cost.amountMicrosUsd verbatim and explicitly comments "we do NOT subtract cost.cacheDiscountMicrosUsd again — that would double-count the discount"; spec acceptance #2 reads "Per-process cost cap deducts cache discount before refusing a new call." docs/openrouter-integration.md:374-379 anchors the no-subtract posture ("treat usage.cost as authoritative billed cost; never recompute"). Implementation is correct per the doc treaty; only the AC text reads ambiguously.

findings.title: Stale doc comment on AdapterCapabilities::level_matrix still references deleted with_level_matrix builder
findings.evidence: crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs:381-385 — field doc says 'When `new` is called without `with_level_matrix`, this is conservatively derived from `reports` via [`AdapterCapabilityMatrix::derive_from_reports`]. Detectors should declare it explicitly...' This contradicts the new invariant from the constructor docstring at lib.rs:396-408 ('every adapter MUST declare its 4-rung AdapterCapabilityMatrix at construction. There is no silent fallback'). The `with_level_matrix` builder has been deleted; there is no longer a 'without with_level_matrix' construction path. The conservative-derivation description is also misleading now that the matrix is a required constructor arg.

findings.title: recordOutcome throws RepairJobServiceError with the wrong `code` field ('missing_pair') when the actual problem is 'job not in-flight'
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/repair/repair-job-service.ts:172-176 — code:"missing_pair" is hard-coded for the not-in-flight branch; the typed code is one of the three the class advertises, and a caller switching on err.code would mis-route the error. The message text is correct but the structured code is wrong. The RepairJobServiceError union should add an 'unknown_job_id' (or 'not_in_flight') variant and be used here.

findings.title: drop() silently no-ops on unknown jobIds; no history event recorded for a typo'd drop call
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/repair/repair-job-service.ts:189-199 — if the jobId is neither queued nor in-flight, the function returns without throwing AND without appending a history event. A future caller that mistypes a job id loses the operation silently. Acceptable for a skeleton, but a typed 'unknown_job_id' error (or an explicit job_dropped event with reason='unknown_job_id') would close the silent-failure mode.

findings.title: parentJobId is accepted verbatim without verifying the parent exists in this service's history
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/repair/repair-job-service.ts:117-148 — enqueue() copies input.parentJobId onto the RepairJob without checking it points at a job this service ever saw. A typo or a stale id silently breaks the repair-chain provenance the parentJobId field is supposed to provide (types.ts:163-164 doc-comment claims the chain lets the dashboard 'render the repair tree without re-walking the trigger log' — but the chain may dangle).

findings.title: human_decision project scope returns affectedBridgeUnitIds: [] — downstream loops over the array will rerun nothing instead of everything
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/repair/affected-work-selector.ts:138-147 — when scope.kind === 'project' the selector returns affectedBridgeUnitIds: []. The contract is that consumers must check affectedScope === 'project' separately and enumerate the project from elsewhere. A naive consumer that iterates affectedBridgeUnitIds will silently no-op. Documented behavior, but is a footgun the skeleton could close by either (a) requiring the human decision to enumerate units explicitly, or (b) widening the AffectedWorkSelection type so 'project' is a distinct variant.

findings.title: Deterministic jobId minting collides across distinct service instances when the same trigger arrives at the same counter slot
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/repair/repair-job-service.ts:238-245 — the seed is `${trigger.trigger}|${triggerStableKey(trigger)}|${counter}` and the counter resets to 0 on every new RepairJobService instance. Two services that ingest overlapping triggers in parallel produce identical jobIds. The test 'mints stable job ids deterministic in trigger + counter' relies on this for replay determinism, but a future caller merging histories from two services will see jobId collisions. Either add a service-instance entropy seed OR document the single-service-per-process invariant in the public RepairJobService docs.

findings.title: history snapshot returned by repairHistory() shares the same RepairJob reference embedded in job_enqueued events; a caller that mutates the returned job retroactively edits history
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/repair/repair-job-service.ts:146 (push embeds the same job object the caller received) + line 215 (history.slice() shallow-copies the array but not the events). TypeScript's readonly types prevent normal mutation, but the structural append-only guarantee relies on the type system, not on the runtime. Append-only-history claim in the spec would be more robust with structuredClone on push or freeze on emit.

findings.title: assertSeverity runtime guard for missing enum entries is dead code (the order table is Record<RepairJobSeverity, number>, so TS rejects missing keys at compile time)
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/orchestrator/repair/repair-job-service.ts:271-281 — the for-of loop over REPAIR_JOB_SEVERITIES checks each known severity is in `order`, but the Record type already forces this at the type level. The loop runs on every enqueue and never trips. Minor gold-plating; remove or convert to a static once-at-construction assertion.

findings.title: Fixture repair loop deliverable is realized as an in-file test fixture, not a JSON fixture under fixtures/
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/test/repair-job-service.test.ts:410-526 — the 'ITOTORI-038 fixture repair loop' describe block builds synthetic triggers inline. There is no fixtures/repair-* file (find fixtures/ apps/itotori/test/fixtures/ -name '*repair*' returns nothing). Verification config lists 'Repair-loop fixture review' as the manual check, so an in-test fixture is plausibly sufficient for a skeleton, but a real on-disk fixture would let downstream nodes (e.g. ITOTORI-222's repair stage) replay the exact trigger set without re-instantiating the service.

findings.title: importRuntimeFeedback contract docstring claims persisted-vs-supplied evidenceTier comparison, but implementation only does shape checks
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/action-service.ts:104-115,183-202 — docstring 'the service asserts the supplied value matches the persisted value on the item. This is the explicit hand-off contract that prevents runtime evidence feedback from losing its tier' is not enforced; importRuntimeFeedback() only calls assertNonEmpty/assertNonEmptyArray and never fetches the item to compare against persisted evidenceTier/observationEventIds/artifactHashes. A caller can supply a different tier than the persisted one and the transition metadata records the mismatched value (the item row remains correct due to the SQL discriminant).

findings.title: Allowed-transition graph silently drops repair_requested -> in_review and in_review -> pending (un-claim)
findings.evidence: packages/itotori-db/src/repositories/reviewer-queue-repository.ts:176-192 — repair_requested can go to {pending, accepted, rejected} but not back to in_review; in_review has no path back to pending (cannot un-claim). Design choice, but unstated in spec; reviewer who claims an item and then needs to release it has no path other than escalate/accept/reject.

findings.title: Optimistic-lock collision returned as invalid_transition obscures concurrency from the dashboard
findings.evidence: packages/itotori-db/src/repositories/reviewer-queue-repository.ts:396-405 — when the UPDATE WHERE state=existing.state matches 0 rows (concurrent transition), the repo throws reviewer_queue_item_invalid_transition rather than a distinct concurrency code. Caller cannot distinguish 'someone else won the race; retry' from 'genuinely illegal edge'. Not a partial-state bug (the SELECT/UPDATE/INSERT are inside a tx and the UPDATE simply didn't match), but the conflated error code reduces the diagnostic precision the spec wanted ('Denied, stale, duplicate, and invalid-transition actions return semantic diagnostics').

findings.title: Spec doc-comment promises 'typed error' on LZSS length mismatch but implementation surfaces a warning + partial buffer
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/g00.rs:83-85 (doc says 'surfaces a typed error rather than silently truncating') vs g00.rs:929-993 (lzss_decode_classic breaks the loop on EOF and returns Ok((partial_buffer, Some(G00Warning::PayloadLengthMismatch)))). The unit test lzss_classic_short_stream_emits_length_warning_not_silent_pass at g00.rs:1119-1144 confirms the warning behavior, but the prose contract reads as Err. Tighten one or the other so doc, audit-focus item, and behavior agree.

findings.title: G00DecodeError::UnexpectedEndOfStream variant is defined and Displayed but is never constructed
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/g00.rs:362-369 (variant) and g00.rs:422-431 (Display arm). The LZSS decoder treats input exhaustion as a warning at g00.rs:931-935 / 946-948 / 955-957 and breaks; no code path raises UnexpectedEndOfStream. Either wire a hard-error path for the mid-token EOF case or drop the variant.

findings.title: G00DecodeError::OutputOverflow is unreachable because the back-reference inner loop breaks at dst.len() >= uncompressed_size
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/g00.rs:962-971 inner loop breaks on >= ; g00.rs:975-981 post-loop check fires on > only. The break guarantees dst.len() == uncompressed_size, so the check is dead. Either drop the variant or relax the inner break so the defensive check has a real path.

findings.title: parse_lzss_section silently clamps a malformed compressed_size < 8 into a 0-byte payload
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/g00.rs:849-868: declared_payload_end = preamble_off + compressed_size; payload_end = declared_payload_end.min(input.len()).max(payload_start). When compressed_size < 8 (i.e. declared_payload_end < payload_start), .max(payload_start) hides the malformed header and produces an empty payload that decodes to PayloadLengthMismatch warning instead of a typed TruncatedHeader / malformed-size error.

findings.title: Type-2 decode does not pad/truncate decoded pixel buffer to width*height*4 (asymmetric with type-0)
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/g00.rs:807-816 (decode_type2 returns raw lzss output as pixels_rgba). decode_type0 at g00.rs:647-651 calls pad_or_truncate to width*height*4. Documented as 'type-2 may be an atlas of regions, not a flat canvas' (g00.rs:274-279) so semantically intentional, but the asymmetry is worth noting since acceptance criterion 3 only pins regions usability and the audit-focus 'BGR-as-RGB silently' check skips any 1-3 byte tail via chunks_exact_mut(4).

findings.title: Missing SCREENSIZE_MOD silently disables pointer dispatch — SYSCALL_MISSING_SCREEN_SIZE_CODE never emitted
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/syscall.rs:87-91 (constant declared with doc 'emitted when SCREENSIZE_MOD is missing') and crates/utsushi-reallive/src/syscall.rs:507-511 (route_for_input_event uses `let screen = self.screen_size?;` which returns None silently). The Pointer match arm never logs/warns/errors when screen_size is None — pointer events vanish without diagnostic. Constant SYSCALL_MISSING_SCREEN_SIZE_CODE is `pub` and re-exported from lib.rs:177-181 but no call site emits it. grep returns one hit (the declaration).

findings.title: MOUSEACTIONCALL namespace scan breaks on first gap after index 0 — non-contiguous indices silently dropped
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/syscall.rs:377-379: `if index > 0 && gameexe.get(&area_key).is_none() { break; }` — the comment at 372-376 claims 'the namespace is enumerated by checking each slot, not by trusting a last index sentinel', but the break statement is exactly a last-index sentinel. A Gameexe with MOUSEACTIONCALL.000.SEEN and MOUSEACTIONCALL.002.SEEN (gap at 001) would silently drop MOUSEACTIONCALL.002 because index=1 has neither SEEN nor AREA. Sweetie HD is contiguous so the regression is not observable in the corpus, but the audit-focus pin 'pretends to pass' is at risk for sister RealLive titles.

findings.title: WBCALL hard-capped at 8 slots — RealLive titles declaring WBCALL.008+ would be silently ignored
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/syscall.rs:96 (`WBCALL_SLOT_COUNT: u8 = 8`) and 419 (`for index in 0..WBCALL_SLOT_COUNT`). Sweetie HD declares WBCALL.000-007 exactly so the cap matches the corpus, but RLDEV documents WBCALL as a larger namespace (≤64). No diagnostic emitted if WBCALL.008+ keys are present. Acceptable for alpha (Sweetie HD only) but worth flagging for the ≥2-game beta multi-game-validation rule.

findings.title: invoke() debug_assert fallback path silently advances on unexpected FarcallOp outcome
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/syscall.rs:556-572: if FarcallOp.dispatch returns something other than DispatchOutcome::FarCall, the debug_assert fires only in debug builds; in release the outcome is still forwarded to apply_dispatch_outcome. Given the inline-constructed args are always 4 well-typed ExprValue::Int values, FarcallOp cannot currently return Advance, so the branch is dead today. Risk surfaces if a future FarcallOp variant adds a typed failure mode — release builds would silently swallow it.

findings.title: entry_count with EXAFTERCALL_MOD=0 not directly asserted (only kind-count for the real bytes path)
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/src/syscall.rs:694-708 asserts entry_count=15 only when EXAFTERCALL_MOD=1. The real-bytes (and synthetic-mirror) path with EXAFTERCALL_MOD=0 asserts route_count = SYSCALL_KIND_COUNT - 1 (= 7 kinds) but does not pin entry_count = 14. A regression that double-adds a route in the EXAFTERCALL=0 case would slip through. Minor — the kind-count assertion is the load-bearing acceptance check.

findings.title: Missing engine-specific boundary notes for UTSUSHI-212, UTSUSHI-214, UTSUSHI-215
findings.evidence: docs/research/reallive-engine.md:992-1037 §M.3 enumerates boundary notes for UTSUSHI-201/203/207/209/211/216/217/218 but omits UTSUSHI-212 (string/memory ops with Shift-JIS hantozen/Uppercase semantics, rng/SnapshotStore round-trip — RealLive-specific Shift-JIS half/full-width conversions per RLDEV), UTSUSHI-214 (rlvm-derived 256-graphics-object stack model), and UTSUSHI-215 (rlvm module_grp / module_obj_* opcode catalogue: allocDC, wipe, shake, fade, objAlloc, objSetPos byte-codes). Each of these UTSUSHI-2xx node specs (qd node show UTSUSHI-212/214/215) carries ACs that name RealLive-specific byte layouts / opcode names which would break under a Siglus reuse claim. AC3 wording: 'Any UTSUSHI-200..UTSUSHI-220 node whose acceptance criterion would break under a Siglus reuse claim emits a documented engine-specific boundary note'.

findings.title: M.1 'Reusable' anchors cite substrate types not consumed by the inline Siglus scaffold
findings.evidence: docs/research/reallive-engine.md:910-948 §M.1 table names SnapshotStore, TextSurfaceSink, AudioEventSink, FrameArtifactSink, Inspectable, Restorable, ReplayLog, LogicalClockTick, StateTree, StatePath, ChoiceIndex as substrate-facade carriers proving reusability. crates/utsushi-reallive/tests/cross_engine_facade_only_imports.rs:62-67 inline Siglus scaffold imports only the bare scaffold-contract subset (no SnapshotStore, no TextSurfaceSink, no Inspectable/Restorable, no ReplayLog, no StateTree/StatePath, no ChoiceIndex). The audit-prompt criterion 'verify each reusable anchor in Appendix M.1 cites a concrete substrate type the inline Siglus scaffold imports' is therefore strictly satisfied only for: AssetPackage, EnginePort, PortManifest, REQUIRED_LIFECYCLE_STAGES, SinkSet, EvidenceTier, FidelityTier, LifecycleStage, PortCapability. Substrate facade DOES export the deeper anchors (verified at crates/utsushi-core/src/substrate.rs:28-48), so the reusability claim holds at the substrate-API surface, but it is proven by RealLive's consumption + facade existence rather than by inline-Siglus consumption. The §M.4 'scaffold-contract level' caveat and the test's SUBSET-not-EQUALS posture (cross_engine_facade_only_imports.rs:580-594) disclose this stance honestly.

findings.title: Inline Siglus scaffold is inert by design — 'second engine family' is contract-level only
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/tests/cross_engine_facade_only_imports.rs:80-87, 162-166: scaffold is single-file, lifecycle methods all return EnginePortError::Lifecycle{Unimplemented}, MANIFEST.limitations explicitly says 'every lifecycle method returns a typed Lifecycle error' and 'Real Siglus VM behaviour is out of alpha scope'. Per feedback_multi_game_validation.md, substrate work means ≥2 engine families with ≥2 real games per family. The inline scaffold satisfies the ≥2-families requirement only at the scaffold-contract level; no Siglus real-bytes evidence is exercised. The spec explicitly acknowledges this is intentional ('the alpha tier targets a single engine family, RealLive against Sweetie HD; the full Siglus VM stays research-only') and the substrate-API extensibility claim is the load-bearing one for UTSUSHI-221.

findings.title: Appendix M.2 mentions 'module_jmp' under M.2 table but no M.3 boundary note pin
findings.evidence: docs/research/reallive-engine.md §M.2 row 'UTSUSHI-210 module_jmp (control-flow)' lists goto_if/goto_unless/goto_on/gosub/farcall as RealLive-only opcode byte-codes, but §M.3 has no UTSUSHI-210 boundary-note entry. UTSUSHI-210 ACs naming these opcode bytes would break under a Siglus reuse claim per the same AC3 wording.

findings.title: Appendix M.2 mentions UTSUSHI-213 system-call dispatch but no M.3 boundary note pin
findings.evidence: docs/research/reallive-engine.md §M.2 row 'UTSUSHI-213 system-call dispatch' notes RealLive-only (9999, 10)-style scene-id+entrypoint Gameexe convention, but §M.3 has no UTSUSHI-213 boundary-note entry. UTSUSHI-213 ACs would break under Siglus reuse.

findings.title: apply has no runtime guard against partial-sourced patches
findings.evidence: crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs:172-183 (`apply` arm) and crates/kaifuu-delta/src/lib.rs:192-219 (`apply_delta`): no inspection of any `partial` field, no provenance tag carried through `DeltaPackage`. The PR doc (docs/subprojects-kaifuu.md:153) asserts "`apply` MUST refuse to ingest any envelope whose `partial` field is `true`" but no code path enforces it. The patch input to `apply` is a `DeltaPackage` produced from extract→translate→diff, so even if the extract was partial, the partial flag is lost by `apply` time. audit_focus[0] ("partial output must not be confused with a complete extract by downstream apply/verify") is doc-only.

findings.title: Acceptance criterion's `status: partial` field is missing from the envelope
findings.evidence: Spec acceptance #3: `kaifuu verify <Sweetie HD>` ... "exits 0 with `status: partial`". PartialAdapterReport (crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs:13388-13402) has no `status` field; the envelope encodes partial-ness via the boolean `partial: true` plus `detected: false` (test assertions match this at crates/kaifuu-cli/tests/partial_extract.rs:225-227). Functionally equivalent for dashboard ingestion but literal acceptance language is unmet.

findings.title: report_id is a constant per kind/index pair, not per-run
findings.evidence: crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs:13510 — `report_id: deterministic_id("kaifuu-partial-adapter", 193)` always returns the same string (`deterministic_id` at lib.rs:14944 is purely a function of inputs). Schema-stable but not unique-per-run; dashboard de-duplication by reportId would collapse independent partial runs. Consistent with existing project patterns elsewhere in the codebase.

findings.title: Unrelated changes bundled into UTSUSHI-218 commit (provider edits + 322-line audit-doc deletion)
findings.evidence: Commit db79086 modifies apps/itotori/src/providers/dev-pair.ts (DEFAULT_COST_CAP_USD comment $0.5 -> $1.00, ~600 -> >1000 calls), apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts (`deepseek-v4-flash` -> `deepseek-v3.2-exp`), and deletes docs/audits/code-audit-2026-06-26-mints.json (47 lines) plus docs/audits/code-audit-2026-06-26.md (275 lines). None of these relate to the AVG-derived save format. The audit docs contained pending KAIFUU/UTSUSHI/SHARED-300 mint proposals (P2/P3) that may be lost silently. This violates 'one node = one change' hygiene and the `feedback_no_legacy_compat` posture's intent of clean per-node landings.

findings.title: Spec acceptance text writes Shift-JIS title trailing space as \u{8140} but implementation/test use \u{3000}
findings.evidence: Spec acceptance criterion writes `"オシオキSweetie＋Sweets!! HD Edition\u{8140}"`. The literal codepoint U+8140 is a Han ideograph (腀), which would be wrong. The two bytes `81 40` are the Shift-JIS encoding of U+3000 IDEOGRAPHIC SPACE; the implementation correctly decodes via encoding_rs::SHIFT_JIS to `\u{3000}` (src/save.rs:411-413 doc comment + tests/save_real_sweetie_hd.rs:72 + tests/save_synthetic.rs:57). The implementation is correct; the spec text is wrong/misleading. The acceptance assertion as literally worded would fail, but the spirit (round-trip of the Shift-JIS title) is met. Recommend amending the spec text rather than the code.

findings.title: Real-bytes test silently no-ops when KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH unset
findings.evidence: tests/save_real_sweetie_hd.rs:83-92 (load_or_skip) returns None when the env var is absent and the test bodies eprintln + return Ok. The three spec verification commands (`cargo test -p utsushi-reallive save_reads_avg_system_save` etc.) will report `passed` even with zero assertions executed. CI that does not set the env will produce false-green coverage. This is consistent with existing real-bytes test patterns in the crate (e.g. replay_validate_real_sweetie_hd.rs) but worth pinning explicitly.

findings.title: Doc claims writable-path KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH override is rejected, but no runtime check exists
findings.evidence: tests/save_real_sweetie_hd.rs:25-27 doc-comment states 'the test does not accept a KAIFUU_REAL_SWEETIE_HD_PATH override that points at a writable directory — every read goes through the path the audit doc declares, with no fall-back.' However, resolve_savedata_path (line 74) just consumes the env var as-is without any writability check or path equality assertion. Since the test has zero write calls, the practical risk is nil, but the doc-vs-code mismatch is a maintainability hazard.

findings.title: EvidenceTier import in test bypasses the substrate facade
findings.evidence: tests/save_synthetic.rs:25 uses `use utsushi_core::EvidenceTier;` (root path) rather than `utsushi_core::substrate::EvidenceTier`. EvidenceTier is re-exported via the substrate facade (utsushi-core/src/substrate.rs:93) so the facade-discipline rule is satisfied logically but the import-site grep would flag it. lib.rs documents a known exception for CaptureOutcome with a written justification; EvidenceTier here has no such justification.

findings.title: GlobalSave / ReadFlags do not validate file size against any cross-check (by design)
findings.evidence: src/save.rs:366-380 GlobalSave::decode and 423-440 ReadFlags::decode skip the leading_u32-vs-bytes.len() cross-check. The doc-comment (src/save.rs:355-358) explains that leading_u32 for these formats is a per-format constant (0xA4 / 0x98) not a file size, so the SystemSave check would not apply. This is correct behaviour, but means the audit-focus 'silently truncating slots' protection covers only SystemSave; GlobalSave/ReadFlags rely on the magic-string termination check for slot-end safety. A truncation that severs the payload but leaves the null-terminated magic intact would decode silently. Recommend documenting this asymmetry in the audit-focus pin.

findings.title: supportBoundary text always emitted as [REDACTED] in the proof report due to '/' tokens tripping local-absolute-path heuristic
findings.evidence: XP3_PROFILE_PROOF_SUPPORT_BOUNDARY (crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs:5419) contains the substring ' / extract / patch_back'. When passed through redact_for_log_or_report() the slash tokens (' /', '/ ') are picked up by text_contains_local_absolute_path() -> is_local_absolute_path() (starts_with('/')), causing the entire boundary string to be replaced with '[REDACTED:kaifuu.secret_redacted]'. Reproduced by running `kaifuu xp3 profile-proof` against fixtures/kaifuu/kirikiri/xp3-profile.json: supportBoundary in the resulting proof.json is `[REDACTED:kaifuu.secret_redacted]`. This silently strips public-safe boundary text from every proof and weakens the spec acceptance criterion 'The proof report includes ... semantic remediation' — consumers cannot read the boundary. Suggested fix: rewrite the constant without lone '/' tokens (e.g. 'detect, extract, patch_back'), or run support_boundary through a non-redacting copy.

findings.title: xp3.patch_capability.overclaim message and xp3.crypt_profile.missing message are redacted, hiding the human-readable failure reason from the report
findings.evidence: Diagnostic messages such as 'fixture declared patch_back but only plain XP3 may claim extract or patch_back' (lib.rs:5985) and 'encrypted XP3 fixtures must declare a crypt profile' (lib.rs:6107) survive the redactor for synthetic fixtures, but for real-bytes runs the same messages emerge as '[REDACTED:kaifuu.secret_redacted]' in the final report (see /scratch real-bytes Bukkake Ranch proof). The cause is the same slash-tokenisation heuristic firing on words like 'patch_back' adjacent to '/' tokens that appear earlier in the input via redact_for_log_or_report. Net effect: the proof communicates only the diagnostic code and remediation; the message field is opaque. Not a leak risk, but an information-quality issue that hides the routing reason from consumers.

findings.title: Closed taxonomy collapses queue.read denial into permission_denied_manage status
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/batch-preview.ts:381 buildDeniedPreview sets status=permissionDeniedManage with requiredPermission="queue.read" and diagnostic code reviewer_batch_permission_denied_read. The closed status enum (reviewerBatchPreviewStatusValues, lines 89-98) lacks a permissionDeniedRead member; the single permissionDeniedManage value is overloaded to mean two distinct denials (read vs. manage). Downstream consumers cannot distinguish a read-gate refusal from a manage-gate refusal by status alone, weakening the closed-taxonomy guarantee for per-item diagnostics.

findings.title: Atomicity claim weaker than "no partial writes": per-item dispatch loop can leave items 1..N-1 applied if item N races
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/batch-execute.ts:208-239 dispatches one action per selection in a sequential loop. Each per-item dispatch is itself a single-row DB transaction, but inter-item atomicity is NOT guaranteed: if item k throws ReviewerQueueRepositoryError mid-loop (e.g., concurrent move, repository-level refusal), items 0..k-1 are already persisted. apps/itotori/test/reviewer-batch-execute.test.ts:210-256 explicitly asserts this behavior (result.applied[0]?.kind === "applied" alongside result.applied[1] refused). The header comment (batch-execute.ts:1-19) admits this and frames it as acceptable, but the focus item demands "fully apply OR fully rollback" — there is no rollback path for already-written transitions on a mid-loop refusal.

findings.title: Route file contains rendering helpers (renderLoading/renderError), violating route/view split
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/batch-route.ts:75-98 defines renderLoading() and renderError() which emit raw HTML directly into the root element, alongside their own escapeHtml() helper (lines 100-107). The focus item requires "parsing in batch-route, rendering in batch-view; no rendering in route, no parsing in view". The loading shell and error pane should live in batch-view.ts next to renderReviewerBatchPreviewView and the shared escapeHtml. Currently the route owns three independent rendering surfaces and a duplicate escapeHtml.

findings.title: Dead ternary in notFoundRow ignores canManage parameter
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/batch-preview.ts:476 requiredPermission: canManage ? "queue.manage" : "queue.manage" — both branches return the identical string, so the canManage argument is unused. Either the branch should differentiate (e.g. queue.read for the can-manage-false case to mirror buildDeniedPreview) or the parameter should be dropped. Cosmetic but signals the read/manage required-permission distinction was not fully thought through here.

findings.title: XP3 replace trusts manifest payload path before writing
findings.evidence: replace_plain_xp3_entry_payload joins dir with manifest-controlled entry.payload_relative_path and writes without the safe relative path check used by pack_plain_xp3_from_directory. A tampered manifest.json can point payloadRelativePath outside --input-dir, so kaifuu xp3 replace can write outside its intended tree. Audit ref: crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs around replace_plain_xp3_entry_payload and pack_plain_xp3_from_directory.

findings.title: Compressed XP3 profiles fall through to protected-executable diagnostic
findings.evidence: Focused audit found read_plain_xp3_archive only recognizes encrypted and helper-required markers before falling through to UnsupportedProtectedExecutable; existing synthetic compressed fixture uses XP3-COMPRESSED and the acceptance requires compressed-unknown profiles to fail before writes with semantic diagnostics. Current tests cover compressed entry replacement but not compressed-profile unpack/refusal.

findings.title: Harden XP3 unpacked payload materialization against symlink traversal
findings.evidence: Focused audit follow-up: replace_plain_xp3_entry_payload and pack_plain_xp3_from_directory validate manifest-controlled relative path strings before fs::write/fs::read, but the local validate_safe_relative_path helper does not canonicalize/materialize paths. Prior traversal P1 is fixed, but symlink-aware materialization would further harden unpacked directory handling without blocking KAIFUU-098 acceptance.

findings.title: Reviewer action transition and rerun enqueue are non-atomic
findings.evidence: Focused audit 2026-06-26: apps/itotori/src/reviewer/action-service.ts applies repository.applyAction before scheduling, and repair-rerun-scheduler enqueues the chain one job at a time. A scheduler failure can leave a committed reviewer transition with no rerun chain or a partial dependency chain. Acceptance: reviewer transition plus full rerun chain must be committed atomically, or a same-transaction outbox/recovery mechanism must prove no externally visible partial state; add a failure test.

findings.title: Reviewer rerun scheduler is optional and unwired in production action paths
findings.evidence: Focused audit 2026-06-26: ReviewerQueueActionService defaults deps to {}, only schedules when rerunScheduler is supplied, and repository search found ReviewerRepairRerunScheduler constructed only in tests. Existing action-service callers can still mutate reviewer actions without rerun jobs. Acceptance: production reviewer action composition must require or inject a durable scheduler backed by ItotoriEventQueueRepository, with tests covering the real API/batch action path.

findings.title: Harden DB evidence leakage guard for key-value private paths
findings.evidence: Final CATALOG-007 audit noted the DB evidence leakage guard catches private roots at string starts, whitespace, and quoted boundaries, but may miss key-value strings such as source=/private/corpus. No P0/P1 impact; add focused hardening when convenient.

findings.title: Private-local-only works can be emitted as public opportunity rows
findings.evidence: Audit: readCatalogOpportunityRanking can emit workId/canonicalTitle/originalLanguage/engineName/local aggregate fields for works with only private/local provenance after filtering private provenance from the row. Public opportunity rows must require public source/provenance or avoid identity leakage. Refs: packages/itotori-db/src/repositories/catalog-repository.ts around readCatalogOpportunityRanking/build rows; .plan/CATALOG-061.md private-local aggregate constraints.

findings.title: Public opportunity fixture expectedDefaultReadModel is not schema-valid
findings.evidence: Audit: fixtures/catalog-opportunities/fixture.json expectedDefaultReadModel rows omit required catalog.opportunity_ranking.v0.1 fields such as originalLanguage, sourceIds, readiness, runtimeEvidenceReadiness, translationStatuses, demandFacts, localOwnership, marketPrevalence, provenance, and demotions. API schema would reject it, so the public expected-output artifact is only compact presentation data.

findings.title: Runtime evidence readiness counts missing or unknown evidence as positive
findings.evidence: Audit: engine_capability_evidence.status can be present, partial, missing, or unknown, but opportunity readiness groups rows by evidenceSource and ignores status. Missing/unknown evidence can produce public_fixture/private_local_aggregate/public_and_aggregate runtime readiness and score positively. Tests only seed present evidence.

findings.title: DLsite rating average and work_type do not influence ranking or explanations
findings.evidence: Audit: CATALOG-061 plan requires DLsite sales/downloads, rating, wishlist, rank, and work_type to influence ranking through explicit weights or rule explanations. Current bucket/scoring uses dlCount, wishlistCount, bestRank, ratingCount, but ratingAverage and workType are only copied into output/evidence refs and tests only assert presence.

findings.title: Clarify catalog opportunity public runtime evidence count path
findings.evidence: Final CATALOG-061 audit at spec/catalog-061 commit 6e5d84d: after runtime-readiness source semantics were tightened, DB-generated opportunity rows keep publicFixtureEvidenceCount effectively at 0 while API and fixture shapes still allow public runtime-readiness states. Clarify this intentional split or remove the dead DB branch later. Ref: packages/itotori-db/src/repositories/catalog-repository.ts around runtime evidence count construction.

findings.title: Add main dashboard panel for catalog opportunities
findings.evidence: Final CATALOG-061 audit at spec/catalog-061 commit 6e5d84d: the node delivers the opportunity API/read model and dashboard seed fixture consumer test, but the main dashboard panel integration remains optional/deferred per .plan/CATALOG-061.md. Track a future UI integration slice rather than blocking this merge.

findings.title: Post-run verifier ignores provider-run artifacts
findings.evidence: suite/scripts/localize-sweetie-hd/verify-artifacts.mjs only checks agentic-loop bundle, patch report, replay log, and telemetry aggregates; audit found it does not read run-summary.artifacts.providerRunArtifacts or provider-runs/*. The persisted OpenRouter proof lives in provider-run artifacts with run.routingPosture and adapterMetadata.providerRouting.
findings.expected: Verifier joins provider-runs to successful bundle invocations by providerProofId/runId and rejects missing, stale, fake/local, mismatched model/pair, non-ZDR, or non-billed live success artifacts.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend verify-artifacts.mjs and tests to load provider-runs from run-summary/run dir, validate exactly one matching provider-run artifact per successful invocation, assert run.routingPosture.zdr === true and adapterMetadata.providerRouting.zdr === true, and assert billed cost for live OpenRouter successes.

findings.title: Telemetry proof can pass with stale or mismatched telemetry
findings.evidence: verify-artifacts.mjs checks only internal postRunEvidence booleans/counts from the supplied telemetry JSON and does not compare telemetry counts to the agentic-loop bundle invocation count, run project/window, or invocation model/provider pairs. Telemetry summary JSON also lacks projectId/from/to metadata, so broad stale windows can satisfy the verifier.
findings.expected: Telemetry summary JSON carries projectId/from/to and pair-scoped post-run evidence; verifier asserts the supplied window matches the current run, zdr invocation count equals cost-kind invocation count equals bundle invocation count, all telemetry pairs match invocation/provider artifacts, and every run cost_kind is billed.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend telemetry summary shape and verifier/tests to include and validate project/window metadata, pair/model counts, count equality with bundle invocations, no stale model IDs, and billed-only cost-kind rows for the run.

findings.title: Strengthen localize driver provider artifact validation beyond count-only
findings.evidence: Final re-audit: suite/scripts/localize-sweetie-hd/run.mjs still counts provider-runs/*/provider-run.json and only checks artifact count >= invocation count after the stage. The offline verifier now performs strong ID/content validation, but run.mjs itself can still miss stale, duplicate, mismatched, fake/local, non-ZDR, or non-billed artifacts.
findings.expected: Driver-side provider artifact validation either calls the same strong verifier logic after telemetry exists or validates providerProofId/runId, live OpenRouter provenance, model/provider pairs, ZDR routing posture, and billed successful live costs instead of only count >= invocation count.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a future suite-hardening slice that shares provider-run validation with verify-artifacts.mjs or replaces the count-only check in run.mjs with equivalent ID/content validation.

findings.title: Make Sweetie HD localize workflow self-proving with telemetry and verifier outputs
findings.evidence: Final re-audit: just localize-sweetie-hd still only builds and runs suite/scripts/localize-sweetie-hd/run.mjs. The main recipe does not automatically run telemetry-summary or verify-artifacts.mjs, so a successful live workflow can still omit telemetry-summary.json and post-run-verification.json unless the orchestrator manually runs the post-run commands.
findings.expected: The Sweetie HD workflow exposes an explicit post-run recipe or self-proving command path that captures telemetry-summary.json and post-run-verification.json for the run, or prints durable exact post-run commands with run start/end/project metadata.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a future suite workflow slice that either extends just localize-sweetie-hd or adds a companion recipe to generate telemetry summary and run verify-artifacts.mjs against the latest run directory.

findings.title: CI authorization matrix misses model ledger cost-kind gate
findings.evidence: qd ci run failed on 2026-06-27: packages/itotori-db test/authorization-matrix.test.ts expected model-ledger-repository.ts:countCostKindsByPair:catalogRead but sourcePermissionGates() did not detect it. Log: .qd/logs/ci-generalize-localize-workflow-command-surface-2026-06-27T20-06-46-972Z.log

findings.title: qd read commands require writable .qd database in read-only audit worktrees
findings.evidence: Post-wave audit at f551388 in /scratch/worktrees/itotori-audit-wave-qd-20260628: qd status/ready/stats/critical-path/export all failed opening .qd/qd.db on a read-only filesystem; committed .qd has no qd.db, while qd import --dry-run can parse roadmap/spec-dag.json. This blocks clean read-only subagent audits unless they hydrate a temp DB manually.

findings.title: Milestone-scoped qd stats and snapshot mix global and filtered views
findings.evidence: Post-wave audit after hydrating qd from roadmap/spec-dag.json: qd stats --json --milestone alpha returned global stats.nodes=710 and ready=279 while criticalPath/eta were alpha-scoped; qd snapshot --json --milestone alpha likewise returned global status/ready with only criticalPath filtered. This makes milestone progress and alpha readiness easy to misread.

findings.title: Expose benchmark seed adapterIds through catalog API filters
findings.evidence: ITOTORI-089 re-audit at cf23180 found repository and selector paths support explicit adapterIds, but /api/catalog/benchmark-seeds query parsing still returns only existing fields through limit. HTTP consumers cannot request explicit adapter-scoped seed selection even though direct repository/selector paths can.

findings.title: Avoid high-volume false positives from benchmark set minCapability prefilter
findings.evidence: ITOTORI-089 re-audit at cf23180 found final selection correctly requires every required capability, but toCatalogBenchmarkSeedFinderFilter still collapses multiple requiredCapabilities to the highest minCapabilityLevel for source read-model prefiltering. In a high-volume corpus, false-positive rows could consume the read-model limit before final selector filtering.

findings.title: Retire legacy spec-dag lifecycle commands that can corrupt qd export
findings.evidence: Broad qd/process audit at cfd5ab2: roadmap/spec-dag.json is qd export shape, but docs/spec-dag.md still instructs operators to use node scripts/spec-dag.mjs worktree/claim/ingest-audit/complete; scripts/spec-dag-lifecycle.mjs writes legacy status values in_progress/complete and camelCase statusReason/blockedBy into the canonical qd export, conflicting with qd statuses ready/claimed/working/done and status_reason validation.

findings.title: Make audit disposition non-destructive for live qd databases
findings.evidence: Broad qd/process audit at cfd5ab2: scripts/qd-wrapper.mjs audit dispose loads a snapshot, then removeLocalDatabase unlinks .qd/qd.db, .qd/qd.db-wal, and .qd/qd.db-shm before qd import has proven a replacement valid. qd is documented as the live orchestration ledger; audit disposition should be atomic/reversible and should not delete live DB files before validation and exclusive locking.

findings.title: Reject real-game patch source/target aliases before writes
findings.evidence: Broad alpha/generalization audit at cfd5ab2: suite/scripts/localize-project/run.mjs uses resolvePath string checks, always delegates patching with --force, and crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs copies source to target and writes patched Seen.txt before re-hashing the source. Source/target aliasing, symlink targets, and nested canonical paths must be rejected before any copy or write to protect real corpora.

findings.title: Redact private corpus paths from live localize-project logs
findings.evidence: Broad alpha/generalization audit at cfd5ab2: suite/scripts/localize-project/run.mjs prints full command lines containing raw gameRoot/source/target/Seen.txt paths, and failure paths in the suite runner and kaifuu-cli print raw private paths. Existing leakage coverage is dry-run only. Live stdout/stderr and artifacts must use corpus IDs, env placeholders, hashes, or redacted labels for private corpus and target roots.

findings.title: Redact private source identities from catalog completeness/conflict APIs
findings.evidence: Broad API/security audit at cfd5ab2: catalog completeness/conflict read models collect all provenance IDs and return catalogSource/sourceId/sourceRecordKind/rawContentRedactionClass without filtering; API schema only type-checks those fields. Existing tests include localCorpus/privateCorpus provenance. Public catalog read models should omit local/private source IDs or expose only aggregate counts.

findings.title: Gate project status and cost reads behind permissions
findings.evidence: Broad API/security audit at cfd5ab2: GET /api/projects/status, /api/projects/cost, and /api/projects route directly to project workflow without requireApiPermission; project read workflow methods pass without an actor; getProjectCostReport has no requirePermission and selects provider/model/routing fields plus translation-memory targetText. Cost/status reads should require a read permission and split redacted dashboard summaries from privileged ledger details.

findings.title: Gate runtime status reads and redact runtime evidence previews
findings.evidence: Broad API/security audit at cfd5ab2: /api/runtime/v0.2/status is an unauthenticated GET path, workflow passes through without actor, and repository getRuntimeStatus returns observed/prompt text previews, finding messages, and artifact uri/hash. Runtime evidence reads should require project-scoped read permission; unprivileged summaries must redact text previews and managed artifact URIs.

findings.title: Make affected Rust gates cover all matching workspace crates
findings.evidence: Broad CI/dev-infra audit at cfd5ab2: scripts/affected.mjs maps every crates/kaifuu-* change to just ci-kaifuu and every crates/utsushi-* change to just ci-utsushi, but justfile ci-kaifuu omits kaifuu-reallive and kaifuu-vault-source, while ci-utsushi omits utsushi-reallive and utsushi-siglus despite those being workspace members. Affected output must be conservative and run a gate that covers the changed crate.

findings.title: Canonicalize roadmap export after audit disposition
findings.evidence: Broad qd/process audit at cfd5ab2: normal qd export --out roadmap/spec-dag.json is wrapper-canonicalized, but scripts/qd-wrapper.mjs audit dispose calls the real qd export directly after import, bypassing canonicalizeSpecDagExport. Every wrapper path that writes roadmap/spec-dag.json should use the same canonicalization and validation path.

findings.title: Count public fixture evidence in opportunity runtime readiness
findings.evidence: Broad API/security audit at cfd5ab2: catalog opportunity aggregation has a branch to count public_fixture evidence, but the predicate rejects every non-private_local_aggregate row first, so public fixture adapter-matrix/key-validation evidence cannot contribute to publicFixtureEvidenceCount/readiness even though tests and fixtures treat public fixture evidence as valid input.

findings.title: Make compose DB defaults safe for parallel worktrees
findings.evidence: Broad CI/dev-infra audit at cfd5ab2: justfile defaults every worktree to 127.0.0.1:55433; scripts/itotori-db-compose-env.mjs derives per-worktree compose project names but still publishes the fixed parsed port; docker-compose.yml publishes that host port; db-reset truncates shared tables. just db-up in two worktrees should not collide or share/reset the same DB by default.

findings.title: Active alpha validation node still names obsolete localize-sweetie-hd artifacts
findings.evidence: Broad alpha audit 2026-06-28 at main c6a6522: UTSUSHI-231 title/spec still describe re-running localize-sweetie-hd and require artifacts/localize-sweetie-hd/<timestamp>/, while the actual generic driver writes artifacts/localize-project/<timestamp>-<project> from suite/scripts/localize-project/run.mjs. Verification commands were normalized to just localize-project --project sweetie-hd-alpha-1, so the node acceptance text now points reviewers at the wrong artifact root. Acceptance: update UTSUSHI-231 title/spec/deliverables/status notes to localize-project and artifacts/localize-project/<run-dir>/; keep Sweetie HD only as explicit project data; require telemetry and verifier outputs under the generic run dir.

findings.title: Promoted audit-fix nodes are ready but not implementable specs
findings.evidence: Broad orchestration audit 2026-06-28 at main c6a6522: active qd export includes many promoted audit-fix nodes with acceptance exactly 'Finding is addressed and verified.', empty verification, empty audit_focus, and null group/milestone. Example qd export around roadmap/spec-dag.json entry for report-id-is-a-constant-per-kind-index-pair-not-per-run. This violates docs/orchestration-operating-model.md observable-artifact anti-pattern and docs/spec-dag.md node verification expectations, so qd can offer vague unauditable work to parallel agents. Acceptance: qd finding promotion creates nodes with concrete acceptance, verification, project/group/milestone where applicable, and audit_focus; validator rejects generic acceptance and empty verification/audit_focus for active promoted nodes.

findings.title: Nested target symlinks can redirect patch writes outside TARGET
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28: suite/scripts/localize-project/run.mjs rejects a symlink only at TARGET root before invoking kaifuu-cli patch --force, and crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs validate_patch_target_root/copy_directory_tree also reject only source symlinks and the target root. If an existing TARGET contains REALLIVEDATA -> SOURCE/REALLIVEDATA or another writable path, fs::create_dir_all/fs::copy/fs::write can follow that target-side symlink and mutate the readonly source or write outside TARGET before the post-write source-hash invariant detects it. Expected fix: reject symlink components/entries under existing TARGET before copying, or copy into a new symlink-free staging tree and promote with no-clobber semantics; add tests for TARGET/REALLIVEDATA symlink to source and to another directory.

findings.title: Permission verifier accepts non-string SQL expressions in IN list
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28: packages/itotori-db/scripts/verify-permission-constraints.mjs captures permission in (...) and extractSqlStrings only reads quoted strings. It does not reject leftover SQL tokens. A constraint whose list contains all expected quoted permissions plus the bare expression permission would pass the verifier while allowing arbitrary permissions because permission IN (..., permission) is always true for the row value. Expected fix: make the SQL list parser strict so, after valid string literals, commas, and whitespace are consumed, no tokens remain; add regression fixture for bare permission or other non-string expressions in the permission list.

findings.title: Public fixture runtime evidence is never counted in opportunity readiness
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28: packages/itotori-db/src/repositories/catalog-repository.ts capabilityEvidenceCountsByAdapter calls isRuntimeReadinessEvidence before counting evidence. isRuntimeReadinessEvidence currently returns false for every evidenceSource other than private_local_aggregate, making the later public_fixture branch unreachable. Impact: public fixture runtime evidence contributes publicFixtureEvidenceCount: 0 and can downgrade adapter readiness evidence in catalog opportunities. Expected fix: classify valid public_fixture runtime evidence kinds as readiness evidence, preserve private aggregate counts, and add DB-backed regression coverage for public-only and mixed public/private evidence.

findings.title: Completeness and conflict API guards allow extra private fields through
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28: apps/itotori/src/api-handlers.ts validates API payloads but returns the original object, and apps/itotori/src/api-schema.ts uses permissive asRecord for catalog completeness and conflict read-model structures. Unknown keys such as localPath, rawPayload, payloadHash, or private scan metadata can survive validation and serialize on /api/catalog/completeness or /api/catalog/conflicts. Expected fix: use strict record/object validation for these response shapes or sanitize to a constructed public response, and add negative API fixtures proving extra fields cause internal_error before serialization.

findings.title: Current qd export still contains non-portable reused CI evidence
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found roadmap/spec-dag.json still contains historical reused CI records with local .qd/logs log_path and summaries, despite docs/worktree-lifecycle.md requiring portable URL, external-id, or checked-in repo-relative evidence. Wrapper now blocks future bad record-pass calls, but current export/backlog evidence remains non-portable for fresh audit worktrees.

findings.title: atlas-cloud alternate can fail OpenRouter provider pair validation
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found presets/localize-project.pair-policy.json declares providerId atlas-cloud while evidence says OpenRouter returns body.provider='AtlasCloud'. apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts accepts lowercase equality or OPENROUTER_KNOWN_PROVIDERS entries, but atlas-cloud is not registered and atlas-cloud != atlascloud. Pair mismatch is hard non-429 failure, so UTSUSHI-231 can still block after earlier alternates even if AtlasCloud succeeds.

findings.title: Provider-run artifacts persist upstream HTTP error messages verbatim
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found apps/itotori/src/providers/openrouter.ts writes error.message from providerErrorMessage(response body) into failed provider-run artifacts, and providerErrorMessage returns body.error.message unchanged. Tests assert raw upstream messages are preserved. For private_corpus invocations, upstream errors could echo prompt snippets, private paths, or title/corpus details into persisted provider artifacts.

findings.title: Reviewer dashboard and routes remain fixture/static-wired in integrated app
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found apps/itotori/src/dashboard.ts exposes projects/status/decisions/cost/runtime but no reviewer queue data, while apps/itotori/src/main.ts wires reviewer routes with static authorization/no grants and static loaders. The only reviewer dashboard endpoint is MSW-only test fixture. Operators cannot reach real DB-backed queue review/detail/batch flows through the integrated dashboard.

findings.title: Benchmark seed API silently ignores unknown query parameters
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found parseCatalogBenchmarkSeedFinderFilter in apps/itotori/src/api-handlers.ts reads supported params but never calls assertKnownQueryParams, unlike adjacent catalog opportunity parsing. Misspelled benchmark seed filters can return valid-looking unfiltered seed data.

findings.title: Benchmark seed API guard accepts runtimeEvidenceReadiness absent from DB read model
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found CatalogBenchmarkSeedRow and repository output do not include runtimeEvidenceReadiness, but apps/itotori/src/api-schema.ts allowlists optional runtimeEvidenceReadiness for benchmark seed rows and api-handler tests explicitly accept it. Public API contract is looser than typed repository output.

findings.title: localize-project proof artifacts contain absolute checkout paths
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found suite/scripts/localize-project/run.mjs writes run-summary.json with absolute runDir and artifact paths, and verify-artifacts.mjs writes post-run-verification.json with absolute runDir/telemetrySummary paths. findings.title: Audit disposition lock has no stale-lock recovery
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at 538edbc found scripts/qd-wrapper.mjs creates .qd/audit-disposition.lock and aborts on EEXIST without owner metadata, PID/age checks, malformed-lock recovery, or force semantics. A crashed audit disposition can block follow-up audit recovery indefinitely even though live-lock refusal is still needed.

findings.title: qd-import can destroy live qd state before validated replacement
findings.evidence: Broad audit 2026-06-28 at main 5ddd78b: justfile qd-import removes .qd/qd.db* before qd import has proven a replacement, then rewrites qd config to plain just check/just qd-full-ci instead of committed nix-wrapped commands. This can strand qd orchestration if import/setup fails and can recreate check/CI drift after rebuild.

findings.title: Portable qd CI evidence backfill misses local-log reuse records

Dashboard navigation to /reviewer-queue/batch can therefore 404 outside dev fallback.
