findings.title: Fixture key does not actually decrypt the envelope (disjoint disclosed scaffolding)
findings.evidence: encrypted-envelope.xp3 and decrypted-inner.xp3 are disjoint synthetic fixtures glued by the descriptor; the resolved key never decrypts. Disclosed scaffolding, not a real decrypt pipeline — recorded so the surface walk isn't mistaken for one.

findings.title: Per-item preview status is not enum-validated at the API boundary (pre-existing, all status values)
findings.evidence: assertReviewerBatchPreview validates the aggregate as a strict record (new permissionDeniedRead key added, validated as non-negative integer) but only calls asArray(preview.items) without per-item shape/enum validation. So the per-item `status` string (e.g. "permission_denied_read") is not checked against reviewerBatchPreviewStatusValues at the schema boundary. This is PRE-EXISTING and affects all 10 status members, not a regression introduced by this change; the read/manage distinction is still genuinely observable at the boundary via the distinct aggregate.permissionDeniedRead count (schema-validated) and the distinct per-item status string. Noting as a follow-up to fully harden the closed-taxonomy guarantee, not a blocker for this finding.

findings.title: Zero-dimension SCREENSIZE_MOD (e.g. 999,0,720) parses to a degenerate Some(ScreenSize) that silently routes pointer to a collapsed axis instead of emitting MissingScreenSize
findings.evidence: The fix only triggers SyscallDispatchError::MissingScreenSize when self.screen_size is None (syscall.rs:546-560). But parse_screen_size accepts width/height of 0 because u32::try_from(0) succeeds (syscall.rs:703-704), yielding Some(ScreenSize{width:0,...}). A pointer event then takes the success path: pointer_to_pixel returns 0 for the zero axis (syscall.rs:241-250) and route_for_pointer_pixel matches against the degenerate coordinate. So a malformed `#SCREENSIZE_MOD=999,0,720` does NOT emit the diagnostic, even though both the constant doc ('missing or malformed', syscall.rs:87-91) and the error message/doc ('SCREENSIZE_MOD is missing or malformed', syscall.rs:312-318) claim malformed input is covered. Note render_pipeline.rs:622 already has render_pass_rejects_zero_screen_size — the syscall dispatcher has no equivalent zero guard, so the two consumers of ScreenSize disagree on whether a zero dimension is valid. Out of the original finding's scope (which pinned the None / missing case specifically) and not a silent total-disable (the pointer still routes, just to a degenerate axis), hence P3 follow-up rather than in-scope. Wrong-arity and negative SCREENSIZE_MOD values DO return None and are correctly caught; only the zero-dimension case slips the 'malformed' claim.

findings.title: §M.2 RealLive-only surface table omits UTSUSHI-212/214/215 (symmetry follow-up)
findings.evidence: This node's acceptance is UTSUSHI-221 AC3 = 'engine-specific boundary note' = §M.3 only, which the diff fully satisfies. The §M.2 table (lines 966-979) is a separate UTSUSHI-221 deliverable ('Appendix documenting reusable vs RealLive-only surfaces') and is not named by AC3. Moreover the file's own precedent already decouples the two sections: partially-reusable nodes UTSUSHI-203 and UTSUSHI-207 appear in §M.3 but NOT §M.2. UTSUSHI-212 (rng path carries) and UTSUSHI-214 (FrameArtifactSink carries) are likewise partially-reusable, so their absence from a table titled 'RealLive-only (does NOT carry to Siglus)' is arguably correct, not a defect. Only UTSUSHI-215 (mostly RealLive-only opcode byte-codes + REALLIVEDATA/g00 path) is a genuine §M.2 candidate, and even it has substrate carriers. RECOMMENDATION: do not block this merge; track §M.2 symmetry (decide whether to add a UTSUSHI-215 row, and whether 212/214 belong) as a separate P3 follow-up, NOT a re-open of this node.

findings.title: Cosmetic: UTSUSHI-214 bullet loses 2-space hanging indent on the 'image_ref, layer_order)' continuation line
findings.evidence: In the added UTSUSHI-214 bullet the wrapped line 'image_ref, layer_order)` state, and a RealLive Gameexe' begins at column 0 instead of the 2-space list-continuation indent used by the surrounding lines. It is still part of the same paragraph (no intervening blank line) so it renders correctly as a bullet; this is purely a source-formatting wart, not a rendering or content defect. Optional tidy, not merge-blocking.

findings.title: Batch reviewer surface records importRuntimeFeedback tier verbatim with no persisted-vs-supplied check (parallel recurrence vector)
findings.evidence: The new enforcement lives only in the single ReviewerQueueActionService.importRuntimeFeedback method (action-service.ts:283-296, which calls repository.getItem + assertMatchesPersisted*). The batch surface does NOT route through that method: applyPreparedBatch -> repository.applyActionsAndEnqueueJobs, and the action input for batch is built in batch-execute.ts:423-439 which only does shape checks (assertNonEmptyPayload/assertNonEmptyPayloadArray on evidenceTier/observationEventIds/artifactHashes) and then records the SUPPLIED values onto the transition metadata verbatim. applyActionInTransaction (reviewer-queue-repository.ts:573-648) validates only the state transition + optimistic lock; its UPDATE .set({...}) never compares the metadata evidence against the persisted evidence_tier/observation_event_ids/artifact_hashes columns. So for a runtime_evidence item, a caller using the batch path can still record a different (e.g. higher) tier on the audit-trail transition log than the item actually carries -- exactly the drift the single-method fix prevents. The item row's evidence columns stay correct (immutable, SQL discriminant), but the transition metadata (the audit trail this whole feature exists to protect) can be falsified via batch. This is PRE-EXISTING and out of the audited change's declared scope (the batch method's docstring makes no enforcement claim, so it is not a doc/behavior mismatch and not a regression), but the fix's stated goal is to 'prevent recurrence' and the batch surface is an obvious open recurrence vector. Recommend a DAG follow-up to apply the same persisted-vs-supplied assertion on the batch path (ideally a shared chokepoint, e.g. inside applyActionInTransaction gated on item_kind = runtime_evidence).

findings.title: importRuntimeFeedback now also requires queue.read (latent permission coupling)
findings.evidence: The added getItem call runs requirePermission(queueRead) (reviewer-queue-repository.ts:514), whereas the action itself requires queueManage (line 481). Permissions are non-hierarchical exact-match grants (authorization.ts:54-73) -- queue.manage does NOT imply queue.read. So importRuntimeFeedback now requires BOTH queue.read and queue.manage, a behavior change from before. Currently harmless: the only grant site is bootstrapLocalUser (authorization.ts:81-86) which grants ALL permissions to local-user, and no code path grants queue.manage without queue.read, so a manage-without-read actor is not reachable. Noting as a latent coupling to be aware of if a read-restricted manage role is ever introduced.

findings.title: Pre-fetch is outside the action transaction; safe only because evidence fields are immutable
findings.evidence: getItem is called before (not inside) the transactional applyActionInTransaction, which re-reads the row independently. This is NOT a real TOCTOU because evidence_tier/observation_event_ids/artifact_hashes are create-only: the only UPDATE path (reviewer-queue-repository.ts:630-648 .set({state, updatedAt, resolvedAt, affectedArtifactIds})) never mutates them, and the SQL discriminant (migrations/0043:item_kind<->evidence non-null) is fixed at create. The only divergence window (item absent at fetch -> enforcement skipped, then present at apply) requires the deterministic review_item_id to be created between fetch and apply, which the reviewer-acts-on-an-existing-queued-item workflow does not produce; and even then apply validates the transition from pending. Defense-in-depth: moving the comparison into applyActionInTransaction would make it fully race-free and also close the P2 batch gap at one chokepoint. Optional.

findings.title: Int-arg skip semantics itself remains unvalidated against real games (pre-existing, separate concern)
findings.evidence: The fix correctly aligns the emitted index with the stored choices Vec GIVEN the existing design decision that an interleaved ExprValue::Int arg is dropped from the choice list (module_sel.rs:386-396, `continue`). That skip behavior is pre-existing and outside this node's scope, but it is worth a DAG follow-up: in RealLive the conditional variants (notably select_w) carry interleaved condition/flag operands that are NOT user-visible labels, and silently dropping them (rather than e.g. pairing a condition with the following label) may misrepresent conditional choice lists. The store-register value written on resume (vm.rs:661 set_store(index)) is the contiguous post-skip Vec index, which only matches original-engine choice numbering if the engine likewise renumbers after dropping non-label args. Recommend validating the select/select_w arg shape against >=2 real games (per multi-game-validation policy) before relying on the skip semantics; this audit confirms only the internal index-alignment invariant, not the skip's fidelity to the original engine.

findings.title: Sibling spec-dag node count-public-fixture-evidence-in-opportunity-readiness (P2, ready) proposes the OPPOSITE and would reintroduce the false advertisement
findings.evidence: roadmap/spec-dag.json contains node id 'count-public-fixture-evidence-in-opportunity-readiness' (status=ready, priority=P2). Its spec: 'Fix catalog opportunity runtime readiness aggregation so public_fixture adapter-matrix AND key-validation evidence contributes to publicFixtureEvidenceCount and public_fixture readiness instead of being rejected by the private-local-only predicate.' The 'adapter-matrix ... contributes to publicFixtureEvidenceCount' clause directly contradicts the audited resolution (isRuntimeReadinessEvidence in catalog-repository.ts:3336-3347 deliberately excludes public_fixture adapter_matrix because the static capability matrix must not masquerade as runtime readiness) and is exactly the false-advertisement CATALOG-061 warns about. This node is ALSO partially stale: it claims public_fixture evidence is currently 'rejected by the private-local-only predicate', but the sibling node 'count-public-fixture-runtime-evidence-readiness' (status=done) already implemented public_fixture key_validation counting (locked by catalog-repository.test.ts:1616). RECONCILIATION: the key_validation part is already DONE; the remaining adapter_matrix-counting part is WRONG and should be cancelled (or corrected to drop the adapter_matrix clause). NOTE ON IDS: the audit brief referenced numeric ids 31918 & 32543, but roadmap/spec-dag.json uses slug ids and exposes exactly ONE open conflicting node (the slug above). The numeric ids are qd-internal; I did not run qd (per audit constraint), so the orchestrator must map 31918/32543 to slug nodes -- one is almost certainly this slug node. I could not locate a SECOND distinct conflicting open node in spec-dag.json; if 32543 is a separate qd row not yet exported to spec-dag.json, it needs the same cancel/correct treatment.

findings.title: Missing key_validation public-fixture producer leaves public_fixture / public_and_aggregate runtime-readiness states unreachable in production (warrants a follow-up node)
findings.evidence: The production producer apps/itotori/src/services/catalog-local-capability-evidence.ts declares CatalogCapabilityEvidenceKind = 'adapter_matrix' | 'local_corpus_sidecar' (line 30) -- key_validation is NOT a producible kind. public_fixture evidence is hard-coded to evidenceKind 'adapter_matrix' (lines 353-354) and the validator actively REJECTS any other public_fixture kind (lines 392-396: 'evidenceKind is not supported'). private_local_aggregate emits 'local_corpus_sidecar' (lines 188-189, 218-219). Consequently no production path ever produces public_fixture key_validation evidence, so capabilityEvidenceCountsByAdapter never increments publicFixtureEvidenceCount in production and the public_fixture / public_and_aggregate states are currently unreachable except in tests. This confirms the implementer's 'missing producer' diagnosis and is consistent with the doc block (catalog-repository.ts:3300-3303). It is a genuine feature gap, not a defect in the audited change. RECOMMEND a follow-up DAG node to add a key_validation public-fixture producer (e.g. validating decryption keys against a public fixture) so the advertised public runtime-readiness states become DB-reachable in production.

findings.title: New boundary test is a real, discriminating contract (adapter_matrix public -> unknown/0); doc block is accurate
findings.evidence: catalog-repository.test.ts ('does not count public_fixture adapter_matrix evidence as runtime readiness') inserts a public_fixture row with status=present (opportunityRuntimeEvidenceWeight=1, i.e. NON-zero so it clears the weight gate at catalog-repository.ts:3315) and evidenceKind=adapter_matrix via the new publicFixtureKind helper param (test helper lines 1854-1856, 1877). It asserts runtimeEvidenceReadiness === {status:'unknown', publicFixtureEvidenceCount:0, privateLocalAggregateEvidenceCount:0} and that runtimeEvidenceFactor yields weightedScore 0 / explanationCode 'runtime_evidence_readiness:unknown'. Because the row has weight>0, the test proves the EXCLUSION is driven by isRuntimeReadinessEvidence (kind check), not merely by a zero-weight skip -- a genuine contract. The helper default for publicFixtureKind remains key_validation, so the positive test at line 1616 (status public_fixture/public_and_aggregate, count 1) is unchanged -- no regression. The doc block at catalog-repository.ts:3294-3308 accurately describes the source split, names both locking tests, and correctly states production keeps the count at 0 until a key_validation producer exists. Doc-only-clarify + negative boundary test is the right resolution for this P3: removing the public dimension would delete a live, tested capability (locked by line 1616 and by the done node count-public-fixture-runtime-evidence-readiness) and force a golden rescore for no correctness gain, since no false advertisement actually exists.

findings.title: Object-form costUsd literal slips through when formatted across multiple lines
findings.evidence:
The audit is strictly line-based (findViolations splits on /?
/ and tests each pattern per line). Three of the four costLiteral patterns are single-key (amountMicrosUsd, bare cost, bare costUsd:number) and therefore always occupy one line, so they are robustly caught. But the fourth, 'hardcoded non-zero costUsd object amount literal', requires `costUsd:` AND its inner `amount: "<digit>"` on the SAME line: regex / ["'`]?costUsd["'`]?\s*:\s*\{[^}]* ["'`]?amount["'`]?\s*:\s*["'`](?=[\d.]*[1-9])/. PROBE: a file containing
  costUsd: {
    unit: "usd",
    amount: "0.0125",
  }
over separate lines was scanned with `node scripts/audit-no-hardcoded-cost.mjs <probe>` -> 'audit passed', exit 0 (MISS). This is the natural prettier output once the object exceeds printWidth, and `amount:` on its own line carries no `cost` token so the bare-cost pattern cannot catch it either. A production file hardcoding ONLY the decimal-string costUsd object (without the single-line amountMicrosUsd that would otherwise trip the guard) would pass CI silently. It is rated P2 rather than P1 because the PRIMARY integer field (amountMicrosUsd) and the single-line object form are caught, and a realistic hardcoded cost usually also carries amountMicrosUsd; but a production hardcoded cost CAN slip through this specific multi-line shape, so a reviewer could reasonably argue P1. Suggested fix: match the decimal-string `amount:` standalone (the rip-out rule forbids any hardcoded decimal-USD amount regardless of enclosing key) or normalize the costUsd object across newlines before matching.

findings.title: draft-attempt-fixtures.ts lives in src/ while being cost-literal-exempt
findings.evidence: apps/itotori/src/draft/draft-attempt-fixtures.ts is explicitly allow-listed for cost literals yet sits in the production src/ tree (and is the only src-tree path so exempted). Today it is unreachable from production (sole importer is apps/itotori/test/draft-attempt-recorder.test.ts; no barrel re-export), so there is no live leak. The latent risk: a future production import would ship its synthetic costs (amountMicrosUsd 12_500, cost 0.0125, costUsd "0.01250000") while the guard stays silent. Lower-entropy alternative would be relocating the fixture under apps/itotori/test/ so it is covered by the generic test/ exemption and cannot be production-imported, removing the need for a bespoke src-path allow-list entry.

findings.title: Agentic-loop bundle per-invocation/stage costUsd remains micros-derived (loses sub-micro tail)
findings.evidence: costUsd=microsToAmount(telemetry.costMicros) / microsToAmount(stage.costMicros); costMicros=assertBilledCost(providerRun.cost) (micros). microsToAmount pads to exactly 6 fractional digits, so a 0.00000602 invocation reports '0.000006'. The AgenticLoopInvocation.costUsd doc (localization-bridge-schema/agentic-loop-bundle.ts:98-101) claims 'the real billed USD cost as a decimal string', which is now imprecise for sub-micro costs — the full-precision authority lives in ProviderCost.amountUsd / ledger cost_amount. This bundle is not persisted under the migration-0041 CHECK and is not the ledger authority, so it is not a P0/P1 integrity bug; it is a parallel-representation fidelity/doc gap out of this node's scope.

findings.title: New migration-0041 DB CHECK tests are DATABASE_URL-gated (not runtime-verified in this audit)
findings.evidence: Both the sub-micro positive-insert test and the rounded negative-control (expect pgCode 23514) are inside describe.skipIf(!process.env.DATABASE_URL). No DATABASE_URL was set in this audit, so they were skipped; the CHECK behavior was validated by reading the SQL (0041 lines 102-107) rather than executed.

findings.title: Require-flag contract is not consumed by any CI/just target; enforcement is operator-manual
findings.evidence: grep for ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES across the repo (excluding crates/kaifuu-reallive/tests and target/) returns only the node's own definition in roadmap/spec-dag.json:34326,34344 — no justfile/nix/CI yaml sets the flag. Consequence: the false-green is eliminated only when an operator explicitly sets ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES=1. A bare `cargo test -p kaifuu-reallive -- --ignored` with env unset and the flag unset still early-returns and reports a green PASS (now preceded by a 'SKIP (no silent pass)' eprintln). This matches the node's stated acceptance (which only required the flag contract to exist and fail-loud under the flag), so it is NOT a regression against scope — recorded as a follow-up: wire ITOTORI_REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES=1 into the CI/just target that stages Sweetie HD so the contract is actually enforced rather than dormant.

findings.title: Sibling crate utsushi-reallive has the identical silent-pass anti-pattern, unaddressed (recommend follow-up node)
findings.evidence: crates/utsushi-reallive/tests has the same `let Some(..) = real_seen_txt_path() else { eprintln!("ITOTORI_REAL_GAME_ROOT unset; ... no silent pass ..."); return; };` early-return-with-green across ~17 real_bytes test files and ~50 #[ignore]d tests (e.g. decompressor_real_bytes.rs:54-60, replay_scene_real_bytes.rs:150-151,185-186, plus g00/nwa/ovk/audio_rlop/gameexe/scene_header/scene_index/etc.). Its support helper crates/utsushi-reallive/tests/support/real_corpus.rs has NO REQUIRE_REAL_BYTES_ENV / skip_or_require_real_bytes — only skip_message. The kaifuu fix does not touch utsushi. Out of scope for this node, but a real test-integrity hole of larger blast radius. Recommend a new audit-fix node 'utsushi-reallive-real-bytes-tests-silent-pass-env-unset' (P2) porting the same skip_or_require_real_bytes contract to utsushi's helper and its ~50 corpus-gated tests.
